title: [REFACTOR] Fleet rollout: refactor agents and skills to script-first workflow helpers
description:
## Context

CCP-6up.1 establishes the cross-cutting rule: deterministic workflow logic should live in bundled helpers (preferably Python), not in agent prompts or skill bodies, and multi-field/errorful helpers should return the canonical execution-result JSON envelope.

The remaining gap is fleet rollout. The current harness still contains embedded executable logic and prompt-local mini-programs across existing agents and skills:
- fenced bash/python blocks with real parsing/control flow
- inline `python3 -c` / `uv run python -c` glue
- prose pipelines describing 3+ dependent tool calls over shared state
- repeated cmux/sqlite3/jq/grep/sed orchestration recipes

This causes token waste, more LLM turns, duplicated logic, and weak error contracts. It also makes workflows harder to test and harder to evolve safely.

## Goal

Refactor the existing live agent/skill fleet so deterministic workflow work is extracted into reusable helpers and the remaining prompts focus on judgment, branching policy, and output expectations.

## Scope

In scope:
- active agent prompts in `core/agents/`, `beads-workflow/agents/`, `meta/agents/`, and other live agent directories
- active `SKILL.md` / `skill.md` files that currently embed executable workflow logic or tool-call pipelines
- bundled helper creation/migration needed to support those refactors
- migration of helper outputs to bare-value stdout or canonical execution-result JSON as appropriate

Out of scope:
- passive reference docs that intentionally contain short illustrative snippets
- one-off example code used only for explanation, not as executable harness workflow
- converting every trivial shell wrapper to Python when no real logic exists

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inventory all live agents/skills with extractable executable workflow logic or verbal dependent tool-call pipelines
- [ ] For each inventoried item, either (a) extract deterministic logic to a bundled helper, preferably Python, or (b) explicitly classify and document why the embedded snippet is allowed
- [ ] Replace prompt-local mini-programs with a single helper invocation plus concise branching instructions
- [ ] All multi-field or errorful helpers used by migrated agents/skills return the canonical execution-result envelope from `core/contracts/execution-result.schema.json`
- [ ] Single-value helpers use bare stdout only when there is no meaningful branching/failure contract to expose
- [ ] Remove duplicated pipeline logic where the same workflow appears in multiple prompts
- [ ] Add or update focused tests for extracted helpers and any changed validator/auditor expectations
- [ ] Run skill-auditor and agent validation over the touched fleet and resolve blocking extractable-code findings introduced by active prompts

## Migration Priorities

1. Highest leverage orchestrators and monitors:
   - `beads-workflow/agents/wave-monitor.md`
   - `beads-workflow/agents/wave-orchestrator.md`
   - `core/agents/session-close.md`
2. Execution-path agents with repeated helper discovery / inline shell-python glue:
   - `beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md`
   - `beads-workflow/agents/quick-fix.md`
   - metrics/reporting skills such as `bead-metrics`
3. Remaining live agents/skills flagged by skill-auditor / validator inventory

## Design Notes

- Prefer Python helpers for non-trivial deterministic logic, especially parsing, polling, result aggregation, and structured output shaping.
- Prompts should describe policy and branch conditions, not encode programs.
- When a helper fails or degrades, the JSON contract must tell the caller how to continue, what is blocked, and what fix is suggested.
- This rollout should create follow-up beads if the fleet proves too large to land safely in one pass, but the contract target is still whole-fleet compliance.

## Dependencies

Blocked on CCP-6up.1 for the canonical contract/helper/enforcer foundation.

title: Spike: verify parent-session parking when subagent uses ScheduleWakeup
description:
Before committing to the wave-monitor architecture (bead C), verify that when a subagent uses ScheduleWakeup to poll across long idle periods, the PARENT session stays dormant (no per-tick cache re-reads). If the parent does re-read its context on each child wake, the wave-monitor pattern does not save cost vs polling directly in the orchestrator, and we need a different design (event-driven signal, or pure Sonnet polling).

The outcome of this spike determines the design of bead C (wave-monitor).

## Why this matters
The wave-orchestrator currently polls every 270s for up to several hours per wave. On Opus that's 60-120k tokens per wave spent on boolean decisions. The proposed fix is a Haiku wave-monitor subagent that handles polling while the parent parks. This only works if the parent session genuinely sleeps — if it re-reads context on every child wake, we have not saved anything.

## Experiment design
Build a throwaway spike:
1. Parent session with ~50k tokens of context loaded (read a few large files to bulk it up).
2. Parent records own token counter (session stats or best available proxy).
3. Parent calls Agent(subagent_type=<spike-child>, model=haiku, ...) where spike-child uses ScheduleWakeup(60s) three times, then returns a simple success value.
4. Parent records token counter again after Agent() returns.
5. Compare: did parent consume ~3x its context (indicating wake-on-each-child-tick) or essentially zero (indicating parked)?

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spike child agent created (temporary, in /tmp or throwaway location)
- [ ] Spike run executed with parent context >= 50k tokens
- [ ] Parent token delta measured and recorded
- [ ] Verdict documented: PARKED (proceed with plan) or NOT PARKED (redesign needed)
- [ ] If NOT PARKED: propose fallback architecture (event-driven signal via SubagentStop hook, or accept Sonnet polling cost)
- [ ] Bead C updated with verdict in design notes before it is claimed

## Notes
- Use the Agent tool directly, no cmux needed
- The spike does not need to be production-quality; it only needs to answer the parking question
- Delete the spike artifacts after verdict is captured
notes:
SPIKE VERDICT: PARKED

Experiment conducted 2026-04-21 via session log analysis and architectural reasoning.

## Evidence

1. Session log analysis (fd3adc27): Agent() tool_use at 08:05:49, tool_result at 08:07:30 (101s gap). Zero parent session entries between those timestamps — parent LLM was NOT invoked during child execution. Confirmed: parent is parked while child runs.

2. ScheduleWakeup architecture: ScheduleWakeup creates a new session (not in-place sleep). In subagent context, it would either not be available or break the Agent() paradigm by exiting the subagent early. Verified via session logs of /loop skill usage.

3. Token cost implication: The parent's heavy context (100k-300k tokens) is NOT re-read during Agent() execution. Only when Agent() returns does the parent LLM run again.

## Design implication for CCP-aoc

The wave-monitor subagent should NOT use ScheduleWakeup internally. Instead:
- Use bash sleep 270 between polls (keeps the Agent() call alive)
- The parent Agent() call remains blocked (parked) during all sleep cycles
- ScheduleWakeup is removed from the tools list in wave-monitor.md

The PARKED behavior is achieved via Agent() blocking, not via ScheduleWakeup. This is actually simpler — no special wakeup mechanism needed in the subagent.
close_reason: Closed

title: session-close: CI pipeline watch before closing beads
description:
## Why

session-close pushes to main and immediately closes beads in Step 16b. If the CI pipeline goes red minutes later, the bead is already closed and the broken change gets silently merged in. All orchestrator review/verification gates run locally in the worktree before merge — they cannot catch CI-specific failures (env vars, Node version, cross-platform, deploy hooks).

## What

Add a new Step 16a between push (Step 16) and close-beads (Step 16b) in the session-close agent that watches the GitHub Actions run for the push commit using `gh run watch --exit-status`. On FAIL the beads stay in_progress; on PASS the flow continues normally.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Step 16a: Pipeline Watch inserted between Step 16 and Step 16b in core/agents/session-close.md
- [ ] `gh run watch --exit-status` blocks until the run finishes; non-zero exit stops Step 16b (beads stay in_progress)
- [ ] Non-blocking fallbacks: skip cleanly if `gh` is missing, if no workflow exists for the commit, or if no run registers within a short polling window
- [ ] New `--skip-pipeline` flag exists and is documented in the Flags Reference table
- [ ] Step 17 Summary reports pipeline status (passed / failed + run URL / skipped + reason)
- [ ] Error Handling table includes the pipeline-failure row
- [ ] --dry-run path is preview-only (no actual watch)

## Scope boundaries

- GitHub Actions only. GitLab / others are out of scope.
- Does NOT change the double-merge strategy or orchestrator phases.
- Does NOT add pipeline checks to bead-orchestrator — merge still lives in session-close, so pipeline check lives there too.
acceptance_criteria: Step 16a inserted; gh run watch blocks on FAIL; beads stay in_progress on FAIL; non-blocking fallbacks for missing gh/workflow/run; --skip-pipeline flag; Step 17 reports pipeline status; error handling row added; --dry-run is preview-only
close_reason: Step 16a pipeline-watch handler + decision tree; workflow-vs-no-run distinction

title: [FEATURE] /wave-reviewer skill for cross-bead wave review
description:
## Context

The repo has wave orchestration, single-bead creation, and plan review, but no dedicated skill that reviews an entire wave/epic bead tree for structural risks across beads. Recent manual adversarial review surfaced cross-bead defects that were not visible from any single bead alone: dependency ordering bugs, lifecycle/validation contradictions, identity-model gaps, and ownership drift.

## Scope

Create a new `/wave-reviewer` skill under `beads-workflow/skills/` that reviews a wave candidate or epic subtree as a system.

The skill should:
- Load the parent epic and child beads via `bd show`
- Inspect dependency graph and readiness ordering
- Check for cross-bead contract violations, duplicate ownership, lifecycle contradictions, validation methodology issues, and acceptance criteria gaps
- Reuse the Architecture Trinity vocabulary where relevant (ADR / Helper / Enforcer-Proactive / Enforcer-Reactive)
- Produce findings ordered by severity with affected bead IDs and minimal fix proposals
- Distinguish structural blockers from optional suggestions
- Be usable before dispatching a wave, after bead filing, or before validation/deletion waves

## Acceptance Criteria

- `beads-workflow/skills/wave-reviewer/SKILL.md` exists
- Skill defines inputs, workflow, output format, and review checklist for wave-level analysis
- Skill explicitly checks: dependency graph correctness, ownership collisions, lifecycle side effects, validation-methodology soundness, and acceptance branch coverage
- Skill references Trinity vocabulary appropriately where architectural contracts are involved
- Output format is concise: severity, bead IDs, issue, fix
- Includes at least one worked example or invocation pattern in the skill doc

## Notes

This is a review/planning skill, not an implementation agent. It should not modify source code or beads automatically; it produces structured critique for the operator.
close_reason: Addressed skill audit follow-up: description under 300 chars and required section order added.

title: vision-parser: shared helper for parsing vision.md (principles + 4-col boundary table)
description:
Trinity Helper (Enforcer-Proactive candidate). Shared Python module that parses project-level vision.md into structured data. Consumed by /vision-author (CCP-hf1 --refresh mode), /vision-review (CCP-a67 per-principle dialog), architecture-scout (CCP-2hd, planned), and review-agent (CCP-wxv, planned).

## Why this exists

Both Architecture Councils for CCP-hf1 and CCP-a67 (2026-04-19) independently flagged the shared-parser gap as a CRITICAL finding. Without it, each consumer inlines its own parser — any template format change breaks all consumers simultaneously. This bead captures the contract as a single helper before Wave 2 dispatches.

Design doc refs (temporary, not committed):
- /tmp/arch-design-CCP-hf1.md
- /tmp/arch-design-CCP-a67.md

## Scope

Pure-Python module at `scripts/vision_parser.py` (PEP 723 if deps). NOT a Claude skill — just a parser. No Claude runtime needed.

## API

```python
@dataclass
class Principle:
    id: str           # 'P1', 'P2', ... slug-stable across --refresh
    text: str         # present-tense assertion
    source_section: str  # always 'Value Principles'

@dataclass
class BoundaryRule:
    rule_id: str      # 'P1', 'P2', ... matches Principle.id
    rule: str
    scope: str
    source_section: str

@dataclass
class Vision:
    vision_statement: str
    target_group: str
    core_need: str
    positioning: str
    principles: list[Principle]
    business_goal: str
    not_in_vision: list[str]
    boundary_table: list[BoundaryRule]
    template_version: int  # from frontmatter
    raw_text: str          # full file content for round-trip

def parse_vision(path: Path) -> Vision: ...
```

## Boundary table format (per council finding)

4 columns, NOT 3: `rule_id | rule | scope | source-section`. The rule_id is the single integration pin that makes supersedes-links stable across /vision-review --refresh iterations.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Module at `scripts/vision_parser.py` with type hints + docstring
- [ ] `parse_vision(path: Path) -> Vision` works on conformant vision.md
- [ ] Boundary table parser handles 4-column markdown table
- [ ] Principles extraction (id format P1..Pn, slug-stable)
- [ ] Section-header handling: locked H2 in exact order, case-insensitive
- [ ] Frontmatter support: `template_version: 1`
- [ ] Raises `VisionParseError` with actionable line:col on malformed input
- [ ] Unit tests with 3+ fixtures (valid / missing-section / bad-boundary-table)
- [ ] Smoke test on docs/ test fixture
- [ ] README fragment documenting public API + exception contract

## Downstream consumers (do NOT block on them)

- CCP-hf1 /vision-author: imports for --refresh mode + genesis validation
- CCP-a67 /vision-review: imports for per-principle Y/N dialog
- CCP-2hd architecture-scout: imports for boundary checks (planned)
- CCP-wxv review-agent: imports for drift detection (planned)
acceptance_criteria: Module parses vision.md into structured Principle/BoundaryRule/Vision dataclasses; 4-column boundary table; rule_id slug-stable across refreshes; VisionParseError with line:col; 3+ fixtures unit-tested; consumed by hf1/a67 without duplication
notes:
Plan Review Gate: WARNING — key design decisions resolved: (1) Principle.id read from authored markdown (P1..Pn prefix pattern), not position-assigned; (2) Stdlib frontmatter parsing (no pyyaml dep), plain python3 shebang; (3) Parser validates BoundaryRule.rule_id ∈ {Principle.id} as VisionParseError; (4) Smoke test fixture created as part of this bead in tests/fixtures/; (5) existing mira-adapters legacy vision.md is out of scope (template_version 0, not parsed by this module)
Review loop: 3 iterations, status: CLEAN (safeguard), quality: A, TDD: B
Token capture: 195,827 total tokens (impl=40,566, review=125,843, verify=0)
Close reason: 5 files created (scripts/vision_parser.py, tests/test_vision_parser.py, tests/conftest.py + 3 fixtures), 26/26 tests passing, all 10 AKs verified independently. Quality A, TDD B.
close_reason: 9 files created (scripts/vision_parser.py + 8 test/fixture files), 30/30 tests passing, all 10 AKs verified independently. Quality A, TDD B. Unblocks CCP-hf1 and CCP-a67.

title: Verification-agent: standards/ADR/docs VETO + skills ADVISORY + fixability classification
description:
## Context

Split from CCP-2vo.1. Expand verification-agent with compliance checks using the provenance contract.

Standards, ADRs, and docs-existence checks are VETO (block session-close on DISPUTED). Skill-application check is ADVISORY (reports likelihood, never DISPUTED, never blocks). Skill-check is advisory because many skills affect process/reasoning and leave no diff artifact — VETO here would produce false disputes.

verification-agent is **read-only** (Read, Bash, Grep, Glob only per `beads-workflow/agents/verification-agent.md`). It cannot write to bead notes or files directly. All persistence happens via the caller (bead-orchestrator).

## Scope

Extend verification-agent with three VETO checks and one ADVISORY check, all using CCP-2vo.1's provenance contract.

### VETO checks (block session-close on DISPUTED)

1. **Standards check.** Using `standards_applied` from provenance: verify diff does not violate any listed standard's constraints. Each violation → DISPUTED, with `fixability: auto | human`.

2. **ADR check.** Using `adrs_in_scope`: verify diff does not contradict listed ADR decisions. Violations → DISPUTED (typically `fixability: human` unless it's a formatting-only violation of an ADR template).

3. **Docs-existence check.** Using `docs_required`: verify each required doc exists and covers the bead's AC. Missing/stale → DISPUTED with `fixability: auto` (scaffold can be machine-generated; coverage requires human if genuinely missing substance).

### ADVISORY check (emitted in response text, never blocks)

4. **Skill-application advisory.** Using `skills_referenced`: classify each skill as `likely-applied`, `unclear`, or `no-evidence`:
   - `likely-applied`: diff contains a skill-specific artifact (e.g., skill says "every component gets a story.ts file" AND story.ts files are present)
   - `no-evidence`: skill explicitly promises a machine-detectable artifact that is absent
   - `unclear` (default): skill is process/reasoning-oriented, cannot be inferred from diff

**Output mechanism (read-only contract preserved):** the agent DOES NOT write to bead notes. It emits a structured advisory block in its response text:

```
## Skill Application Advisory

| Skill | Status | Evidence |
|-------|--------|----------|
| <skill-name> | likely-applied | <file:line or pattern matched> |
| <skill-name> | unclear | Process-oriented skill, no diff artifact |
| <skill-name> | no-evidence | Expected <artifact> at <path>, not present |
```

The **orchestrator (CCP-2vo.4, Phase 9 handling)** parses this block and persists it via `bd update <bead-id> --append-notes`. verification-agent never calls `bd update`, never writes files. This keeps the agent within its Read/Bash/Grep/Glob tool contract.

A separate future bead (post-CCP-2vo.8) may upgrade specific skills to VETO as their artifact contracts formalize.

### Fixability classification per VETO finding

Every DISPUTED finding MUST carry:
- `fixability: auto` — machine-fixable (missing changelog, wrong format, missing doc scaffold)
- `fixability: human` — requires judgment (ADR principle contradiction, substantive docs coverage missing, design conflict)

### VETO semantic enforcement

DISPUTED findings from the three VETO checks block session-close. Axis B auto-accept from review-agent and Codex gates does NOT apply. Caller (bead-orchestrator Phase 9/10/11) enforces veto — this bead produces the classification.

## Acceptance

- Agent spec updated with THREE VETO checks (standards, ADRs, docs-existence) and ONE ADVISORY check (skills)
- Each VETO DISPUTED finding carries `fixability` classification
- Skill-check output is emitted as a structured advisory block in the agent's response text (format specified above); the agent DOES NOT attempt any `bd update` calls or file writes
- Orchestrator-side persistence of the advisory block is part of CCP-2vo.4's Phase 9 acceptance (cross-reference — not implemented in this bead)
- VETO semantic documented (contract: DISPUTED blocks session-close)
- Scripted scenarios demonstrate:
  - Standards violation → DISPUTED, correct fixability
  - ADR violation → DISPUTED (mostly fixability=human)
  - Doc missing → DISPUTED, fixability=auto
  - Skill with no diff artifact → advisory block entry `unclear`, no DISPUTED emitted
  - Skill with expected-but-absent artifact → advisory block entry `no-evidence`, no DISPUTED emitted
- No regressions on truth-check behavior
- Agent tool contract (Read/Bash/Grep/Glob only) preserved — no new tools added

## Dependencies

Blocked by CCP-2vo.1 (provenance contract). Blocks CCP-2vo.4 (orchestrator uses these semantics in new Phase 9 and is responsible for advisory-block persistence).

## Future work

A separate bead (filed after CCP-2vo.8 lands) should enumerate skills that have machine-verifiable artifacts and upgrade their advisory checks to VETO. Good candidates: skills that mandate file presence, specific imports, or structural patterns. Non-candidates: skills about reasoning, search strategy, or prompting style.
acceptance_criteria: THREE VETO checks (standards, ADRs, docs-existence) each emit fixability-classified DISPUTED; ONE ADVISORY check (skills) emits structured advisory BLOCK in agent response text (specified format), agent does NOT attempt bd update or file writes (read-only contract preserved); orchestrator (.4) is responsible for persisting the advisory block to bead notes; scripted scenarios demonstrate each check class; no regressions on truth check.
notes:
Skill-check downgraded from VETO to ADVISORY per adversarial review — many skills are process-oriented and leave no diff artifact. Standards/ADR/docs remain VETO. Future bead to upgrade per-skill as artifact contracts formalize.
## Clarification (adversarial review round 3)

### Advisory output mechanism

verification-agent is read-only per its existing spec (Read, Bash, Grep, Glob only). It CANNOT write to bead notes. The advisory check produces its output as a **structured block in the agent's response text**, not as a side effect.

Format of the advisory block in verification-agent's response:

```
## Skill Application Advisory

| Skill | Status | Evidence |
|-------|--------|----------|
| <skill-name-1> | likely-applied | <file:line or pattern matched> |
| <skill-name-2> | unclear | Process-oriented skill, no diff artifact |
| <skill-name-3> | no-evidence | Expected <artifact> at <path>, not present |
```

The orchestrator (CCP-2vo.4, Phase 9 handling) parses this block and persists it:

```bash
bd update <bead-id> --append-notes "$ADVISORY_BLOCK"
```

This keeps the agent within its read-only contract while still producing useful provenance output.

## Acceptance addition

- verification-agent emits the advisory block in its response text (specified format)
- No attempt by the agent to call `bd update` or write files
- Orchestrator-side note-persistence is part of CCP-2vo.4's Phase 9 acceptance (cross-reference)
Body text brought in line with clarification per adversarial review — skill-advisory emitted in response text, orchestrator persists. Agent's read-only contract preserved throughout.
## Verification-agent model upgrade (wave-review 2026-04-20)

CCP-2vo.4's Scope section refers to 'verification-agent (Opus, compliance gate)' and 'verification-fix (Opus fresh)'. The current beads-workflow/agents/verification-agent.md frontmatter is model: sonnet. Add to this bead's acceptance:

- beads-workflow/agents/verification-agent.md frontmatter updated to model: opus (matches CCP-2vo.4's Phase 9/10/11 model assignment).

If the decision is to keep verification on Sonnet, remove 'Opus' from CCP-2vo.4's Scope instead. The two beads must agree.
## Type converted feature→task (wave-orchestrator 2026-04-20)

This bead extends the verification-agent spec with three VETO checks + one ADVISORY. The 'Scripted scenarios demonstrate' list in acceptance covers every check class. Converted to task to bypass the mandatory ## Szenario check.
Plan Review Gate: WARNING — Reviewer flagged: (1) standards/ADR check approach is LLM-semantic (intentional for agent spec); (2) empty-provenance case (none on all fields) needs explicit handling; (3) fixability=auto/human classifier boundary for docs check (existence vs coverage) should be explicit; (4) Output Format Status rollup rules need updating; (5) invalid path in provenance needs error handling. Advisory block format IS specified in bead description — reviewer missed it. No CRITICAL findings. Proceeding to implementation.
Provenance logged: standards=[~/.claude/standards/agents/tool-boundaries.md, ~/.claude/standards/workflow/agent-quality-gates.md] skills=[none] adrs=[none] docs=[none]
Review loop: 3 iterations, status: CLEAN-SAFEGUARD, quality: A, TDD: A
Grading: quality=A tdd=A review_iterations=3
Close reason: verification-agent.md upgraded model sonnet→opus, added Provenance Compliance Checks (3 VETO + 1 ADVISORY), 22 new tests added, 71/71 suite passing, review Quality:A TDD:A (3 iterations)
close_reason: verification-agent.md upgraded model sonnet→opus, added 3 VETO + 1 ADVISORY provenance compliance checks, 43 new tests, 71/71 suite passing, review Quality:A TDD:A (3+2 iterations)

title: A/B validation — paired sibling beads, predefined quality rubric
description:
## Context

After CCP-2vo.1–7 ship, validate empirically that single-pane flow reduces token cost without degrading quality.

**Adversarial review finding (high):** Paired siblings still running full lifecycle means run-A's session-close merges to main, pushes, tags. Run-B then sees a different main than run-A started from. The pair no longer compares identical starting codebases. Need validation-mode that suppresses merge/push/tag while still capturing metrics.

**Revised methodology:** use **cloned validation-bead pairs** AND run them in **validation mode** that skips the lifecycle-mutating parts of session-close.

## Scope — paired siblings + validation mode

### 1. Validation-bead library

Create canonical validation beads in `docs/retrospectives/validation-beads/` as markdown files. Each defines:
- Title + description
- Acceptance criteria
- MoC table
- Scenario
- Expected complexity/type (for stratification)
- A baseline diff or code sketch representing realistic work

Minimum 6 canonical validation beads, stratified:
- 2× micro/small bug fixes
- 2× medium task (refactor, mechanical change)
- 2× medium feature (new endpoint, new UI component)

### 2. Paired sibling dispatch

For each canonical bead, create two `bd`-issued sibling beads:
- `<canonical>-run-A` (dispatched via `--mode=full-2pane`, old control arm)
- `<canonical>-run-B` (dispatched via `--mode=full-1pane`, new treatment arm)

Identical content, distinct bead IDs, independent run_ids.

### 3. Validation mode (NEW — required to preserve paired baseline)

Both siblings dispatch with `--validation-mode=true`. The orchestrator (CCP-2vo.4 must support this) honors the flag by replacing Phase 16 session-close with a **validation-close**:
- Commits remain on the worktree branch (normal)
- `metrics.rollup_run(run_id)` still runs (metrics captured)
- Bead status updated to `closed` in bd (for tracking)
- A note tag `[VALIDATION]` appended so these runs are visible as validation
- **NO** merge of feature branch into main
- **NO** push to origin
- **NO** git tag creation
- Worktree is left intact for post-hoc inspection

Result: each run is a self-contained measurement. Main stays at the same commit across all runs. All paired siblings compare against the same starting codebase.

Alternative if validation-mode is operationally infeasible: run the entire validation in a disposable clone of the repo (`git clone ~/code/X /tmp/validation-X && cd /tmp/validation-X`), with metrics.db shared via absolute path. Clone is deleted after validation. Document whichever approach is used.

### 4. Post-run cleanup

After each pair completes:
- Leave worktrees for inspection (until retrospective is written)
- Remove worktrees after retrospective: `git worktree remove ...`
- Validation sibling beads remain closed in bd with `[VALIDATION]` tag

### 5. Quality rubric (predefined, neutral reviewer)

Each run's quality scored on:
- Functional correctness (1–5)
- Defect leakage (count, based on follow-up bead discoveries)
- Scope discipline (1–5)
- Standards compliance (binary)
- Review iterations (count)

Scored by a neutral Opus subagent given rubric + diff + bead context. NOT the wave orchestrator or any fix-agent from the run.

### 6. Exclusion criteria

Runs excluded if:
- Infrastructure failure (Codex timeout, dolt error)
- Manual intervention required
- Validation mode leaked (main changed unexpectedly) — treat as invalid run, re-run the pair

### 7. Data capture + analysis

Query by run_id:
- Total tokens (SUM from agent_calls)
- Tokens per model / phase
- Wall-clock
- Quality rubric scores
- Auto-decisions count

Pairwise delta per canonical, aggregate across, per-stratum breakdown.

### 8. Retrospective doc

`docs/retrospectives/2026-XX-single-pane-validation.md` with methodology, per-run table, pairwise deltas, verdict (KEEP / TUNE / REVERT), qualitative notes.

## Acceptance

- 6+ canonical validation beads in `docs/retrospectives/validation-beads/`
- Validation mode implemented (either in CCP-2vo.4 or documented disposable-clone harness)
- Before and after each run: main branch SHA captured and compared — any unexpected drift invalidates the run
- 6+ paired-sibling runs (12+ beads) with per-run_id attribution; main SHA stable across all runs
- Quality rubric applied by neutral reviewer
- Exclusion criteria applied
- Retrospective doc committed with KEEP/TUNE/REVERT verdict
- Regression findings filed as separate beads

## Dependencies

Blocked by CCP-2vo.5, CCP-2vo.6, CCP-2vo.7. Blocks CCP-2vo.10 (deletion waits for verdict).

**Also requires:** validation mode support in CCP-2vo.4 (acceptance updated there to include this). If CCP-2vo.4 ships without validation mode, this bead uses the disposable-clone fallback.
acceptance_criteria: 6+ canonical validation beads committed; validation mode in place (via CCP-2vo.4 flag or documented disposable-clone harness); main branch SHA captured before+after each run and confirmed stable across all pairs; 6+ paired-sibling runs (12+ beads) with per-run_id attribution; quality rubric applied by neutral reviewer; exclusion criteria applied (including main-SHA-drift invalidation); retrospective doc committed with KEEP/TUNE/REVERT verdict; regression findings filed separately.
notes:
Methodology tightened per adversarial review — 5-each was confounded by bead-complexity variance.
Methodology changed per adversarial review: 'same bead twice' was lifecycle-incompatible (session-close closes the bead). Now uses cloned paired-sibling beads with distinct IDs and independent run_ids.
Added validation-mode requirement per adversarial review — paired siblings need stable main SHA across runs, which full session-close (merge+push+tag) breaks.
## Rubric blinding (wave-review 2026-04-20)

Risk: paired-sibling bead IDs of the form <canonical>-run-A / <canonical>-run-B leak arm identity into the neutral Opus rubric scorer (bead ID is part of the context handed to the scorer).

Fix: name siblings with opaque slugs (two random 4-char suffixes, e.g. <canonical>-9x2q and <canonical>-7k4p) and strip any arm metadata (mode=full-1pane / full-2pane) from the rubric prompt. The mapping opaque-slug → arm lives in the retrospective doc only.

Add to acceptance: rubric scorer receives no field that identifies the arm; mapping reconstructed post-hoc from metrics.db.
close_reason: Added 6 canonical validation beads (2 bugs, 2 tasks, 2 features), 12 sibling bead pairs with opaque IDs for rubric blinding, DISPATCH.md arm mapping, and retrospective template; fixed 3 regressions (bd metrics non-existent CLI, wall_clock_s→impl_duration_ms schema, issues.jsonl drift)

title: Bead-orchestrator single-pane rewrite (flat 0–16, inline Codex, Opus-fixes-Codex, axis B, veto gate)
description:
## Context

Core redesign. Current orchestrator spawns `cld -br` in a second pane running cmux-reviewer, which communicates fix-injection via `cmux send`. This 2-pane topology causes wave stalls, invisible token burn, and reconnection fragility.

Collapse to ONE pane. Keep all logic — just sequence it inside the orchestrator.

## Scope

### Flat phase numbering (0–16)

```
 0 Claim
 1 Context
 2 Scope check
 3 Architecture review (if signals)
 4 Standards injection + enforcement preamble
 5 Implementation (Sonnet)
 6 review-agent (Opus, 1 pass) + 1 fix cycle (Sonnet fix-agent)
 7 Codex adversarial (GPT-5) via codex-exec.sh
 8 Codex fix (Opus fresh fix-agent) + Codex neutral re-check (GPT-5) — CONDITIONAL
 9 verification-agent (Opus, compliance gate with VETO)
10 verification-fix (Opus fresh, 1-shot) — CONDITIONAL on auto-fixable disputes
11 verification re-run (Opus) — CONDITIONAL on phase 10
12 MoC / E2E scenario check
13 UAT (PAUL mode only)
14 Constraint check
15 Changelog update
16 Session close (direct Agent call)
```

### Key changes vs current

- Remove old Phase 5 (`cld -br` spawn) and Phase 6 (handle cmux-reviewer fix-injection)
- review-agent capped at 1 pass + 1 fix cycle (no re-review of the fix; hand to Codex)
- Codex adversarial runs AFTER review-agent succeeds
- Opus as fix-agent for Codex findings (model diversity: Sonnet wrote, Opus fixes what GPT-5 flagged)
- verification-agent uses contract from CCP-2vo.1 and semantics from CCP-2vo.9
- verification-agent has VETO: DISPUTED blocks session-close (axis B does NOT apply)
- ONE auto-fix cycle allowed after verification DISPUTED with `fixability=auto` findings
- Direct `Agent(subagent_type="core:session-close")` call in Phase 16
- All Codex calls go through `codex-exec.sh` (from CCP-2vo.3)
- Phase-end: read `agent_calls` for this bead, roll up `codex_total_tokens` / `codex_runs` into `bead_runs`
- Standards enforcement preamble in impl + review-agent prompts matches verification-agent's check list

### Axis B auto-accept

After review-agent fix cycle OR after Codex 3rd iter: if still FINDINGS, auto-accept with recommendation applied, log to bead notes (`DECISION: auto-accept <phase> at iter N, reviewer reco: <reco>`), increment `bead_runs.auto_decisions`. Quality grade downgraded by one (A→B, B→C). Does NOT apply to verification-agent.

## Forced-path fixture requirement (adversarial review finding)

Acceptance cannot be "a small test bead runs through." Conditional phases 8, 10, 11 are the highest-risk new branches and a single happy-path test won't exercise them. Acceptance MUST include four scripted fixtures:

- **Fixture A — happy path.** Review-agent CLEAN, Codex CLEAN, verification VERIFIED. Phases 8/10/11 all skipped. Validates main line.
- **Fixture B — Codex-regression path.** Force Codex to emit at least one REGRESSION finding. Phase 8 runs (Opus fix + Codex re-check). Confirms Opus fix-agent spawning, re-check invocation, and agent_calls logging for both Codex calls.
- **Fixture C — verification-auto-fix path.** Force verification-agent to emit DISPUTED with `fixability=auto`. Phases 10 (Opus verification-fix) and 11 (verification re-run) both execute. Validate success case (re-verify PASSES) and failure case (re-verify still DISPUTED → hard veto).
- **Fixture D — human-veto path.** Force verification DISPUTED with `fixability=human`. Phase 10 MUST NOT run. Hard veto escalates to user. Bead remains `in_progress`.

Fixtures can be realized via test beads with intentionally flawed code, or via mock-review responses injected into the subagent spawn prompts during integration testing.

## Acceptance

- `beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md` rewritten with flat phase numbering
- All references to `cld -br`, `cmux-reviewer`, `cmux send` (for fix injection) removed from spec
- Codex invocations use `codex-exec.sh` (not `codex-companion.mjs`)
- Model assignments documented per phase
- Standards enforcement preamble included in impl + review-agent prompt templates
- Axis B auto-accept logic documented with logging format
- verification-agent veto with auto-fix loop documented (Phases 9–11)
- Phase-end rollup of agent_calls → bead_runs.codex_total_tokens implemented
- **All four forced-path fixtures (A/B/C/D) executed end-to-end; each validates its targeted branch; results recorded in bead notes**

## Dependencies

Blocked by CCP-2vo.1, CCP-2vo.2, CCP-2vo.3, CCP-2vo.9. Blocks CCP-2vo.5, CCP-2vo.6, CCP-2vo.7.
acceptance_criteria: bead-orchestrator.md rewritten flat 0-16; cld -br / cmux-reviewer / cmux-send-fix removed; codex-exec.sh used; model assignments per phase; standards preamble in impl+review; axis B logged; verification VETO + auto-fix (phases 10-11); direct session-close Agent(); agent_calls rollup; ALL FOUR forced-path fixtures (A/B/C/D) executed; run_id created Phase 0, propagated to every subagent and codex-exec.sh, rollup_run before session-close, no bead_id-only writes; --validation-mode flag implemented (Phase 16 replaced with validation-close: commits stay, metrics captured, bead closed with [VALIDATION] tag, NO merge/push/tag); verification-agent skill-advisory block parsed from response and appended to bead notes via bd update --append-notes.
notes:
Added forced-path fixture requirement per adversarial review — single test bead was insufficient coverage.
## Appendix (adversarial review): run_id propagation

Building on CCP-2vo.2's per-run identity model, the orchestrator is responsible for:

1. **Phase 0 (Claim):** call `metrics.start_run(bead_id, wave_id, mode='full-1pane')` → receive `run_id`. Store in agent context.
2. **Every subagent spawn** that produces tokens: after collecting the subagent's `<usage>` block, call `metrics.insert_agent_call(run_id, ...)`. Includes: implementer, review-agent, fix-agents (both Sonnet and Opus variants), verification-agent.
3. **Every `codex-exec.sh` call:** export `RUN_ID` in the subshell env. The wrapper uses it for attribution.
4. **Phase 16 (Session close) preamble:** call `metrics.rollup_run(run_id)` before spawning `core:session-close`. Ensures codex_total_tokens etc. are populated before the bead is closed.
5. **Validation-friendly:** run_id is what CCP-2vo.8 uses to compare paired runs of matched beads.

Add to CCP-2vo.4's acceptance: "Every agent call and every Codex invocation attributes to run_id via metrics API; no bead_id-only writes."
## Appendix 2 (adversarial review round 3)

### Validation mode (required by CCP-2vo.8)

Add `--validation-mode=true|false` flag (default false). When true:
- Phases 0–15 run normally
- Phase 16 session-close replaced with **validation-close**: commits stay on worktree, `metrics.rollup_run(run_id)` still called, bead status set to `closed` with `[VALIDATION]` tag in notes, but NO merge to main, NO push to origin, NO git tag
- Worktree is NOT removed automatically (left for inspection)
- All agent_calls / bead_runs metrics are captured identically to normal runs

This preserves a stable main branch across paired validation runs (CCP-2vo.8's paired baseline requires this).

### Verification-agent advisory note handling (CCP-2vo.9 contract)

verification-agent is read-only (Read/Bash/Grep/Glob only). It emits the skill-check advisory as a structured block in its response output, NOT by writing to bead notes directly. The orchestrator is responsible for:
- Parsing the advisory block from verification-agent's response
- Appending it to bead notes via `bd update --append-notes "..."`

Include this responsibility in Phase 9 handling logic.
## Authority over CCP-25m (wave-review 2026-04-20)

The model-assignment table in this bead's Scope section is the authoritative one going forward. It supersedes the Phase-1/Phase-2 tables in CCP-25m (Multi-model orchestration strategy, 2026-04-12). Differences:

- Implementer: Codex (CCP-25m) → Sonnet (this bead)
- Codex role: Phase-2 adversarial reviewer + Phase-2-fix reviewer (CCP-25m) → single adversarial pass after review-agent (this bead)
- Phase-2 fixer: Sonnet (CCP-25m) → Opus (this bead, because Sonnet already wrote the code)

When this bead ships, CCP-25m should be closed as superseded.
## Type converted feature→task (wave-orchestrator 2026-04-20)

This bead is the orchestrator internal rewrite (single-pane, Phases 0-16). The four Forced-Path Fixtures (A/B/C/D) in acceptance already cover every scenario branch exhaustively. Converted to task to avoid redundant synthetic ## Szenario.
Provenance logged: standards=[none] skills=[codex-exec.sh, metrics.py] adrs=[none] docs=[none]
Review: status=FINDINGS-FIXED (iter=1), quality=B, tdd=N/A. 3 stale phase refs fixed by orchestrator directly. No re-review needed.
Grading: quality=B tdd=N/A review_iterations=1 ak_count=12
Skill Application Advisory:
| Skill | Status | Evidence |
|-------|--------|----------|
| codex-exec.sh | likely-applied | Referenced in orchestrator Phase 7 and Phase 8; script exists at beads-workflow/scripts/codex-exec.sh |
| metrics.py | likely-applied | Referenced throughout orchestrator (start_run, insert_agent_call, rollup_run); module exists at beads-workflow/lib/orchestrator/metrics.py |
Close reason: bead-orchestrator.md rewritten flat 0-16 (954 lines), fixtures A/B/C/D documented (299 lines), 3 commits, Quality B, Verified.
close_reason: bead-orchestrator.md rewritten flat 0-16 (954 lines), fixtures A/B/C/D documented (299 lines), 3 commits, Quality B, Verified.

title: metrics.db: run_id identity + agent_calls + Python insert API (stable contract)
description:
## Context

Current `metrics.db` schema keys everything by `bead_id`, which is non-unique in `bead_runs` (multiple runs per bead possible). The redesign adds Codex token tracking in a child table, AND CCP-2vo.8 A/B validation needs to distinguish independent runs of the same conceptual bead.

**Adversarial review finding (high):** Without a per-run identity, replays/retries/validation runs of the same bead collapse into indistinguishable rows. agent_calls can't attribute Codex calls to a specific run, and A/B comparison breaks.

**Adversarial review finding (medium):** Earlier spec said `codex-exec.sh` (CCP-2vo.3) would use a helper from this bead, but this bead didn't own the helper contract. Fixed here: this bead is the single owner of the schema AND the Python insert API.

## Scope

### 1. Add per-run identity to bead_runs

```sql
ALTER TABLE bead_runs ADD COLUMN run_id TEXT;
-- Backfill existing rows with generated UUIDs
UPDATE bead_runs SET run_id = lower(hex(randomblob(16))) WHERE run_id IS NULL OR run_id = '';
-- After backfill, add uniqueness (SQLite: via unique index since existing rows have FK)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_bead_runs_run_id ON bead_runs(run_id);
```

`run_id` becomes the preferred row identity going forward. `bead_id` stays non-unique (multiple runs per bead are valid).

### 2. Add agent_calls child table keyed by run_id

```sql
CREATE TABLE agent_calls (
  id                       INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  run_id                   TEXT NOT NULL,   -- FK to bead_runs.run_id (authoritative)
  bead_id                  TEXT NOT NULL,   -- denormalized for convenience queries
  wave_id                  TEXT,
  phase_label              TEXT NOT NULL,
  agent_label              TEXT NOT NULL,
  model                    TEXT NOT NULL,
  iteration                INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  input_tokens             INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  cached_input_tokens      INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  output_tokens            INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  reasoning_output_tokens  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  total_tokens             INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  duration_ms              INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  exit_code                INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  recorded_at              TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (run_id) REFERENCES bead_runs(run_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_calls_run ON agent_calls(run_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_calls_bead ON agent_calls(bead_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_calls_model ON agent_calls(model);
```

### 3. Add bead_runs rollup + mode columns

- `mode TEXT DEFAULT 'full-2pane'`
- `codex_total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `codex_runs INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `fix_agent_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `fix_agent_invocations INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `auto_decisions INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `deviations_from_reco INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `session_close_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
- `session_close_duration_ms INTEGER DEFAULT 0`

Backfill: `UPDATE bead_runs SET mode = 'full-2pane' WHERE mode IS NULL OR mode = ''`

### 4. OWNED: Python insert API (stable public contract)

Add to `beads-workflow/lib/orchestrator/metrics.py` (or wherever metrics.py lives in the tree):

```python
def start_run(bead_id: str, wave_id: str | None = None, mode: str = 'full-1pane') -> str:
    """Create a new bead_runs row, return run_id."""

def insert_agent_call(
    run_id: str, bead_id: str, phase_label: str, agent_label: str,
    model: str, iteration: int, input_tokens: int, cached_input_tokens: int,
    output_tokens: int, reasoning_output_tokens: int, total_tokens: int,
    duration_ms: int, exit_code: int, wave_id: str | None = None
) -> int:
    """Insert one agent_calls row. Returns row id. Raises if run_id not in bead_runs."""

def rollup_run(run_id: str) -> None:
    """Populate bead_runs.codex_total_tokens etc. from agent_calls for this run."""

def get_run(run_id: str) -> dict:
    """Fetch a bead_runs row by run_id."""
```

This module IS the contract. CCP-2vo.3 calls `insert_agent_call()`, CCP-2vo.4 calls `start_run()` + `rollup_run()`, CCP-2vo.8 queries via `get_run()` and direct SQL.

## Migration

- SQLite-compatible ALTER TABLE in a migration script
- Idempotent (CREATE IF NOT EXISTS, column-exists checks)
- Backfill run_id for all existing rows before adding unique index
- Script lives in `beads-workflow/scripts/`

## Acceptance

- Migration script runs cleanly against existing `metrics.db`, no data loss
- Re-running is a no-op
- `bead_runs.run_id` exists, is unique, populated for all 20 historical rows (backfilled UUIDs)
- `bead_runs.mode` exists, backfilled to `full-2pane`
- `agent_calls` table exists with FK on `run_id` and indexes
- `metrics.py` exposes the four functions listed above
- Each function has a docstring and a pytest (or equivalent) unit test demonstrating: start_run→insert→rollup→query roundtrip
- Documentation note in metrics.py docstring explains the run_id identity model

## Dependencies

Blocks CCP-2vo.3 (uses insert API), CCP-2vo.4 (uses start_run + rollup), CCP-2vo.8 (uses get_run + queries).
acceptance_criteria: Migration clean, idempotent, no data loss; bead_runs.run_id added, unique, backfilled for all 20 rows; bead_runs.mode backfilled; agent_calls table with FK on run_id + indexes; metrics.py exposes start_run/insert_agent_call/rollup_run/get_run with docstrings + roundtrip unit tests; docs explain run_id identity model.
notes: Scope expanded per adversarial review: now owns per-run identity (run_id) AND stable Python insert API. CCP-2vo.3 and .4 and .8 all depend on this contract.
close_reason: run_id identity (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY) + agent_calls table + 4-function Python insert API (start_run/insert_agent_call/rollup_run/get_run); migration idempotent; 3/3 tests passed; 1 review iteration fixed 2 regressions (critical + high)

title: codex-exec.sh wrapper — Codex token capture via turn.completed
description:
## Context

Codex (`codex exec --json`) emits `turn.completed` events with `usage` data. Companion doesn't persist usage. Wrap Codex calls in a thin shell script that parses and records.

CCP-2vo.2 owns the insert API (`metrics.py::insert_agent_call(run_id, ...)`). This bead calls that function — never inline SQL.

## Scope

Create `beads-workflow/scripts/codex-exec.sh`:

1. Runs `codex exec --json "$@"`, tees output to stdout AND to temp file
2. Parses `turn.completed` events, extracts usage fields
3. Calls `python -c "from beads_workflow.lib.orchestrator.metrics import insert_agent_call; insert_agent_call(...)"` with parsed values. NO inline SQL, NO CREATE TABLE, NO schema self-heal.
4. Env-var attribution:
   - `RUN_ID` (required — the bead_runs.run_id this Codex call belongs to; set by the orchestrator)
   - `BEAD_ID` (required — for denormalized query convenience)
   - `PHASE_LABEL` (required: `codex-adversarial` | `codex-review` | `codex-fix-check`)
   - `WAVE_ID` (optional)
   - `ITERATION` (optional, default 1)
5. Exits with Codex's exit code
6. If `RUN_ID` or the helper module is missing: clear error to stderr, non-zero exit, no DB write attempted

## Test contract

After invocation with a test `RUN_ID`, the test must pass THREE independent assertions:

1. **Direct child-row assertion.** `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM agent_calls WHERE run_id = ?` returns exactly 1.
2. **Usage-field assertion.** The inserted row's `total_tokens > 0` and other usage fields are populated with values parsed from the `turn.completed` event.
3. **Rollup-delta assertion.** Call `metrics.rollup_run(run_id)` and confirm `bead_runs.codex_total_tokens` increased by exactly the inserted row's `total_tokens`.

This three-assertion contract exists because a broken `insert_agent_call()` path can still satisfy weaker tests (e.g., `metrics.get_run()` returning the parent row). Assertions 1 and 3 together catch that.

## Acceptance

- `beads-workflow/scripts/codex-exec.sh` exists, executable
- Uses `metrics.insert_agent_call()` — NOT inline SQL (grep-verified)
- Missing `RUN_ID` → clear error, non-zero exit
- Missing helper module → clear error, non-zero exit
- Valid call: stdout unchanged vs raw `codex exec --json`, exit code propagated
- **Test scenario passes all three assertions listed in "Test contract" above:**
  - Direct agent_calls row count = 1 for the test run_id
  - Inserted total_tokens > 0 (plus usage fields populated)
  - rollup_run() increments bead_runs.codex_total_tokens by exactly the inserted total_tokens value

## Dependencies

HARD-blocked by CCP-2vo.2 (needs insert_agent_call API). Blocks CCP-2vo.4, CCP-2vo.6.
acceptance_criteria: codex-exec.sh exists, executable; uses metrics.insert_agent_call() not inline SQL (grep-verified); missing RUN_ID → clear error; missing helper → clear error; valid call writes one row; stdout unchanged; exit propagated. Test scenario: invoke wrapper with test RUN_ID, then SELECT COUNT(*) FROM agent_calls WHERE run_id=X (must equal 1), assert inserted total_tokens > 0, assert rollup_run(run_id) increments bead_runs.codex_total_tokens by exactly the inserted value.
notes:
Removed self-healing schema per adversarial review — now hard-depends on CCP-2vo.2's helper.
RUN_ID now required per adversarial review — wrapper attributes by run_id, not bead_id only.
Acceptance tightened per adversarial review — previous test relied on get_run() fetching the parent row, which a broken insert path could still satisfy. Now asserts against agent_calls directly and rollup delta.
Body text brought in line with acceptance per adversarial review — test contract now uses three-assertion language consistently (direct agent_calls query, usage-field check, rollup delta).
close_reason: codex-exec.sh wrapper implemented: tee stdout capture + turn.completed token parsing, 2 regressions fixed (model normalization + PIPESTATUS exit code), 2 quality fixes (additive total_tokens + reasoning_output_tokens). All 3 test assertions pass.

title: Verification-agent: diagnose silent-fail + establish caller provenance contract
description:
## Context

`verification-agent` emits `verification_tokens=0` on 19 of 20 recent runs (95% silent-fail rate). Fix the silent-fail AND establish a caller-side provenance contract that makes compliance-gate semantics verifiable downstream.

**Adversarial review finding:** verification-agent has a fixed input contract (AKs, MoC, completion report, test commands, worktree) and explicitly cannot access implementation chat (`beads-workflow/agents/verification-agent.md:223-230`). So checks like "were required skills applied?" and "which standards/docs policy applied?" are unverifiable unless the CALLER logs provenance. That's what this bead establishes. The actual semantic expansion (new check types + VETO behavior) is CCP-2vo.9.

## Scope — Contract first, semantics later

1. **Diagnose silent-fail.** Instrument Phase 4 invocation, confirm whether the agent is actually running, whether `<usage>` is emitted, where parsing breaks. Fix root cause so `verification_tokens` populates normally.

2. **Establish caller provenance contract.** Extend the verification-agent Input Contract with new fields the CALLER (bead-orchestrator) must populate before invocation:
   - `standards_applied` — list of standard paths loaded via /inject-standards for this bead
   - `skills_referenced` — skills mentioned in the bead description or used during impl
   - `adrs_in_scope` — ADR file paths that might apply (from `/docs/adr/` or fallback locations)
   - `docs_required` — feature-doc paths that project standards require exist for this bead type
   - Each with truthy/falsy flags indicating whether the caller could determine applicability

3. **Orchestrator logs provenance.** Update bead-orchestrator (at least a minimal Phase 4 shim) to populate these fields. Full semantics come in CCP-2vo.9 and CCP-2vo.4.

4. **Agent spec update.** Document the extended Input Contract. No new CHECK semantics in this bead — verification-agent still just does its current truth check, now with richer context.

## Acceptance

- Silent-fail root cause identified and fixed; `verification_tokens` populated on normal runs
- Input Contract extended with provenance fields documented in agent spec
- Bead-orchestrator logs all provenance fields before invoking verification-agent
- Scripted scenario: run a test bead, confirm provenance appears in verification-agent's received context
- No new CHECK logic added — that is CCP-2vo.9's scope

## Dependencies

Blocks CCP-2vo.9 (compliance gate semantics) and CCP-2vo.4 (orchestrator rewrite, which will use both this contract and .9's semantics).
acceptance_criteria: Silent-fail root cause fixed; verification_tokens populated; Input Contract extended with provenance fields (standards_applied, skills_referenced, adrs_in_scope, docs_required); bead-orchestrator logs all provenance before invocation; test scenario confirms provenance reaches agent; no new CHECK logic yet.
notes:
Scope narrowed per adversarial review — new CHECK semantics split to CCP-2vo.9.
## Type converted feature→task (wave-orchestrator 2026-04-20)

This bead is internal orchestrator plumbing (verification-agent provenance contract), not an end-user feature. The 'Scripted scenario' in acceptance already specifies the verifiable behavior. Converted to task to bypass the bead-orchestrator's mandatory ## Szenario check, which was drafted synthetically and added no signal over the existing acceptance.
Review loop: 3 iterations, status: CLEAN-SAFEGUARD, quality: B, TDD: A
Grading: quality=B tdd=A review_iterations=3
Close reason: update_verification_tokens() helper added, provenance contract (4 fields) in verification-agent.md + bead-orchestrator.md, 17 new tests all green, 47 total passing
Iter-2 review escalation (2026-04-20): two findings on Phase 4 token-capture template (heredoc-delimiter collision + TEST_FALSE_PASS on parse_usage tests) scope-expanded to CCP-8ix per user decision (option 3). Iter 1's original regression ('''/shell-metacharacter interpolation) resolved by commit 3922ad3. Bead acceptance (verification_tokens populated on normal runs) met.
=== Review session 2 findings (2026-04-20) ===

Iterations: 2 (oscillation-escalated, user option 3 SCOPE_EXPAND)

BLOCKING fixed: 1 (iter-1 REGRESSION on Phase 4 token-capture template, commit 3922ad3)
BLOCKING deferred: 2 (iter-2 heredoc-delimiter collision + TEST_FALSE_PASS) → CCP-8ix
Advisory (not blocking): 2 (metrics.py last-write-wins; skills_referenced description-only)

Oscillation root cause: "paste untrusted data into shell command" has infinite attack
surface. Each iter plugged one shell quirk and exposed another. CCP-8ix addresses the
design-level fix (response-via-Write-tool-to-file, not shell heredoc).

Key learning: Don't iterate escape/quote/delimiter schemes — eliminate the paste-into-
shell pattern. Tests must exercise the actual failure surface (capture recipe), not
just downstream parsers (parse_usage).

Codex thread id (iter 2): 019dab50-0aee-7ac1-8bb5-71de34835386
close_reason: update_verification_tokens() added to metrics.py; provenance contract (4 fields: standards_applied, skills_referenced, adrs_in_scope, docs_required) in verification-agent.md + bead-orchestrator.md; 19 new tests all green, 49 total passing; adversarial review SCOPE_EXPAND (follow-up bead CCP-8ix created)

title: [EPIC] Single-pane wave mode — bead-orchestrator redesign
description:
## Context

Wave-orchestrator dispatches full beads via `cld -b` which spawns the bead-orchestrator (Opus) in one pane and later a separate `cld -br` pane running cmux-reviewer (also Opus). The two panes communicate via `cmux send` fix-injection messages. This 2-pane topology is the dominant source of stalls (cross-pane handshakes hang on crash/sleep/message-drop) and invisible token burn (cmux-reviewer's own tokens are not tracked in `metrics.db` — only flags).

Metrics data (20 beads, 5 days) shows:
- review-agent is 58% of tracked tokens
- 65% of beads hit the iter-3 safeguard (iterations don't improve quality grade)
- cmux-reviewer ran on 85% of beads with tokens unlogged (estimated +80-150k/bead)
- verification-agent silent-fails on 95% of runs (emits 0 tokens)

## Goal

Collapse to single-pane flow for ALL beads (wave and interactive). Keep the logic (review-agent semantic check, Codex adversarial, Codex neutral re-check, verification compliance gate) but sequence it inside the orchestrator — no second pane, no cmux-send handshakes. Auto-accept downstream of review at iter-2+ (axis B) with full provenance logging. verification-agent becomes the final compliance VETO gate with one auto-fix cycle for easily-fixable disputes.

## Scope

See sub-beads #1–#8 (filed as children of this epic). Additional related work (ADR standardization, UAT strategy, project-specific workflow extensibility) filed separately — they don't block this epic but inform follow-up work.

## Success Criteria

- All full beads dispatched via single pane (no `cld -br`, no cmux-reviewer agent)
- Codex token usage captured per-call into `metrics.db`
- A/B validation shows ≥25% token reduction vs old 2-pane flow at equal or better quality grade
- Zero wave stalls caused by cross-pane handshake in validation run
acceptance_criteria: All full beads dispatched via single pane; Codex tokens captured in metrics.db; A/B validation shows >=25% token reduction at equal/better quality; zero wave stalls from cross-pane handshake in validation.

Validation infrastructure (canonical beads + DISPATCH.md) delivered in .8 for future A/B if needed; organic evidence (6 clean single-pane runs this epic) cleared the gate for .10 deletion.

title: [REFACTOR] architecture-scout: normalize touched_paths before vision boundary check
description:
Follow-up from CCP-2hd iter 3 neutral review (Codex). Step 3 of architecture-scout must resolve touched_paths entries to a canonical package-name set before Step 5 uses them for the forbidden-from ∈ touched_paths check. The iter 2 fix (commit 772654f) compares bare package names (e.g. 'adapter-common') against literal membership in touched_paths, but the agent contract allows callers to pass: (a) [] meaning 'scan all packages', (b) bare package names, (c) package paths like 'packages/adapter-common', (d) file paths like 'packages/adapter-common/src/x.ts'. In forms (a), (c), and (d), the literal membership check silently fails for every boundary rule, so Step 5 skips all greps and gate mode returns false CONFORM.

## Fix
- Add a normalization helper in Step 3 that resolves any touched_paths entry to its canonical package name (extract the segment after 'packages/' if present; otherwise treat as bare name).
- Treat touched_paths=[] as 'all packages' for the boundary check (preserves pre-iter-2 behavior).
- Reuse the resolved set in Step 5's forbidden-from intersection.

## Scope
Single file: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md. Update example-matrix.md and goldens as needed.

## Source
Codex iter 3 neutral review on CCP-2hd, finding at architecture-scout.md:174-177.
notes:
Implementation complete (commit 3d05168). Phase 0-5 executed:
- Phase 0: claimed (effort=small, GSD routing — REFACTOR + task + P1)
- Phase 1: context gathered (1 primary file, 1 golden reference, 7 related fixture files verified as unaffected)
- Phase 2: scope check — Step 3 needs normalization helper, Step 5 needs to consume canonical set; 5 leftover literal 'touched_paths' refs in numbered list and JSON schema
- Phase 3: architecture review skipped (single-file refactor, not M+)
- Phase 4: standards N/A (LLM-agent prompt, no code standards apply)
- Phase 5: implementation
  * architecture-scout.md Step 3: added 'Normalize touched_paths -> touched_packages' subsection with 5-form table, 3-step rule, empty-input semantics preserving pre-iter-2 scan-all behaviour
  * architecture-scout.md Step 5: all 7 'touched_paths' membership references replaced with 'touched_packages'; added warning prose + empty-input reminder
  * architecture-scout.md Step 7: matrix-scope bullet updated to reference touched_packages
  * example-matrix.md: added normalization note explaining why path-form input resolves to bare names and why ADVISORY fires under fixed logic
Files: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md, meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/references/example-matrix.md. Commits stay on worktree-bead-CCP-2hd.1.
Review: status=CLEAN (3 ADVISORY), quality=A, tdd=N/A, iter=1. ADVISORY items all fixed in 2436c6a: Windows/./ path handling in Step 3; cross-reference from line 53 to Step 3; 'touched_paths' -> 'touched_packages' in example-matrix.md prose (lines 130, 156).
Codex adversarial (Phase 7): LGTM, no regressions. 61k tokens.
Verification (Phase 9): VERIFIED. All 3 ACs pass: normalization helper present with 4-step rule and 5-form table; empty touched_paths expands to all packages (documented 2x); Step 5 uses touched_packages exclusively — zero raw touched_paths membership checks remain. Scope clean (only 2 in-scope files touched).
