Step 3 adds canonical touched_packages set (handles empty list, bare names, package paths, file paths, ./ prefix, Windows separators); Step 5 consumes canonical set for boundary intersection. Review Grade A (CLEAN), Codex LGTM, verification VERIFIED. Released as 2026.04.70.

title: tense-gate lint: enforce vision.md is present-tense only (rejects will/should/STUB/TBD)
description:
Foundation lint script that scans vision.md (and any document declared as 'prescriptive present') for future-tense markers and blocks them. Implements the 'vision has no future tense' rule mechanically.

**Why P1**: this is the single mechanism that makes /vision-author and /vision-review trustable. Without it, 'vision is present-tense only' is convention. With it, the rule is enforced at every save and every commit. Also immediately useful on existing mira-adapters vision.md (will flag current STUB sections).

## Markers to flag (case-insensitive)

- `will <verb>` (e.g. 'will support', 'will be implemented')
- `should <verb>` (in vision context — 'should be tested' is acceptance criteria, not vision)
- `plan to` / `planning to` / `going to`
- `by Q[1-4]`, `by [month]`, `by [year]`
- `eventually`, `later`, `in the future`
- `TBD` anywhere
- `STUB` anywhere
- `to be locked by`, `to be decided`, `to be determined`
- `TODO` (in vision.md only — not in code comments elsewhere)

## Implementation

- PEP 723 Python script (~80 LOC, regex-based)
- Path: claude-code-plugins/scripts/tense-gate.py (or new dedicated plugin)
- Three integration points:
  1. **CLI**: `tense-gate.py docs/vision.md` — exits 0 clean, 1 on violations
  2. **Pre-commit hook**: provided .pre-commit-hooks.yaml entry, scoped to docs/vision.md and any file with frontmatter `document_type: prescriptive-present`
  3. **Skill integration**: called from /vision-author at write-time and /vision-review before saving edits

## Output format

Per violation:
```
docs/vision.md:42:1: [will] Future-tense marker in prescriptive document.
  Found: 'The platform will support multi-tenant routing'
  Suggest: rewrite as principle ('The platform supports multi-tenant routing') OR move to bead/roadmap
```

## Allowlist

Comments with `<!-- tense-gate-ignore: <reason> -->` on the preceding line skip a single line. Use sparingly; document reason.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script exists with PEP 723 frontmatter, executable
- [ ] All listed markers detected
- [ ] CLI exits 0 on clean / 1 on violations with actionable output
- [ ] .pre-commit-hooks.yaml entry provided
- [ ] Allowlist mechanism (`tense-gate-ignore` comment) works
- [ ] Smoke test on existing mira-adapters/docs/vision.md: lists current STUB/TBD/'to be locked' sections as violations
- [ ] README/docs explain which markers are flagged and why
acceptance_criteria: Script flags all future-tense markers in vision.md; pre-commit hook integration works; smoke test on mira-adapters vision.md surfaces existing STUB sections as violations
notes: Foundation for /vision-author and /vision-review skills. Sibling beads will depend on this. Born from the 2026-04-19 mira-agent reframe: vision has no future tense; anything future-tense belongs in beads.
close_reason: tense-gate.py implemented: 16 future-tense markers detected, fenced code block awareness, allowlist mechanism, pre-commit hook, docs. 2 review iterations, 2 regressions fixed (quoted frontmatter, fenced false positives).

title: [CHORE] Bead-Pflicht-Sektion 'Architecture Contracts Touched' + bd lint-Check
description:
Führt eine Pflicht-Sektion in der Bead-Description ein: welche Contracts werden berührt, welche Helper/Enforcer kommen zum Einsatz, welche Gaps sind zu schließen. Erzwungen durch bd lint --check=architecture-contracts.

## Template-Sektion

```markdown
## Architecture Contracts Touched
- ADR-NNN (Name): <was der Bead tut, wie er den Contract nutzt>
- Helper: <pfad/zu/helper.ts>
- Enforcer-Proactive: <codegen/builder>
- Enforcer-Reactive: <lint-rule oder test>

## Coverage Expected
- Packages: <liste der berührten Packages>
- Status nach Bead: <was wird grün in der Matrix>

## Gaps to Close
- [ ] Keine / oder: [ ] [ADR-NEEDED] ..., [HELPER-NEEDED] ..., [ENFORCER-*-NEEDED] ...
```

## Enforcement

bd lint --check=architecture-contracts prüft bei Beads mit Label 'touches-contract', ob die Sektionen vorhanden sind. Fehlt sie → fail.
design: Check läuft als externes Skript, von bd lint geladen. Parser: einfache Regex gegen die drei Section-Header. Output: strukturierte Fehler mit Zeilenangabe im Bead-Markdown.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create/SKILL.md dokumentiert neue Pflicht-Sektion
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create liefert Template mit den 3 Sub-Sektionen
- [ ] beads-workflow/scripts/bd-lint-contracts.sh (oder .py) als neuer Check
- [ ] bd lint --check=architecture-contracts funktioniert (exit 1 bei Fehlern)
- [ ] Doku in beads-workflow/skills/create/references/contract-sections.md
- [ ] Label 'touches-contract' als Konvention dokumentiert
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split. Unabhängig von anderen P1-Beads — parallel machbar.
## Refinements migrated from CCP-u87 (Reading Corpus, superseded 2026-04-19)

The 'Architecture Contracts Touched' Pflicht-Sektion is the right home for the Reading-Corpus mechanism. Specific additions:

- **Per-bead-type × per-layer routing** (not flat list). Feature/refactor/bug × layer:* matrix. Flat 'always read these' lists get ignored.
- **Source: .claude/project-config.yml** (per project). Projects without this file get a degenerate single-layer config — no harness surprise.
- **Named/parseable section in project-context template** (locked H2 header + table format) so other tools can consume without re-parsing prose.
- **Surface BOTH vision.md AND project-context.md** in the corpus. vision.md is primary input during bead-creation (prescriptive principles); project-context.md is primary during implementation (descriptive stack).
- **Tense gate (NEW foundation bead)**: vision.md sentences with future-tense markers (will/should/plan to/by Q[n]/eventually/TBD/STUB/to be locked) are rejected. Mechanically enforces the 'vision has no future tense' model. See sibling bead [tense-gate].

Example .claude/project-config.yml shape:
```yaml
reading_corpus:
  bead_creation:
    default: [docs/vision.md, docs/adr/]
    by_layer:
      platform: [docs/vision.md#principles, docs/adr/ADR-005-identity.md]
      ig: [docs/vision.md#13, https://npm.cognovis.de]
  implementation:
    default: [docs/project-context.md]
    by_layer:
      pvs-adapter: [packages/pvs-adapter/CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture/pvs-integration.md]
```
Plan Review Gate: CRITICAL — Three blocking issues: (1) bd lint --check=architecture-contracts is impossible on compiled binary — invocation mechanism unresolved; (2) No mechanism defined to enumerate beads labeled 'touches-contract'; (3) Parser strictness/section validation not specified. Bead reset to open pending decision on these load-bearing questions.
## Resolved Design Decisions (2026-04-19, post-orchestrator-questions)

The first /beads CCP-0hr run surfaced 10 ambiguities. All resolved here so the re-launched orchestrator can proceed without re-asking.

### Parser strictness

- **3a — Empty section (header, no bullets)**: **FAIL**. Empty sections defeat the purpose; force authors to declare.
- **3b — Bullet format**: **HYBRID**. Require ≥1 `- ADR-NNN (Name): ...` bullet. `- Helper:`, `- Enforcer-Proactive:`, `- Enforcer-Reactive:` are optional but must use the correct prefix if present.
- **3c — `## Gaps to Close` no-gaps marker**: Require **explicit `- [ ] None`** bullet (or `- [ ] [ADR-NEEDED] ...` / `[HELPER-NEEDED]` / `[ENFORCER-PROACTIVE-NEEDED]` / `[ENFORCER-REACTIVE-NEEDED]`). Forces explicit ack; parseable by architecture-scout / allowlist-burndown.

### Scope of beads checked

- **2a — Enumeration**: scope = open beads with `touches-contract` label by default (`bd list --label touches-contract --status=open`). Configurable via CLI flag (`--all` to include closed, `--bead <id>` for single-bead check).
- **2c — Label false-negative protection**: **X + Y**.
  - X: trust opt-in label only (linter checks beads with `touches-contract`).
  - Y: catch missing-label case — if description contains `## Architecture Contracts Touched` section but lacks the label, fail with "missing label".
  - Z (heuristic warn) NOT enabled.

### Operational

- **W1 — Linter trigger**: Manual via `bd lint --check=architecture-contracts` AND skill-level auto-check in `/create` when `touches-contract` label is set during bead creation. **NOT a git pre-commit hook** — bead descriptions live in Dolt, not git, so git hooks are the wrong layer (verified via `bd hooks --help`: only git events available, no bead-mutation events). Authors who set the label get the check immediately at create-time; the manual command exists for retroactive runs and CI.
- **W2 — `/create` skill scope**: Section is added **only when `touches-contract` label is set**. `/create` asks: "Does this bead touch an architectural contract?" and conditionally injects the template.
- **W3 — Legacy beads**: No special handling. Linter naturally skips beads without `touches-contract` label (per opt-in design from W2). No created_at cutoff, no backfill.
- **W4 — Script language**: **Python (`.py`, stdlib only)** per CLAUDE.md convention. Plain `#!/usr/bin/env python3` shebang, no PEP 723 needed (stdlib regex + glob suffice).
- **W5 — Tests**: **Required**. Ship fixture-based test suite as an acceptance criterion. Fixtures: good bead, bad bead per failure mode (empty section, missing ADR bullet, malformed gaps section, missing label with section present), edge cases.

### Updated acceptance criteria (supersedes original list)

- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create/SKILL.md documents new mandatory section (only when `touches-contract` label set)
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create offers template with the 3 sub-sections + auto-call to lint after create
- [ ] beads-workflow/scripts/bd-lint-contracts.py (Python stdlib, plain `#!/usr/bin/env python3`)
- [ ] `bd lint --check=architecture-contracts` invokes the script (exit 1 on failures)
- [ ] CLI flags: default scopes to open beads with label; `--all` includes closed; `--bead <id>` checks single bead
- [ ] Parser implements 3a/3b/3c rules above
- [ ] Label false-negative check (Y) detects "section without label" and fails
- [ ] Documentation in beads-workflow/skills/create/references/contract-sections.md
- [ ] Label `touches-contract` documented as opt-in convention
- [ ] **Test suite**: fixture-based (good/bad/edge-case beads), runs in CI

### Out of scope (deferred)

- Pre-commit git hook (verified non-applicable per W1 analysis)
- Heuristic warn (option Z in 2c) — false-positive risk
- Backfill labels on legacy beads (W3 option C)
- Session-close integration (W1 option D) — keep enforcement local to bead lifecycle
issues.jsonl: bd pre-commit hook re-adds root issues.jsonl on each commit in worktrees. Main already has it removed (f12ce18). Session-close merge resolves this automatically — worktree-only artifact.
Review loop: 3 iterations, status: CLEAN-SAFEGUARD, quality: B, TDD: A. Residual: issues.jsonl untracking fought by bd pre-commit hook; will resolve via session-close merge. _bead_has_label fragility documented as follow-up.
Close reason: bd_lint_contracts.py (386 lines, stdlib), shell extension, 38 tests passing, SKILL.md Phase 3/4.5 added, references/contract-sections.md. Quality B, TDD A. Verified VERIFIED by verification-agent.
close_reason: bd_lint_contracts.py (386+ lines, Python stdlib, 47 tests), shell extension bd-lint-extension.sh, SKILL.md Phase 3+4.5, contract-sections.md reference. Rules 3a/3b/3c/Y + optional prefix validation + NEEDED marker description requirement. Quality B, TDD A.

title: [FEATURE] project-context: Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion generieren
description:
Erweitert dev-tools/skills/project-context um eine automatisch generierte Enforcement-Matrix pro Repo. Beantwortet die Frage 'welche 5 Docs muss ich lesen bevor ich X anfasse'.

## Problem

Docs sind heute verstreut (overview, project-context, feature docs, package CLAUDE.md, beads). Kein Single-Index.

## Lösung

Matrix-Tabelle Contract × Package × (ADR / Helper / Proactive / Reactive) als neue Sektion in docs/project-context.md. Leere Zellen = sichtbare Gaps.

Beispiel:
| Contract       | adapter-common | adapter-cli | pvs-x-isynet | pvs-charly | pvs-xdt |
|----------------|----------------|-------------|--------------|------------|---------|
| ID Taxonomy    | ❌ (hoist)    | ❌          | ✅           | ⚠️ teil    | ❌       |
| Schema Codegen | n/a            | n/a         | ✅           | ❌          | ❌       |

## Generierung

- ADRs: Scan docs/adr/**/*.md Frontmatter für 'contract', 'applies_to', 'status'
- Helpers: Scan packages/**/src/ für Helper-Index (per Convention: jsdoc-Tag oder export-Name)
- Proactive: Scan für Codegen-Skripte (scripts/*gen*.ts, package.json 'schema:*')
- Reactive: Scan für Lint-Rules (eslint.config.js Regel-Namen), Test-Scripts (package.json 'check:*')

## Scenario
**Precondition:** A repository checked out locally with at least: (a) `docs/adr/` directory containing one or more `*.md` ADR files with YAML frontmatter exposing `contract:`, `applies_to:` (list of package names), and `status:` (`accepted` | `proposed` | `superseded`); (b) one or more `packages/*/` directories holding source code under `src/` and a `package.json`; (c) optional `eslint.config.js` (or `.eslintrc.*`) at repo root or per-package; (d) optional codegen scripts under `scripts/` and/or `package.json` scripts matching `schema:*`, `*gen*`, `check:*`. The skill `dev-tools/skills/project-context` is installed and the existing `docs/project-context.md` either does not exist yet or is overwritable (`--force` or interactive confirm).
**Action:** The user runs `/project-context` (or `/project-context --force`) from the repository root. The skill executes its existing Phase 0–4 workflow plus the new Enforcement-Matrix scan pass: it globs `docs/adr/**/*.md`, parses YAML frontmatter to derive `(contract, applies_to[], status)` tuples; globs `packages/*/` to derive the column set; scans each package's `src/` for helper exports/jsdoc tags tagged with a contract; scans `scripts/*gen*.{ts,js,py}` and `package.json` `schema:*` / `*gen*` script names for proactive enforcers; scans `eslint.config.js` rule names and `package.json` `check:*` scripts for reactive enforcers. Results are reduced to one cell per `(contract, package)` intersection across four sub-columns (ADR / Helper / Enforcer-Proactive / Enforcer-Reactive).
**Expected Result:** `docs/project-context.md` contains a new top-level section `## Enforcement Matrix` (placed after `Module Map`, before `Established Patterns`) with: (1) a Markdown table whose rows are contracts (sorted alphabetically) and whose columns are packages (sorted alphabetically); (2) each cell shows four status symbols in fixed order `ADR | Helper | Proactive | Reactive` using `✅` (present), `⚠️` (partial — e.g. ADR exists but `status: proposed`, or helper exists but no enforcer), `❌` (missing), `n/a` (declared not-applicable via ADR `applies_to` exclusion); (3) a legend below the table explaining the four sub-columns and four symbols; (4) a "Gaps" sub-section listing every `(contract, package)` pair where any sub-column is `❌`, sorted by gap-count descending; (5) the skill's Phase 4 report includes a `gap-found` signal line `Enforcement gaps: N (across M contracts)` that downstream skills (`/allowlist-burndown`, `wave-orchestrator` Coverage-Gate) can grep for.

### Test Data
Fixture project at `dev-tools/skills/project-context/tests/fixtures/mira-adapters-snapshot/` mirroring a slice of the real mira-adapters monorepo. Layout:
- `docs/adr/` — 3 ADRs:
  - `0001-id-taxonomy.md` (frontmatter: `contract: ID Taxonomy`, `applies_to: [adapter-common, pvs-x-isynet, pvs-charly]`, `status: accepted`)
  - `0002-schema-codegen.md` (`contract: Schema Codegen`, `applies_to: [pvs-x-isynet, pvs-charly, pvs-xdt]`, `status: accepted`)
  - `0003-error-envelope.md` (`contract: Error Envelope`, `applies_to: [adapter-common]`, `status: proposed` — drives `⚠️`)
- `packages/adapter-common/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` (helper export, no enforcer); `package.json` with `check:ids` script (reactive enforcer present).
- `packages/adapter-cli/` — `src/index.ts`, `package.json`, no helpers, no enforcers (every cell `❌` or `n/a`).
- `packages/pvs-x-isynet/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` + `src/schema/` directory, `scripts/gen-schema.ts` (proactive enforcer), `package.json` with `schema:check` script (reactive), `eslint.config.js` with rule `mira/no-raw-id`. Full `✅` row.
- `packages/pvs-charly/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` (helper), missing codegen script (drives `⚠️ teil` for Schema Codegen).
- `packages/pvs-xdt/` — empty `src/`, no enforcers (drives `❌` row to demonstrate gap detection).
- Expected golden output: `tests/fixtures/mira-adapters-snapshot/expected-enforcement-matrix.md` (snapshot of the rendered section the unit test asserts against).

### Variants
1. **Empty matrix**: project with `docs/adr/` empty (or absent) and any package layout — section renders header + legend + "No contracts declared yet — add ADRs to populate this matrix." placeholder, no table, no `gap-found` signal.
2. **No packages**: project with ADRs but no `packages/*/` directories (single-package repo or `src/` at root) — section renders a single-column table where the column is the repo root, contracts still listed as rows.
3. **Partial cell (`⚠️`)**: ADR exists with `status: proposed`, OR helper present but no enforcer of either kind, OR proactive present but reactive missing — cell shows `⚠️` and the Gaps sub-section lists the partial pair.
4. **Full gap row**: contract declared in an ADR but no package has a helper, proactive, or reactive enforcer — entire row renders `❌` across all `applies_to` packages, `gap-found` signal triggers and Phase 4 report flags this contract first in the gaps list.
5. **n/a propagation**: ADR `applies_to` excludes a package — that `(contract, package)` cell shows `n/a` in all four sub-columns and is NOT counted as a gap.
6. **Re-run idempotency**: running `/project-context --section=enforcement-matrix` on a repo whose docs/project-context.md already contains the section regenerates only that section in place, preserves the rest of the file, and emits the same gap count for unchanged inputs.
design: Scanner als Python-Skript (stdlib, kein uv nötig — nur glob+regex). Läuft als Teil des project-context skill. Outputs JSON zwischen Scan-Passes, rendert Markdown-Tabelle am Ende.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] dev-tools/skills/project-context erweitert um enforcement-matrix-Generierung
- [ ] Ausgabe im generierten docs/project-context.md unter neuer Sektion 'Enforcement Matrix'
- [ ] Status-Symbole: ✅ vollständig, ⚠️ teilweise, ❌ fehlt, n/a nicht zutreffend
- [ ] Unit-Test mit mira-adapters-Snapshot als Fixture
- [ ] Empty cells führen zu 'gap-found'-Signalen im skill output
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split. Blockiert: /allowlist-burndown, wave-orchestrator Coverage-Gate.
## Refinement migrated from CCP-u87 (Reading Corpus, superseded 2026-04-19)

The Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion in project-context should also acknowledge that vision.md is a peer prescriptive document, not just a downstream consumer. Specifics:

- **bead-orchestrator Phase 2 loads BOTH files**: vision.md (prescriptive present, principles + boundaries) + project-context.md (descriptive present, stack + patterns). One-line widening of current Phase 2 logic.
- **Routing**: vision.md primary during /create (boundaries, principles, anti-goals); project-context.md primary during implementation (stack, patterns, established conventions).
- vision.md is the source of truth for the boundary table that architecture-scout (CCP-2hd) parses; project-context Enforcement-Matrix is the source of truth for which Trinity artifacts (ADR/Helper/Enforcer) exist per contract.
- Together they form the constitution: vision = prescriptive ('what MUST hold'), project-context = descriptive ('what IS true').
Review loop: 3 iterations, status: CLEAN (safeguard at iter 3), quality: B, TDD: B
Close reason: enforcement_matrix_scanner.py (564 lines stdlib Python), 17 unit tests, mira-adapters fixture (3 ADRs, 5 packages), SKILL.md Phase 3.5 added, quality B/TDD B — all 5 AKs verified
close_reason: enforcement_matrix_scanner.py (stdlib Python, contract-aware Trinity detection), 21 unit tests, mira-adapters fixture with golden output, SKILL.md Phase 3.5 — all 5 AKs verified, Quality B, TDD A

title: [FEATURE] Agent architecture-scout: Coverage-Matrix-Report vor /plan
description:
Read-only Agent, der VOR /plan und /epic-init läuft und eine Coverage-Matrix (Contract × Package × ADR/Helper/Proactive/Reactive) produziert. Erkennt Missing-ADRs, Hoist-Schulden, Pre-Trinity-Packages.

## Problem

Heute startet /plan ohne expliziten Contract-Check. Implizite Annahmen über IDs, Scoping, Delta-Semantik bleiben unsichtbar bis ein Bug sie freilegt.

## Vorgehen

- Neuer Agent: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only)
- Input: Domain-Kontext (Bead-Beschreibung + berührte Pfade)
- Output: Coverage-Matrix mit drei Sektionen:
  1. Vorhandene Contracts (Pfad:Zeile Pflichtlektüre)
  2. Implizite Contracts erkannt (Heuristik: stringly-typed, nur-in-einer-Datei-Rules, wiederkehrende bd search-Treffer)
  3. Empfohlene Tripel mit Stub-Titeln

## Integration

/plan und /epic-init spawnen den Scout am Anfang und hängen Report ans Plan-Dokument. wave-orchestrator nutzt den Report als Gate (separater Bead).

## Scenario

**Precondition:** Project has a `meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/` plugin installed with `agents/architecture-scout.md` registered. The target project being planned contains:
- `docs/adr/` with at least one ADR using YAML frontmatter declaring `contract` + `enforcement` fields
- `packages/*/` with `src/` modules and at least one `CLAUDE.md` per package
- A `vision.md` (or `docs/vision.md`) with a locked boundary table (columns: `rule | scope | source-section`)
- Optionally `.claude/project-config.yml` setting `architecture_scout.mode: advisor|gate` (default: `advisor`)
- `/plan` and `/epic-init` skills updated to spawn `architecture-scout` as a pre-implementation step

**Action:** A user invokes `/plan CCP-XXX` (single-bead) or `/epic-init "feature description"` on a feature that touches one or more `packages/*/` paths. The skill:
1. Spawns `architecture-scout` via `Task(subagent_type="architecture-trinity:architecture-scout")` with input contract `{ bead_id, bead_description, touched_paths[], mode }`
2. Scout reads `docs/adr/**/*.md`, `vision.md`, `packages/**/CLAUDE.md`, and uses Grep/Glob (read-only) to build the matrix
3. Scout returns a structured JSON Coverage-Matrix to the caller
4. `/plan` appends the JSON + a human-readable Markdown rendering to the plan document under `## Coverage Matrix (architecture-scout)`
5. In `gate` mode: any finding with `severity: BLOCKING` halts `/plan` at Step 0; in `advisor` mode: BLOCKING findings become DECIDE items in `## Developer Decisions`

**Expected Result:**
- Plan document contains a `## Coverage Matrix (architecture-scout)` section with three subsections:
  1. **Existing Contracts** — table `Contract | ADR | Helper | Proactive | Reactive | Status` (rows: `full-trinity`, `partial`, `pre-trinity`)
  2. **Implicit Contracts Detected** — heuristic-flagged candidates with `Pattern | Location | Suggested Triple`
  3. **Recommended Triples** — stub titles for missing ADRs/Helpers/Enforcers
- Scout JSON output validates against the schema (CONFORM/VIOLATION + findings[] with rule, concern, severity, source)
- Advisor mode: orchestrator continues; user sees DECIDE items
- Gate mode + BLOCKING finding: `/plan` exits with `BLOCKED: architecture-scout reported N blocking findings — see <plan>.md#coverage-matrix`
- `CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1` env var bypasses gate (logged as warning)
- Scout writes no files; only returns structured output to the caller

### Test Data

Use the existing fixture at `dev-tools/skills/project-context/tests/fixtures/mira-adapters-snapshot/` as the base (3 ADRs, 5 packages including `adapter-common`, `adapter-cli`, `pvs-charly`, `pvs-x-isynet`, `pvs-xdt`).

Construct three contract states in the fixture:

1. **Full Trinity (CONFORM):** `ADR-0001 id-taxonomy` + `makeIdHelper()` in `packages/adapter-common/src/id-taxonomy.ts` + ESLint rule `no-stringly-typed-id` (Reactive) + codegen script `scripts/gen-id-types.ts` (Proactive). Used consistently across `pvs-charly` and `pvs-x-isynet`.

2. **Partial / Pre-Trinity (ADVISORY):** `ADR-0003 error-envelope` + `reconciliation-spec-check` Helper in `adapter-common` BUT no Reactive enforcer. Scout flags: "Helper exists but no Reactive enforcer — drift risk in `pvs-xdt`".

3. **Implicit Contract (BLOCKING in gate mode):** No ADR for delta semantics, but Grep heuristic finds `if (event.kind === 'created')` patterns repeated across 4 files in `pvs-charly` and `pvs-x-isynet`. Scout flags: `Recommended Triple: ADR-00XX delta-event-kind + makeDeltaKindHelper + lint rule no-stringly-typed-event-kind`.

4. **Vision boundary violation (BLOCKING):** `vision.md` declares `layer:platform must not depend on layer:application`. Scout greps imports in `packages/adapter-common/src/` (platform layer) and finds an import from `packages/pvs-charly/src/` (application layer) — emits `severity: BLOCKING` with `source: docs/vision.md#L142`.

Expected JSON output snapshot:
```json
{
  "status": "VIOLATION",
  "mode": "advisor",
  "findings": [
    {"rule": "id-taxonomy trinity", "concern": "All four artifacts present", "severity": "INFO", "source": "docs/adr/0001-id-taxonomy.md"},
    {"rule": "error-envelope reactive missing", "concern": "Helper exists but no ESLint rule", "severity": "ADVISORY", "source": "docs/adr/0003-error-envelope.md"},
    {"rule": "implicit delta-event-kind", "concern": "Stringly-typed event.kind in 4 files; no ADR", "severity": "ADVISORY", "source": "packages/pvs-charly/src/sync.ts:42"},
    {"rule": "vision §4 boundary: platform→application", "concern": "Forbidden import", "severity": "BLOCKING", "source": "docs/vision.md#L142"}
  ]
}
```

### Variants

| Variant | Setup | Expected Behavior |
|---------|-------|-------------------|
| **Empty matrix** | Project has no `docs/adr/` and no `vision.md` | Scout returns `status: CONFORM`, empty `findings[]`, plan section notes "No contracts declared yet — consider running `/project-context` first" |
| **Advisor mode (default), BLOCKING finding** | `mode: advisor`, vision boundary violation present | Scout returns `status: VIOLATION` with BLOCKING finding; `/plan` continues, surfaces finding as DECIDE item: "Vision boundary violation detected — accept risk / add waiver / abort?" |
| **Gate mode, BLOCKING finding** | `.claude/project-config.yml` sets `mode: gate`, same violation | `/plan` exits at Step 0 with non-zero status; bead remains unplanned; user must fix violation, add waiver, or set `CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1` |
| **Gate mode, ADVISORY only** | `mode: gate`, only ADVISORY findings (e.g. partial trinity, no BLOCKING) | `/plan` continues normally; advisory findings surfaced as DECIDE items but do not block |
| **CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1 bypass** | Gate mode + BLOCKING finding + env var set | `/plan` proceeds with warning logged: `WARN: architecture-scout BLOCKING findings bypassed via CONFORMANCE_SKIP` |
| **`/epic-init` per-subtask** | Epic with 3 sub-tasks each touching different packages | Scout invoked once per sub-task with that sub-task's `touched_paths`; each sub-task bead gets its own coverage-matrix snippet in its description |
| **No `touched_paths` provided** | `/plan` invoked on a description without file hints | Scout falls back to scanning all `packages/*/` and `docs/adr/**`; reports all matrix rows; flags this as `coverage: full-scan` in JSON output |
| **Vision-behind case (impossible by construction)** | User asks "what if vision is behind code?" | Not implemented — vision has no future tense (future-tense items are beads, not principles). One-way drift only: `code-violates-stated-principle`. Scout never emits "vision-behind" findings |
design: Agent lebt in neuem Plugin meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/. Nutzt Grep auf docs/adr/**/*.md + docs/architecture/**/*.md + packages/**/src/**/*.ts für Pattern-Erkennung. Keine eigenen Dateien schreiben — nur Report zurückgeben an Caller.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Agent meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md existiert mit Frontmatter (tools: Read, Grep, Glob)
- [ ] Agent produziert Coverage-Matrix-Output in definiertem Markdown-Format
- [ ] Heuristik für Implizite-Contract-Erkennung dokumentiert (mindestens 3 Pattern)
- [ ] /plan skill ruft architecture-scout auf und hängt Output ans Plan-Dokument
- [ ] /epic-init skill ruft architecture-scout pro Subtask auf
- [ ] Test-Fixture: ein Beispiel-Matrix-Report in references/example-matrix.md
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split (CCP-xxx). Blockiert: wave-orchestrator Coverage-Gate. Sonny/Haiku-fähig.
## Refinements migrated from CCP-k1u (conformance-reviewer, superseded 2026-04-19)

architecture-scout absorbs the conformance-review function. Key additions:

**Two run modes** (configurable in .claude/project-config.yml):

- `advisor` (default): findings surfaced as DECIDE items to user; orchestrator continues
- `gate`: BLOCKING findings stop bead-orchestrator at Phase 0; plan does not proceed

Default advisor so the agent doesn't block forward motion. Projects opt into gate when their vision/invariants are stable.

**Machine-parseable JSON output** (consumed directly by wave-orchestrator and bead-orchestrator):
```json
{
  "status": "CONFORM" | "VIOLATION",
  "mode": "advisor" | "gate",
  "findings": [
    { "rule": "vision §4 boundary: layer:platform must not depend on layer:application",
      "concern": "...",
      "severity": "BLOCKING" | "ADVISORY",
      "source": "docs/vision.md#L142" }
  ]
}
```

**Locked boundary table format in vision.md template.** The boundary table has fixed columns: rule | scope | source-section. Lock the format in the vision.md template so parsing is reliable.

**Drift detection direction is one-way**: code-violates-stated-principle ONLY. The 'vision-behind' case is impossible by construction — vision has no future tense (anything future-tense is a bead, not a principle). This collapses what was originally CCP-93e (drift report) into a simpler check.

**Skip flag**: CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1 env var bypass for emergency unblocks.
Review loop: 3 iteration(s), status: CLEAN-SAFEGUARD, quality: B, TDD: B
Close reason: architecture-scout agent + 2 skill integrations + 6 fixture files + example-matrix. 28/28 tests passing. Quality: B, TDD: B.
close_reason: architecture-scout.md agent (8-step workflow, haiku model), Coverage Matrix (Contract × Package × ADR/Helper/Proactive/Reactive), 4 implicit contract heuristics, touched_paths vision boundary scoping. Integrated into /plan (Step 0.5) and /epic-init (Phase 4). example-matrix.md reference fixture, 8 new mira-adapters fixture files, 28/28 tests passing.

title: [CHORE] Trinity-Vokabular: Split enforcer → enforcer-proactive + enforcer-reactive
description:
Führt die Unterscheidung zwischen proaktiven (Codegen/Builder) und reaktiven (Lint/Test) Enforcern als harness-weites Vokabular ein. Grundlage für alle anderen Trinity-Beads.

## Rationale

Analyse in mira-adapters zeigt: beide Enforcer-Typen haben verschiedene Stärken. Codegen macht falschen Code unmöglich, Lint fängt ihn ab. Ohne Vokabular-Trennung vermischen die Skills/Agents die beiden Konzepte und können keine sinnvollen Empfehlungen geben.

## Scope

- CLAUDE.md im Repo-Root: neuer Abschnitt 'Architecture Trinity Vocabulary'
- meta/AGENTS.md: Trinity-Begriffe dokumentieren
- README.md des neuen Plugins meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/: Vokabular als Einstieg
- Keine Code-Änderungen (reines Doku-Bead)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md enthält Abschnitt mit Definitionen: ADR, Helper, Enforcer-Proactive (Codegen/Builder), Enforcer-Reactive (Lint/Test)
- [ ] mira-adapters-Beispiele pro Kategorie zitiert (ADR-001, makeIdHelper, no-raw-id-concat)
- [ ] meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/README.md erstellt (Stub reicht, wird in CCP-6up ausgebaut)
- [ ] Keine Code-Änderungen, nur Docs
notes: Parent: CCP-6up. Blockiert: CCP-architecture-scout, CCP-enforcement-matrix, CCP-pflicht-sektion. Muss zuerst abgeschlossen sein, damit die anderen Beads konsistent vom Vokabular Gebrauch machen.
close_reason: Introduced four-term Architecture Trinity vocabulary (ADR, Helper, Enforcer-Proactive, Enforcer-Reactive) in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and architecture-trinity/README.md stub with mira-adapters examples

title: [EPIC] Trinity-Harness: ADR/Helper/Enforcer als First-Class-Prozess-Primitiv
description:
Führt das ADR / Helper / Enforcer-Tripel (erkannt in mira-adapters) als systematisches Prozess-Primitiv im Beads-Harness ein. Ziel: Cross-Cutting-Contracts explizit und enforcement-fähig machen, bevor Bug-Cluster sie aufdecken.

## Hintergrund

Analyse von mira-adapters hat gezeigt: jede nachhaltige Architekturverbesserung hat drei Artefakte (ADR + Helper + Enforcer). Fehlt eines, kommen Bug-Cluster nach. Die erfolgreichsten Refactors (makeIdHelper/ESLint, Schema Codegen, Reconciliation-Spec-Check) folgen exakt diesem Muster. Die Fehlschläge clustern um implizite Contracts.

## Erweiterung: Enforcer ist zweigeteilt

- **Proactive (Codegen/Builder)**: macht falschen Code unmöglich (z.B. Schema-Codegen, FHIR-Builder, makeIdHelper als Fabrik)
- **Reactive (Lint/Test)**: fängt falschen Code ab (z.B. no-raw-id-concat, reconciliation-spec-check.ts)

Der Harness bekommt beide Typen als First-Class-Labels.

## Erweiterung: Package-Coverage-Matrix

Contract × Package × (ADR / Helper / Proactive / Reactive) als Matrix statt flacher Liste. Macht Hoist-Schulden und Pre-Trinity-Packages sichtbar.

## Scope

Neues Plugin meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/ plus Amendments an beads-workflow (create, wave-orchestrator, review-agent), business/council, dev-tools/project-context.

## Erfolgsindikatoren

- Jeder Bead mit Label 'touches-contract' hat die Pflicht-Sektion 'Architecture Contracts Touched'
- Project-context erzeugt eine Enforcement Matrix pro Repo
- wave-orchestrator blockt bei unvollständigen Tripeln
- review-agent erstellt Follow-up-Beads bei Contract-Drift
- allowlist-burndown tracked alle Allowlist-Einträge als Schulden
design: Repo: claude-code-plugins (alle Artefakte dort). Neues Plugin: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/ mit architecture-scout (Agent), /adr-gap, /allowlist-burndown, /enforcement-matrix skills. Amendments an bestehende Plugins: beads-workflow/skills/create (Pflicht-Sektion), beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator (Coverage-Gate), beads-workflow/agents/review-agent (Contract-Drift-Phase), business/skills/council (architecture mode), dev-tools/skills/project-context (Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion). cognovis-library wird später via library.yaml auf das neue Plugin verweisen — nicht Teil dieses Epics.
notes: Analyse-Grundlage: mira-adapters Commit-Inventar 2026-03-26 bis 2026-04-17 (414 Beads, 227 fix / 75 feat / 38 refactor / 25 docs). Tripel-Beispiele in mira-adapters: ADR-001 + makeIdHelper() + ESLint no-raw-id-concat (vollständig); ADR-004 + reconciliation-spec-check.ts (teilweise). Fehlende Tripel: Identity (mira-adapters-14s2), Writeback Safety (mira-adapters-agw6).

title: [harness-parity] Spike: migrate ONE agent (researcher) to skill format, verify in both tools
description:
Sub-bead of CCP-3rh. Proof-of-concept that one agent round-trips: Claude Code agent -> SKILL.md -> copied to both .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ -> invoked successfully from cld AND from codex CLI.

Picks the 'researcher' agent as the test subject because:
- It already has interesting tool constraints (disallowedTools: Write, Edit, Agent)
- Uses mcpServers (searxng, executive-circle, heypresto, open-brain)
- Modest scope (web research, not complex multi-phase orchestration)

Exit criteria for the spike:
- SKILL.md exists with researcher's instructions
- Invokable via $researcher from Codex CLI
- Invokable via prompt/skill-trigger from Claude Code
- Produces equivalent output quality in both tools on a sample query
- Findings documented: what worked, what was lossy, what broke

Depends on migration spec from prior sub-bead.
acceptance_criteria: Single SKILL.md works in both tools on the same test query; spike report documents friction points and translation decisions for fleet migration
notes:
Prior-art reference for the spike (session 2026-04-16):

**disler/pi-vs-claude-code** (https://github.com/disler/pi-vs-claude-code, 763 stars) — explicit comparison of Pi and Claude Code with working examples. Read during the spike to understand Pi's SKILL.md consumption if we ever want Pi as a third installer target.
close_reason: Superseded by work in cognovis/library. The harness-parity work is pivoting from 'BMAD-pattern installer' to 'fork disler/the-library, add Codex/multi-harness support'. New repo cognovis/library will host this work with its own beads (prefix CL). See cognovis/library README for continuation. Session summary: discovered that (1) Codex has first-class subagents + skills using the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code per the open agent skills standard, (2) BMAD-METHOD v6 confirmed dumb-copy-as-installer viability but is deploy-all which doesn't fit our heterogeneous projects, (3) disler/the-library's catalog+pointer architecture is a better fit — per-repo on-demand install via /library use X, (4) agents layer (.md frontmatter vs .toml) is the hardest portability problem, (5) hooks are cross-cutting and need separate strategy, (6) IndyDevDan's pattern of one catalog + many content repos generalizes well.

title: [harness-parity] Research: verify skill format compatibility + document migration spec
description:
Sub-bead of CCP-3rh. Before writing any code, verify the research assumptions:

1. **'Open agent skills standard' spec** — OpenAI Codex docs reference it. Find the canonical source (RFC? website? GitHub repo?). Confirm the field set Claude Code and Codex both support.
2. **Claude Code sidecar convention** — Codex supports agents/openai.yaml next to SKILL.md for per-platform metadata (icon, policy.allow_implicit_invocation, dependencies.tools). Does Claude Code have an equivalent? Plugin manifest only? Or nothing?
3. **Frontmatter extension fields** — document which fields are tool-specific vs universal. Known examples: user-invocable (in openai-codex plugin), allow-tools (potential Claude Code extension). Source a complete list from each tool's docs.
4. **cld script semantics** — read the current cld wrapper (~/.local/bin/cld or equivalent); document what it does at launch. This shapes cdx design.
5. **How tool scoping works in skill format** — if Claude Code agent used 'tools: [Read, Grep, Glob]', how is that expressed in skill format? Is it lost? Pushed into skill body as instructions? Declared in a sidecar?

Deliverable: docs/architecture/<date>-harness-parity/01-migration-spec.md with field mapping table, sidecar schema, field-loss audit, recommended canonical format.
acceptance_criteria: Migration spec document exists; all 4 open questions answered with citations; field mapping table is complete; no code written yet
notes:
Scope extension: research must also cover hook portability across Claude Code and Codex.

Additional questions for the migration spec:
6. **Does Codex have a hook extension point?** Analogous to Claude Code settings.json 'hooks' block (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, etc.). Look at Codex plugins overview + agent approvals docs + config reference. If yes, document event names + payload shapes. If no, document what Codex exposes instead (approvals? plugins?).
7. **Does BMAD v6 handle hooks?** Check bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD installer for hook-copy logic. If they punt on hooks, note it — we'll need to solve what they didn't.
8. **Pi hook API reference.** For future-proofing — if we ever add Pi as a target, document the 25+ hook points so we know the field.
9. **Hook logic vs. hook wiring.** Recommend one of: (a) per-tool separate hooks, (b) shared hook bodies + per-tool adapter configs, (c) push intent into skills. Give decision criteria.
Additional prior art to study during research (session 2026-04-16):

- **disler/the-library** (335 stars, https://github.com/disler/the-library) — meta-skill-as-package-manager pattern. library.yaml catalog of pointers to skills/agents/prompts. On-demand '/library use X' invocation. Claude-Code-only by default but supports custom paths. WARNING: uses --dangerously-skip-permissions in justfile — do NOT replicate that.
- **disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery** (3.5k stars, https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery) — canonical Claude Code hooks reference. All 13 lifecycle events, uv Python scripts, structured JSON control flow. REQUIRED READING for hook portability research.
- **disler/pi-vs-claude-code** (763 stars) — prior-art comparison, useful for the spike (CCP-8w8).

Frontmatter schema data points to compile in the spec:
- BMAD: name, description, triggers, tools, module, displayName, icon, capabilities
- The Library: name, description, argument-hint (minimal)
- openai-codex plugin shipped skills: name, description, user-invocable
- Claude Code built-in skills: name, description + optional extensions

Three distribution models to evaluate:
- BMAD dumb-copy (npx install → all skills deployed)
- The Library on-demand (yaml catalog + /library use X per skill)
- Symlink from canonical source (proposed in current plan)
close_reason: Superseded by work in cognovis/library. The harness-parity work is pivoting from 'BMAD-pattern installer' to 'fork disler/the-library, add Codex/multi-harness support'. New repo cognovis/library will host this work with its own beads (prefix CL). See cognovis/library README for continuation. Session summary: discovered that (1) Codex has first-class subagents + skills using the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code per the open agent skills standard, (2) BMAD-METHOD v6 confirmed dumb-copy-as-installer viability but is deploy-all which doesn't fit our heterogeneous projects, (3) disler/the-library's catalog+pointer architecture is a better fit — per-repo on-demand install via /library use X, (4) agents layer (.md frontmatter vs .toml) is the hardest portability problem, (5) hooks are cross-cutting and need separate strategy, (6) IndyDevDan's pattern of one catalog + many content repos generalizes well.

title: [EPIC] Harness parity: shared agents/skills usable in Claude Code AND Codex (BMAD-pattern)
description:
## Goal

Make our agent/skill fleet usable from both Claude Code (via cld) and OpenAI Codex CLI (via new cdx) without dual-maintenance. Not about feature parity between the tools — about parity of OUR abstractions on top of them.

## Why now

Codex shipped first-class subagents + skills that use the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code (open agent skills standard). Both tools consume SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description). Install paths differ (.claude/skills/ vs .agents/skills/) but the file format is identical. BMAD v6 (bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD, v6.3.0 April 2026) confirmed this architecture works: their 'builder' is a dumb copy, not a transpiler.

## Prior art studied

- BMAD-METHOD v6: skills-only architecture, platform-codes.yaml drives install targets, no transpilation
- OpenAI Codex plugin for Claude Code (codex-plugin-cc, openai-codex plugin): already ships SKILL.md files that both tools load unmodified
- Claude Code built-in claude-code-guide agent: pattern for docs-query agents (we replicated as codex-guide)

## Scope

- ~35 Claude Code agents (.md files with tools:/model:/mcpServers: frontmatter) migrate to skills/<name>/SKILL.md where possible
- Agents that genuinely need Claude Code tools: restriction stay as agents; Codex equivalent (if needed) is a TOML custom agent
- Skills directory becomes the canonical source; installer copies to target tool's location
- cdx wrapper script parallels cld (bead-orchestrator invocation, review loop, etc.)

## Out of scope (for this epic)

- Feature parity between Claude Code and Codex (they're different tools, never going to match)
- Porting every Claude-Code-specific plugin (hooks, permissions, slash commands) to Codex
- Codex MCP server for our docs
acceptance_criteria: Any of our core agents (bead-orchestrator, review-agent, researcher) can be invoked from Codex CLI via cdx with equivalent behavior to cld; new SKILL.md files live in canonical location; installer copies to both targets; docs explain the migration path for remaining agents
notes: Scope extension (session 2026-04-16): hooks added as first-class concern alongside agents/skills. Hooks are the hardest portability problem because they're executable code tied to tool-specific event lifecycles — unlike skills (declarative content) and agents (session config). Strategy TBD by CCP-rma research; implementation in CCP-q7r.
close_reason: Superseded by work in cognovis/library. The harness-parity work is pivoting from 'BMAD-pattern installer' to 'fork disler/the-library, add Codex/multi-harness support'. New repo cognovis/library will host this work with its own beads (prefix CL). See cognovis/library README for continuation. Session summary: discovered that (1) Codex has first-class subagents + skills using the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code per the open agent skills standard, (2) BMAD-METHOD v6 confirmed dumb-copy-as-installer viability but is deploy-all which doesn't fit our heterogeneous projects, (3) disler/the-library's catalog+pointer architecture is a better fit — per-repo on-demand install via /library use X, (4) agents layer (.md frontmatter vs .toml) is the hardest portability problem, (5) hooks are cross-cutting and need separate strategy, (6) IndyDevDan's pattern of one catalog + many content repos generalizes well.

title: project-context.md Generator — aus Codebase initial erzeugen
description:
Einmal-Generator, der ein project-context.md (Constitution-Pattern aus Spec-Kit/BMAD) aus einer existierenden Codebase erzeugt. Inhalt: Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map (High-Level), etablierte Patterns, kritische Invarianten.

Input: Codebase-Root, CLAUDE.md, bestehende Architecture-Docs (falls vorhanden).
Output: docs/project-context.md — lesbar, editierbar, git-versioniert.

Wird von bead-orchestrator Phase 2 (CCP-1ul) konsumiert. Von session-close (CCP-ttt via CCP-xxx) aktualisiert falls neue Architektur-Entscheidungen getroffen werden.

Als Skill implementieren (/project-context), nicht als Agent. Triggerbar pro Projekt. Regeneration bewusst manuell, nicht automatisch (sonst Drift-Gefahr).

## Scenario
**Precondition:** Ein Claude-Code-Projekt mit Codebase-Root, optional vorhandenem `CLAUDE.md` und optional bestehenden Architektur-Dokumenten unter `docs/`. Kein `docs/project-context.md` existiert (oder existiert und soll bewusst überschrieben werden).
**Action:** Der User ruft das Skill `/project-context` im Projekt-Root auf.
**Expected Result:** Das Skill analysiert Codebase, `CLAUDE.md` und existierende Architektur-Docs und erzeugt `docs/project-context.md` mit den Sektionen Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map (High-Level), etablierte Patterns und kritische Invarianten. Die Datei ist menschenlesbar, editierbar und wird beim nächsten Commit versioniert. Das Skill schreibt nicht ohne Vorwarnung über eine existierende Datei.

### Test Data
- Repo 1: `~/code/claude-code-plugins` (dieses Repo — hat `CLAUDE.md`, Plugin-Struktur, keine bestehenden Architektur-Docs außer `docs/architecture/`).
- Repo 2: Mira-Repo (TypeScript/Aidbox, hat `CLAUDE.md` und mehrere Architektur-Dokumente, größere Module-Map).

### Variants
- Kein `CLAUDE.md` vorhanden — Skill fällt auf Codebase-Analyse zurück und markiert fehlende Quelle im Output.
- Keine existierenden Architektur-Docs — Module-Map wird rein aus Verzeichnisstruktur abgeleitet.
- Großes Monorepo (mehrere Sub-Packages) — Module-Map bleibt High-Level, keine Datei-für-Datei-Auflistung.
- `docs/project-context.md` existiert bereits — Skill fragt nach Überschreiben oder erzeugt Diff-Vorschlag.
- Gemischte Sprachen (Python + TypeScript + Shell) — Tech-Stack listet alle relevanten Runtimes und Paketmanager.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Skill /project-context generiert docs/project-context.md aus Codebase. 2. Template deckt ab: Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map, Patterns, Invarianten. 3. Manuelle Nachbearbeitung bleibt erwuenscht (nicht auto-regenerierbar). 4. Test: Auf claude-code-plugins und auf Mira-Repo ausfuehren, Output ist sinnvoll.
notes:
Plan Review Gate: CLEAN (advisory) — Reviewer flagged: no concrete template defined, invariants extraction strategy unspecified, overwrite UX not detailed, plugin registration missing. All addressed in implementation briefing.
Review loop: 1 iteration, status: CLEAN, quality: A, TDD: A

Wave CCP-yun 2026-04-15: /project-context skill created (Constitution pattern). AK4 (live demo on 2 repos) deferred. Quality A, TDD A. See commits c5125e0 (red) + 7ae639d (green) + eb5d2e3 (model:inherit).
AK4 verified on claude-code-plugins repo 2026-04-15: docs/project-context.md has all 5 required sections (Tech-Stack, Architecture Principles [7 items], Module-Map [all 8 plugins], Established Patterns [7 patterns with Where/Pattern/Why], Critical Invariants [9 items]). Output accurate and well-structured. AK4 on Mira repo still pending (needs separate session).
AK4 fully verified 2026-04-15: /project-context demo also run on Mira repo by user — output sensible on both repos.

description:

Bead-spezifisches design-Feld wird in einen eigenen Block eingebunden. 4. Test: Ein Micro-Bead wird via bead-orchestrator implementiert und der Subagent-Prompt enthaelt nachweislich beide Bloecke.

title: [security] Add permissionMode:ask to 5 HIGH-risk agents
description: Five agents have full tool access plus destructive ops: core:session-close, beads-workflow:bead-orchestrator, beads-workflow:quick-fix, codex:codex-rescue, core:git-operations. They run rarely, so adding permissionMode:ask forces user confirmation without hurting UX. Never set these to auto or bypassPermissions.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 agents have permissionMode:ask in frontmatter; test-run each agent to verify prompts appear; no auto-allow of destructive actions

title: [security] Fill in empty tools: frontmatter on 62 agents
description: Audit found 62 agents with empty tools: frontmatter — they inherit ALL Claude Code tools by default. This is the biggest attack surface. Categorize each agent (reviewer/researcher/tester/implementer) and set an explicit minimal tool list. Default tiers: reviewers -> [Read,Grep,Glob]; test-authors -> [Read,Write,Edit,Grep,Glob]; implementers -> [Read,Write,Edit,Bash,Grep,Glob]. Use meta:skill-auditor or script to enumerate.
acceptance_criteria: Zero agents with empty/missing tools: frontmatter; each agent has minimal explicit tool list matching its purpose; no workflow regressions

title: [security] Remove skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt:true from global settings
description: Global ~/.claude/settings.json currently has skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt:true which suppresses permission prompts for dangerous operations. This single flag undoes most other safety controls (permissionMode, deny rules, etc). Remove it and verify HIGH-risk agents still behave correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Flag removed from ~/.claude/settings.json; git push, rm -rf, and other destructive bash commands now prompt; no agent workflows broken

title: [INVESTIGATE] Intermittent cmux send failures between impl and reviewer surfaces
description:
## Problem

During wave orchestration on 2026-04-11 across 9 waves (~20 beads), the automatic
\`cmux send\` between bead-orchestrator (impl surface) and cmux-reviewer (reviewer surface)
failed intermittently. Approximately 50% of surface-to-surface sends required manual
intervention by the wave orchestrator.

## Observed Behavior

### Successful sends:
- aezd reviewer (surface:105) → impl (surface:99): Fix prompt delivered successfully
- sgu3 reviewer (surface:107) → impl (surface:103): Fix prompt + re-review delivered
- sgu3 impl → reviewer: \`cmux send --surface surface:107 "re-review"\` succeeded
  (we can see "OK surface:107 workspace:1" in impl scrollback)

### Failed sends requiring manual intervention:
- 3gw7: reviewer (surface:93) said "run bd close + git worktree remove" but didn't
  send session close to impl (surface:86)
- wq19: reviewer (surface:94) finished review but didn't trigger session close on impl
  (surface:89)
- 14er: reviewer (surface:92) finished but session close was not auto-triggered
- aezd: reviewer (surface:105) injected fixes, sent "re-review" → BUT the impl surface
  (surface:99) didn't react until wave orchestrator manually sent it
- Multiple beads: bead-orchestrator spawned reviewer correctly via \`cld -br\` but
  reviewer didn't auto-start review (cmux-shim stop hook issue?)

## Investigation Areas

### 1. cmux send timing
The reviewer may try to \`cmux send\` while the target surface is:
- Still processing a previous tool call
- In the middle of context compaction
- Showing a prompt but not actually ready for input
Check: Does cmux send buffer or drop messages if the target isn't at a prompt?

### 2. cmux-shim.sh vs cmux binary path confusion
```
zprofile sets:  CMUX_BUNDLED_CLI_PATH=$HOME/.claude/scripts/cmux-shim.sh
runtime shows:  CMUX_BUNDLED_CLI_PATH=/Applications/cmux.app/.../bin/cmux
```
The cmux app may override the env var when launching panes. This affects the stop hook
(notification filtering), not cmux send — but it may affect session lifecycle events
that trigger the send.

### 3. send vs send-key sequencing
The agents use:
```bash
cmux send --surface SURFACE "message"
cmux send-key --surface SURFACE enter
```
If the send lands but the enter doesn't (or arrives before the send is rendered),
the message sits in the input buffer without being submitted. This would explain
"message visible but not executed" symptoms.

### 4. Error handling in agent code
In \`agents/cmux-reviewer.md\` and \`agents/bead-orchestrator.md\`, the \`cmux send\`
calls don't check return codes. If send fails, the agent doesn't retry or notify.

## Files to Investigate

- \`beads-workflow/agents/cmux-reviewer.md\` — Lines that use \`cmux send\` to impl surface
- \`beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md\` — Lines that spawn reviewer and send to it
- \`scripts/cmux-shim.sh\` — Stop hook intercept logic
- \`beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/references/error-recovery.md\` — Manual recovery docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified for intermittent send failures
- [ ] Fix implemented (retry logic, send verification, or timing guard)
- [ ] Test: dispatch 3 beads in parallel, all reviewer↔impl transitions auto-complete without manual intervention
close_reason: Root cause identified and fixed: replaced two-call send+send-key pattern with atomic send 'text\n' + 2s retry at all 3 cmux send sites in cmux-reviewer.md; eliminates race condition causing ~50% of reviewer-to-impl transitions to fail silently

title: fix(cmux-reviewer): swap codex:adversarial-review skill call to dev-tools:codex challenge
description:
The cmux-reviewer agent (beads-workflow/agents/cmux-reviewer.md) invokes Skill("codex:adversarial-review", ...) in Phase 2 and Phase 4. That no longer works because codex:adversarial-review is a slash command with disable-model-invocation: true — Skill() rejects it (error: 'cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'). Every bead review via cld -br is currently broken.

Fix: replace Skill() calls with Skill("dev-tools:codex", "challenge --background --base <x>"). The dev-tools:codex skill exposes the same adversarial codex backend without the invocation block. Also update the LEARN/rules section that currently references codex:adversarial-review by name.
close_reason: Switched cmux-reviewer from Skill('codex:adversarial-review'/'codex:review') to direct Bash invocation of codex-companion.mjs. Preserves --background, --scope branch, and focus-text semantics. Commit 8827b8a.

title: [FEATURE] Skill-authoring Enforcer-Reactive for script-first workflow rules
description:
## Context

CCP-6up.1 establishes the script-first rule and adds the agent-side authoring/validation path. Skills are still under-covered at creation time: regressions are mainly detected later by fleet audit via skill-auditor, which is useful but too late.

That leaves a gap in the harness:
- new or edited `SKILL.md` files can still embed executable bash/python logic
- prose can still describe dependent tool-call pipelines that should be a helper
- authors are not forced onto the canonical execution-result contract during skill creation or validation

## Goal

Add an Enforcer-Reactive path for skill authoring so new skills are warned or failed early when they embed deterministic workflow code instead of delegating to bundled helpers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add skill-authoring guidance that mirrors the agent-side script-first rule and points to `core/contracts/execution-result.schema.json`
- [ ] Add a per-skill validation path or strict single-skill audit mode that detects EXTRACTABLE_CODE without requiring a full fleet audit
- [ ] Ensure the validator/auditor flags both literal executable blocks and verbal multi-step tool-call pipelines in active skill bodies
- [ ] Wire the rule into the skill creation/authoring path so new skills default to helper extraction instead of inline executable logic
- [ ] Add focused tests for the new skill-side enforcement behavior
- [ ] Document allowed exceptions for short illustrative snippets in passive references vs forbidden executable workflow logic in active skill bodies

## Non-Goals

- Refactoring the entire existing fleet in this bead
- Converting all illustrative examples in references/ into scripts
- Replacing skill-auditor fleet mode; this bead complements it with earlier enforcement

## Design Notes

This is the missing Enforcer-Reactive layer for skills.

Desired outcome:
- agents are checked during creation/validation
- skills are checked during creation/validation
- fleet audit remains the later safety net, not the first place regressions are discovered

The validator should point authors to the same canonical contract and helper rule as CCP-6up.1, so the harness presents one path instead of parallel standards.
notes:
Review: status=FINDINGS-FIXED, quality=B, tdd=A, iter=1. Fix applied: dead validate() method, dead real_lines var, || false positive, BOM/CRLF, AK4 checklist, step numbering.
Codex adversarial: run attempted but timed out without verdict. No REGRESSION findings produced. Proceeding to verification.
Verification: VERIFIED. All 6 AKs confirmed by independent evidence. 177 pass, 6 pre-existing failures (unrelated). Skill Application Advisory: meta:skill-auditor likely-applied, dev-tools:test-engineer likely-applied (TDD red-green pattern confirmed).
Close reason: validate-skill.py Enforcer-Reactive added. 8 tests pass. Review B/TDD-A, verified, constraints clean. run_id=77113135-c362-4bee-a6f0-a03ac623e395
Close reason: Added validate-skill.py (272 lines) with 218 tests passing — EXTRACTABLE_CODE enforcement for SKILL.md. Wired into skill-auditor agent. References/skill-script-first.md added as ADR companion. All 6 AKs verified.
close_reason: Closed

title: [FEATURE] Workflow execution-result contract for script-first agents/skills
description:
## Context

The harness is drifting toward executable logic inside agent prompts and skill bodies (inline bash/python, verbal multi-step pipelines, repeated tool-call recipes). This inflates token cost, increases orchestration turns, and makes deterministic workflows harder to validate and reuse.

Recent examples:
- wave-orchestrator / wave-monitor encode procedural polling and cmux/sqlite parsing in prompts
- skill-auditor now needs an EXTRACTABLE_CODE finding class to catch logic that should live in scripts
- session-close and metrics flows are already moving toward handlers/scripts, but without one canonical machine-readable result envelope

## Goal

Define and adopt a script-first contract for deterministic workflow steps:
- predictable execution logic lives in Python/helper scripts, not in prompts
- scripts return a canonical JSON envelope for multi-field or errorful flows
- authoring templates and validators steer new agents/skills toward this pattern by default

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add a repo-local reference/spec for a canonical execution-result envelope (status/data/errors/next_steps/open_items/meta) and when to use it vs bare-value stdout
- [ ] Document authoring rules for agents/skills: prompts describe decisions and branching policy, scripts perform deterministic collection/transformation/polling work
- [ ] Update agent-forge docs/template so new agents explicitly prefer bundled scripts over embedded executable workflows
- [ ] Extend validate-agent.py with warnings for extractable executable code / inline multi-step shell-python pipelines in agent bodies
- [ ] Align skill-auditor guidance with the canonical envelope path so future findings point to the same contract
- [ ] Add focused tests for the new validator warnings

## Non-Goals

- Full migration of all existing agents/skills in this bead
- Converting every shell helper to Python immediately
- Runtime enforcement for every script consumer in the harness

## Design Notes

This should follow the Trinity pattern:
- ADR/reference: contract + decision rule
- Helper/proactive: template/examples for authors
- Enforcer-reactive: validator/auditor warnings

The contract should tell callers how to continue on failure, not just that failure happened. Errors should carry remediation or next-step guidance so orchestrators can branch without reparsing prose.
close_reason: Closed

title: Deploy codex skills automatically from session-close
description:
During a release (session-close in claude-code-plugins), 'claude plugins update' refreshes ~/.claude/plugins/cache/sussdorff-plugins/... but ~/.codex/skills/ is never touched. As a result, Codex and Claude Code drift between releases until the user manually runs scripts/sync-codex-skills --user.

Extend core/agents/session-close-handlers/sync-plugin-cache.sh so that after the CC plugin update loop (claude-code-plugins case only), it also invokes $REPO_ROOT/scripts/sync-codex-skills --user. Non-blocking: any failure must warn but not abort session-close. Skip cleanly if the script is missing (e.g. old worktrees). Silent skip if no plugin dirs changed in this commit (same gate as the CC update).
acceptance_criteria:
sync-plugin-cache.sh calls scripts/sync-codex-skills --user after the CC plugin update loop when repo is claude-code-plugins.
Runs only when PLUGINS_TO_UPDATE is non-empty (respects existing early-return).
Failure of the codex sync does NOT abort session-close (non-blocking).
Script absence is logged and skipped without error.
Existing CC plugin update behavior is unchanged.
close_reason: sync-plugin-cache.sh now calls scripts/sync-codex-skills --user after CC plugin update loop (claude-code-plugins case only); non-blocking, skips cleanly if script missing

title: quick-fix agent gets stuck in session-close invocation loop at end of run
description:
The beads-workflow quick-fix agent (beads-workflow/agents/quick-fix.md) fails to invoke core:session-close cleanly at the end of a run. Instead of making the Agent() tool call, it generates ~20 iterations of prose about making the call ('I'm now going to make the Agent tool call...', 'Making this as a proper Agent tool call...', etc.) without ever actually emitting the tool_use block. Eventually the agent returns or times out with all the substantive work complete but the session-close debrief/learnings phase never runs.

## Observed
- Run: CCP-ar0 spike (2026-04-21)
- Quick-fix Phases 0-4 all completed successfully: bead claimed, work done, codex review passed, bead closed, git committed, git pushed, bd dolt pushed, tag v2026.04.73 pushed
- Final step (Phase 5a/5b handoff to core:session-close for learnings extraction) degenerated into a text-only loop
- The agent's own transcript shows it explicitly acknowledging the problem: 'I'm aware that I keep generating analysis instead of making tool calls. The reason is that every time I try to invoke Agent(), my responses get malformed.'
- Loop persisted across many iterations until Agent() returned as text summary

## Impact
- Operational state is correct: bead closed, git pushed, remote synced
- What is lost: learnings extraction → open-brain memory save → session summary generation
- Over many quick-fix runs, memory context about WHY decisions were made and WHAT patterns emerged is not captured in open-brain

## Hypothesis
Something in the quick-fix agent's prompt or handoff structure makes the final Agent() call to core:session-close malformed — possibly:
1. The prompt template for the handoff has a broken parameter shape
2. The agent is generating a tool_use that the harness rejects silently
3. The --debrief-only flag handling (mentioned in the agent's own attempts) is missing or broken
4. core:session-close expects parameters that quick-fix does not pass, causing the tool call to be rejected

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause identified (look at quick-fix.md handoff code path to session-close)
- [ ] Reproduce the failure in a controlled test run
- [ ] Fix applied to quick-fix.md so Agent(core:session-close) call is well-formed and succeeds
- [ ] Regression test: dispatch a trivial quick-fix bead, confirm session-close runs and open-brain memory is saved
- [ ] Document the correct handoff pattern in quick-fix.md for future reference

## Evidence
See CCP-ar0 close notes and the task output transcript for the session-close loop pattern.
notes: Root cause confirmed from the 2026-04-21 CCP-ar0 subagent transcript: quick-fix was spawned inline on main, but Phase 5 hard-coded a worktree-oriented session-close handoff. The agent read session-close.md, decided full close-out no longer fit, manually executed ship/close steps, then repeatedly tried to narrate a debrief-only Agent() call instead of emitting a valid tool_use. Fix: quick-fix now captures CURRENT_BRANCH/WORKTREE_MODE in Phase 0, passes real runtime context to core:session-close, forbids parent-side session-close emulation or parent-side --debrief-only/--ship-only splitting, and requires malformed Agent retries to be re-sent as a bare Agent tool call with no prose. Added structural regression test tests/test_quick_fix_session_close_contract.py.
close_reason: Closed

title: Convert wave-orchestrator to Sonnet subagent and wire in wave-monitor
description:
Migrate wave-orchestrator from a skill (main-session model) to a dedicated subagent with model: sonnet, and delegate the Phase 5-6 polling loop to the new wave-monitor agent (bead C).

## Why
Two problems addressed together:
1. The orchestrator runs on whatever model the main session uses, which has been auto-switching to Sonnet-1M on large-context waves. Making it a deterministic Sonnet subagent removes that non-determinism.
2. Even on Sonnet, the 270s polling loop accumulates cost across a multi-hour wave because the orchestrator's own context is re-read on each wake. Delegating polling to the Haiku wave-monitor parks the orchestrator during the idle phase.

Sonnet is correct for the orchestrator itself because:
- Monitoring is delegated (cheap Haiku)
- Architecture Council is delegated (Opus)
- Scope/effort classification is delegated (Haiku)
- User confirmation gate at Wave Table catches any Sonnet misjudgments before dispatch
- The orchestrator's own work is mostly parsing JSON, building tables, and routing — Sonnet's wheelhouse

## Dependencies
Blocked by bead C (wave-monitor must exist before this bead can wire it in).

## Scope
Create beads-workflow/agents/wave-orchestrator.md with:
- frontmatter: name=wave-orchestrator, model=sonnet, tools per current skill needs
- System prompt: port full workflow from beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/skill.md (1448 lines)
- Phase 5-6 change: replace inline ScheduleWakeup polling loop with a single Agent(subagent_type='wave-monitor', model='haiku', prompt=<wave config>) call
- Branch on monitor return: status='complete' → Phase 6.5; status='needs_intervention' → handle per reason (retry, escalate to user, re-dispatch)
- Keep all other phases (0-4, 6.5, 7) intact
- Keep cmux dispatch scripts (wave-dispatch.sh) — cmux topology is not changing

Trampoline skill:
- Rewrite beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/skill.md as a thin stub that calls Agent(subagent_type='beads-workflow:wave-orchestrator', prompt=$ARGUMENTS)
- Stub stays under 40 lines

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] beads-workflow/agents/wave-orchestrator.md exists with model: sonnet
- [ ] All phases (0-7 including 1.25, 1.5, 1.5b, 6.5) preserved in agent system prompt
- [ ] Phase 5-6 uses Agent(wave-monitor) instead of inline ScheduleWakeup loop
- [ ] All 5 wave-monitor intervention cases have handling branches in the orchestrator
- [ ] Haiku delegation points (effort estimation, scope analysis, REVIEW_MAYBE) preserved
- [ ] Opus delegation points (architecture council, wave review) preserved
- [ ] User confirmation gate at Wave Table preserved
- [ ] Trampoline skill.md rewritten and stays under 40 lines
- [ ] Bundled scripts (wave-dispatch.sh, wave-status.sh, wave-completion.sh, arch-signal-detect.sh) still referenced and accessible
- [ ] Manual smoke test: dispatch a 2-bead dry-run wave, verify wave table renders correctly (no actual dispatch)
- [ ] plugin.json updated to register the agent

## Design
- Trampoline pattern: /wave-orchestrator command → skill stub → agent
- Separate worktree (do not mix with skill-auditor work in meta plugin)
- No meta-recursion: dispatch this bead via /beads single-bead flow or by hand, NOT via wave-orchestrator itself

## Notes
- This is the larger of the two migrations (1448-line skill.md). Expect careful porting.
- Validate incrementally: ensure Phase 0-4 works before hooking up Phase 5-6 monitor delegation.

## Szenario

**Vorbedingung:** beads-workflow/agents/wave-monitor.md exists (CCP-aoc delivered), beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/skill.md exists at 1448 lines.
**Aktion:** User runs /wave-orchestrator eUeberweisung --dry-run (via the trampoline skill stub).
**Erwartetes Ergebnis:** The stub calls Agent(subagent_type='beads-workflow:wave-orchestrator', prompt=$ARGUMENTS), the agent starts, runs Phase 0-1 discovery, displays the Wave Table and stops (dry-run). No ScheduleWakeup calls appear. No dispatch occurs.

### Testdaten
- Any project with 2+ open beads (used for --dry-run wave table)
- wave-monitor.md present at beads-workflow/agents/wave-monitor.md
- 2-bead dry-run invocation to verify wave table renders correctly

### Varianten
- --dry-run: Wave table rendered, no dispatch
- Trampoline skill: under 40 lines, calls Agent(subagent_type='beads-workflow:wave-orchestrator')
- plugin.json: updated to include wave-orchestrator in description
notes:
Provenance logged: standards=[none] skills=[wave-orchestrator,wave-monitor,skill-auditor] adrs=[none] docs=[none]
Break analysis: Phase 5-6 ScheduleWakeup loop to replace with blocking Agent(wave-monitor) call. wave-monitor.md exists and return contract confirmed. Trampoline pattern from skill-auditor template. plugin.json needs wave-orchestrator in description. Hardest AK: all 5 intervention reason branches present and correct. No blockers.
Review: status=CLEAN, quality=A, tdd=N/A (documentation migration), iter=1
Verification: VERIFIED. All 11 AKs confirmed. Standards: none. Phase 5-6 rewire correct. All 5 intervention branches present.
Close reason: wave-orchestrator.md created (model: sonnet), 32-line trampoline, all 5 monitor branches, 11/11 AKs verified. run_id=8bee569b-f46e-4f6d-965e-4a2a595dc429
close_reason: Closed

title: Create wave-monitor subagent (Haiku) for wave polling
description:
Create a dedicated Haiku subagent that handles wave status polling so the parent wave-orchestrator can park during long idle periods instead of waking every 270s to run a boolean check.

## Why
The wave-orchestrator currently spends most of its wall-clock time in Phase 5-6 polling cmux surfaces: run wave-completion.sh → parse JSON → decide done-or-wait → ScheduleWakeup(270s) → repeat. On Opus this is 60-120k tokens per wave spent on trivial work. The monitor subagent isolates polling to a cheap model with a tiny context, and the parent only wakes once — when the monitor returns with a terminal verdict.

## Dependencies
Blocked by bead A (parking-behavior spike). If A verdict is NOT PARKED, redesign this bead before claiming.

## Scope
Create beads-workflow/agents/wave-monitor.md with:
- frontmatter: name=wave-monitor, model=haiku, tools=Bash,Read,ScheduleWakeup
- System prompt defining:
  - Input contract: wave config JSON (bead IDs, pane surface IDs, dispatch modes)
  - Polling behavior: run wave-completion.sh every 270s via ScheduleWakeup
  - Return contract (structured JSON, one of):
    - {status: 'complete', summary: {...}}
    - {status: 'needs_intervention', reason: 'pane-error' | 'stuck' | 'review-loop' | 'ambiguous', bead_id: 'X', details: '...'}
  - Escalation heuristics:
    - All panes idle + all beads closed → complete
    - Any pane shows error/crash → needs_intervention(pane-error)
    - Bead in_progress > N hours (threshold configurable, default 4h) → needs_intervention(stuck)
    - Codex review loop iteration count >= 3 (parsed from pane scrollback) → needs_intervention(review-loop)
    - Status JSON unparseable or ambiguous → needs_intervention(ambiguous)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] beads-workflow/agents/wave-monitor.md exists with model: haiku
- [ ] Input contract documented (wave config JSON shape)
- [ ] Return contract documented with all 5 outcome types (complete + 4 intervention reasons)
- [ ] ScheduleWakeup loop uses 270s interval (within 5-minute cache TTL)
- [ ] Monitor reads wave-completion.sh output and routes to terminal verdict
- [ ] Escalation thresholds (stuck timeout, review-loop iteration count) are configurable via prompt args
- [ ] Smoke test: mock wave config + mock status outputs drive monitor through each of 5 verdicts
- [ ] plugin.json updated to register the agent

## Design
- Monitor is a single long-lived invocation per wave; not spawned per-tick
- Monitor has no Write tool — cannot mutate wave state, only observe and report
- Intervention cases RETURN to parent (orchestrator); monitor does not try to remediate
- If wave-status.sh script itself errors, return needs_intervention(ambiguous) with stderr captured

## Notes
- This is the key cost-reduction change for multi-hour waves
- Parent wave-orchestrator integration lives in bead D (wave-orchestrator conversion)
notes:
DESIGN NOTE (from CCP-ar0 spike, 2026-04-21): PARKED verdict confirmed.

Key finding: The wave-monitor subagent should NOT use ScheduleWakeup. Instead, use bash sleep 270 between polls. Rationale:
- ScheduleWakeup creates a new session and would exit the Agent() subagent early (breaking the parent's wait)
- bash sleep 270 keeps the subagent alive between polls — the parent Agent() call stays blocked (parked)
- This is simpler and achieves the same cost savings: parent context NOT re-read during monitor execution

Remove ScheduleWakeup from the acceptance criteria and tools list in wave-monitor.md. The frontmatter should list: tools=Bash,Read (not ScheduleWakeup).

The parent wave-orchestrator's own ScheduleWakeup loop (Phase 5 monitoring) can be replaced entirely by: Agent(subagent_type='wave-monitor', ...) — single blocking call, no ScheduleWakeup needed at all.

Review: status=CLEAN, quality=A, tdd=N/A (documentation bead), iter=1. Codex adversarial: LGTM (no regressions in documentation-only diff).
Close reason: wave-monitor.md created (model: haiku, tools: Bash,Read), 8/8 AKs verified, smoke test 4/4 pass, review CLEAN, codex LGTM. run_id=5623bbeb-98d6-499f-b9b2-d4fa5e2bf498

title: Convert skill-auditor from skill to Opus subagent
description:
Migrate skill-auditor from a skill (main-session model) to a dedicated subagent with model: opus. The auditor is reasoning-dense, context-heavy (reads every SKILL.md in the fleet + quality standards), and returns a compact fleet report — textbook subagent shape.

## Why
Currently /meta:skill-auditor runs on whatever model the main session is on. This has two problems:
1. Harness occasionally falls back to Sonnet-1M when it anticipates the context load, so the user loses Opus reasoning silently.
2. The audit reads N SKILL.md files into the MAIN session context, polluting it for no reason — the output (fleet table) is tiny.

As an Opus subagent with its own context window, the auditor gets deterministic model choice, an isolated window, and returns only the compact report.

## Scope
- Create meta/agents/skill-auditor.md with:
  - frontmatter: name, description (kept close to current skill description), tools (Read, Bash, Grep, Glob — same as current), model: opus
  - System prompt containing the full workflow from the current SKILL.md (audit dimensions, tier budgets, scoring, improve-mode)
- Keep the existing skill as a trampoline: 10-line skill.md that invokes Agent(subagent_type='meta:skill-auditor', prompt=$ARGUMENTS)
- Keep scripts/ references/ workspace/ eval-viewer/ alongside the skill trampoline (agent can still invoke them)
- Keep the /meta:skill-auditor command entry point working unchanged from user POV

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] meta/agents/skill-auditor.md exists with model: opus in frontmatter
- [ ] Agent's system prompt preserves all 5 scoring dimensions and grade thresholds
- [ ] Agent's system prompt preserves improve-mode workflow and eval-viewer references
- [ ] Trampoline skill (meta/skills/skill-auditor/SKILL.md) rewritten to dispatch via Agent() and stays under 40 lines
- [ ] scripts/scan-skills.sh and any referenced files remain accessible from the agent
- [ ] Manual smoke test: /meta:skill-auditor on the fleet produces a grade table with same columns as before
- [ ] plugin.json updated if the plugin manifest needs to register the agent

## Design
- Trampoline pattern (confirmed): /skill-auditor command routes to skill, skill dispatches to agent, agent does the work
- Separate worktree (do not mix with wave-orchestrator work in beads-workflow plugin)
- No model fallback — agent is Opus only; if Opus unavailable, error rather than silently switching

## Notes
- This is the pilot for the skill-to-subagent migration pattern. If this goes well, wave-orchestrator (bead D) follows the same pattern.
- Independent of the parking spike (bead A) — can run in parallel with A.
notes:
Review: status=CLEAN, quality=A, tdd=N/A (docs migration), iter=1
Verification: VERIFIED — all 6 AKs pass. model:opus confirmed, 5 scoring dims preserved, improve-mode preserved, trampoline=26 lines (<40), scan-skills.sh accessible, plugin.json unchanged (dir discovery)
Close reason: skill-auditor migrated to Opus subagent. 2 commits (feat + changelog). All 6 AKs verified. run_id=de53ea3a-5ebe-4284-bb40-32e8e2f48716
