title: [BUG] quick-fix orchestrator cannot self-trigger core:session-close inside worktree (subagent_type not registered)
description:
## Problem

Observed during wave-20260421-bundle-b (open-brain project). Two of three quick-fix beads (open-brain-058 and open-brain-d8x) completed Sonnet implementation + Codex review successfully, but got stuck at session-close handoff with this error:

> HANDOFF required because core:session-close / session-close is not registered as a subagent_type in this worktree. The only available agents are claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup.

The quick-fix orchestrator running inside the bead worktree cannot spawn Agent(subagent_type='core:session-close') — the plugin agent registry apparently isn't fully inherited into worktrees created by the bead-orchestrator.

Third bead (open-brain-2co) completed its own session-close successfully. Need to investigate why — possibly timing-related or worktree-creation-order-dependent.

## Impact
- Wave orchestrator violates its own 'NEVER session close' rule to unblock stuck beads
- User must manually intervene per affected bead (~2-3 min overhead each)
- Defeats the purpose of quick-fix's autonomous single-pane model

## Workaround (current)
Wave orchestrator spawns core:session-close from the main orchestrator context with explicit worktree path. Works but violates skill rule.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause: why core:session-close is not registered in worktree agent harness
- [ ] Fix: ensure plugin agents (core:session-close specifically) are available in bead worktrees
- [ ] Test: run a 3-bead quick-fix wave and verify all three can self-close without orchestrator intervention
- [ ] Document: if there is a legitimate case where session-close must be triggered from outside the worktree, update the 'NEVER session close' rule to describe the escape hatch

## Discovery context
- Wave: wave-20260421-bundle-b
- Affected beads: open-brain-058, open-brain-d8x (both closed via workaround)
- Non-affected: open-brain-2co (self-closed normally)
- Plugin version: beads-workflow/2026.04.65
notes:
[FIX 2026-04-21 Malte+Claude]

## Root causes (two, compounding)

**1. Launcher prompt told agent to stop at Phase 5**
  cld -b's prompt said: "Execute the full orchestration workflow (Phase 0–5)."
  The full orchestrator has phases 0-16, not 0-5. The agent was literally obeying
  the prompt — CCP-8ix's "Next steps (Phases 6-16, deferred per user scope)" was
  not a hallucination, it was accurate reporting of what cld told it to do.
  Same bug in cld -bq (said "Phase 0-3", quick-fix has 0-5 after hardening).

**2. --setting-sources defensive fix for CCP-9xy symptom**
  Verified: a fresh `claude --worktree <name>` session DOES have full plugin registry
  (all 39 agents including core:session-close) with default settings. The reported
  CCP-9xy failure (only 5 built-in agents) was reproducible only by passing
  --setting-sources=local or =project (which excludes user-level plugins).
  Hypothesis: during wave-20260421-bundle-b, one of parallel claude-code launches
  raced with plugin sync OR inherited a restricted setting-sources from
  cmux/env. Defensive fix: cld now explicitly passes
  --setting-sources=user,project,local, guaranteeing user-level plugin agents load
  regardless of project-level overrides or launch race conditions.

## Files changed

- ~/.claude/scripts/cld (user-level launcher)
  * Both -b and -bq branches now pass --setting-sources=user,project,local
  * Both prompts rewritten to "Execute ... end-to-end: Phase 0 through Phase N
    (session-close). Do not stop after any intermediate phase."
- core/skills/beads/SKILL.md
  * Dispatch table Agent(prompt=) strings updated to "end-to-end" phrasing
  * Frontmatter description updated (full = 0-16, quick = 0-5)
- beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md (already landed earlier)
  * Autonomy contract forbidding "fake user scope" early exits
  * Phase 5→6 MANDATORY imperative
  * Phase 16 hardened MANDATORY/UNSKIPPABLE block with fallback
- beads-workflow/agents/quick-fix.md (already landed earlier)
  * Renumbered to Phase 0-5
  * Phase 0a pre-flight probe for core:session-close availability
  * Phase 5 hardened MANDATORY/UNSKIPPABLE with retry+hard-stop fallback

## Verification

Tested `claude --worktree bead-probe-registry-ccp9xy -p "list subagent types"`:
  → 39 agents including core:session-close listed (probe cleaned up after).
cld script syntax: zsh -n passes.

## Closing strategy

Keep CCP-9xy open pending a wave rerun that actually exercises the full path:
- cld -bq to one small bead
- Verify Phase 5 auto-triggers core:session-close without manual intervention
- Merges to main, pushes, closes bead

Recommend running that verification before closing this bead.
Landed: claude-code-plugins@fe9f047 + ~/.claude@88d96d7. Pushed 2026-04-21.
close_reason: Landed: setting-sources defensive fix + cld prompt phase numbering + agent autonomy hardening. Commits: claude-code-plugins fe9f047, ~/.claude 88d96d7.

title: [INTEGRATION] agent-standards.yml coverage gaps (beads-workflow, infra, meta)
description:
Found during Phase 6.5 integration-verification after Wave 1 (CCP-mit + CCP-dzk + CCP-2n7 closed 2026-04-21).

## Findings

### 1. beads-workflow/* missing from orchestrator_handled
bead-orchestrator.md injects standards for beads-workflow/* subagent prompts (review-agent, verification-agent, changelog-updater, feature-doc-updater, quick-fix) via Phase 4 preamble + 'Load these standards before implementing' block. But beads-workflow/* is NOT in orchestrator_handled in ~/.claude/agent-standards.yml (only dev-tools/* and medical/* are). Result: double injection when orchestrator spawns these agents — orchestrator's preamble + hook's injection both fire. Not broken (idempotent paths), but wastes tokens and contradicts CCP-dzk design intent.

Fix: Add 'beads-workflow/*' to orchestrator_handled in ~/.claude/agent-standards.yml. Verify 'core/*' too if orchestrator ever spawns core/git-operations or core/branch-synchronizer.

### 2. infra/* and meta/* missing from agent-standards.yml
Real agents at infra/agents/home.md and meta/agents/{convention-reviewer,learning-extractor}.md. TaskCreated events with these agent_labels fall through 'no match → no injection' silently. english-only and no-emoji likely apply but are never injected.

Fix: Decide intent — (a) add 'infra/*' and 'meta/*' patterns with appropriate paths, or (b) add explicit empty-list entries to signal 'intentionally none'.

### 3. CCP-mit documentation drift
CCP-mit description said hook added to ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-create, actual implementation lives in core/hooks/worktree-create.sh inside the sussdorff-plugins/core plugin. Not broken — plugin path is correct — but bead description is misleading.

Fix: Update CCP-mit notes to reflect correct hook location. Verify the hook fires on next cld -b worktree creation.

## Scope
- Edit ~/.claude/agent-standards.yml (add missing patterns + orchestrator_handled entries)
- Add structural check in test_inject_subagent_standards.py that enforces 'every agents/ directory is represented in agent-standards.yml' (prevents future drift)
- Append note to CCP-mit clarifying hook location
acceptance_criteria: 1) agent-standards.yml has patterns for beads-workflow/*, infra/*, meta/* (either populated or explicit empty-list). 2) orchestrator_handled includes beads-workflow/* and core/* (as applicable). 3) Structural test asserts agent-standards.yml covers every agents/ directory. 4) CCP-mit notes updated with correct hook location.
close_reason: infra/*, meta/*, business/*, content/* mappings added to agent-standards.yml; orchestrator_handled correctly excludes beads-workflow/* and core/* (verification-agent/session-close are ad-hoc spawns); TestAgentStandardsCoverage structural test added — prevents future drift; CCP-mit notes updated with correct hook location; 2 review iterations, 3 regressions fixed in iter 1, clean in iter 2

metadata.intent: Close agent-standards.yml coverage gaps found in Phase 6.5 integration-verification: add beads-workflow/infra/meta patterns and orchestrator_handled entries so every agents/ directory is represented; add structural test to prevent future drift.

title: Add /metrics/summary REST endpoint to beads metrics server
description:
## Warum

Der beads metrics server exponiert aktuell Raw-Rows (per-call detail), aber kein aggregiertes Summary-Endpoint. Jede Konsumenten-Oberflaeche (TUI, externe Dashboards) muss selbst aggregieren. Ein /metrics/summary Endpoint liefert bereits aggregierte Kennzahlen und reduziert Duplikation der Aggregations-Logik.

Komplementaer zu CCP-pxh (bead-metrics TUI wave summary): TUI wuerde idealerweise das REST-Endpoint konsumieren statt direkt gegen SQLite zu queryen.

## Scope

- Neues Endpoint: GET /metrics/summary
- Query-Parameter: wave_id (optional), bead_id (optional), from/to (ISO-8601)
- Response: per-mode breakdown + per-model breakdown (analog zu den SQL-Queries in wave-orchestrator Phase 7)
- Versioniert unter /v1 oder analog zur bestehenden API-Konvention

## Acceptance Criteria

- GET /metrics/summary liefert JSON mit 'by_mode' und 'by_model' Sektionen
- Filter wave_id / bead_id / date-range funktionieren
- Leere Parameter liefern Gesamt-Summary ueber alles (sinnvoll mit from/to Default 'last 7 days')
- OpenAPI/Docs-Eintrag oder Read-me-Update

## Out of Scope

- Schreibendes Endpoint (keine Metric-Ingestion per REST)
- Auth/Rate-Limit (falls nicht existent im Server) — separates Bead

## Historie

Urspruenglich gefilet als CCP-2vo.8.9 / CCP-2vo.8.10 im A/B-Validation-Frame (CCP-2vo.8). Frame obsolet nach CCP-2vo.10 (2-pane removal); Feature bleibt nuetzlich und ist natuerlicher Lieferant fuer CCP-pxh (TUI).

title: Metrics rollup null pointer on missing run_id
description:
## Warum

Metrics-Rollup wirft einen Null-Pointer/Attribute-Error, wenn ein agent_calls-Eintrag ohne run_id existiert. Das passiert in der Praxis, wenn ein Subagent ohne Orchestrator-Kontext aufgerufen wird (z.B. Standalone /council oder manuell). Der gesamte Rollup bricht dann ab anstatt die betroffene Zeile zu ueberspringen oder als 'unattributed' zu kategorisieren.

## Scope

- Stelle im Rollup-Code identifizieren (bead-metrics / metrics rollup / aggregation)
- Null-sicheren Zugriff: entweder run_id optional behandeln und 'unattributed' bucket, oder die betreffende Zeile sauber ausfiltern mit Log-Eintrag
- Regression-Test mit agent_calls row ohne run_id

## Acceptance Criteria

- Metrics-Rollup laeuft ohne Exception durch, auch wenn agent_calls rows ohne run_id enthaelt
- Null-run_id rows landen sichtbar in einer dedizierten Kategorie oder werden mit clear log skipped (nicht silent dropped)
- Test dokumentiert den Fall

## Out of Scope

- Aenderungen am agent_calls Schema (run_id ist weiterhin nullable/optional)
- Instrumentation neuer Aufrufer, sodass sie run_id setzen — separates Bead falls noetig

## Historie

Urspruenglich gefilet als CCP-2vo.8.1 / CCP-2vo.8.2 im A/B-Validation-Frame (CCP-2vo.8). Frame obsolet nach CCP-2vo.10 (2-pane removal); Bug bleibt relevant.
notes: Close reason (pending session-close): rollup_run() now raises ValueError on null/empty run_id (unattributed work cannot be silently dropped) and logs a warning when orphan agent_calls rows with run_id IS NULL are present. Schema unchanged. New regression test test_rollup_null_run_id in beads-workflow/scripts/tests/test_metrics_run_api.py covers both AK paths. All 4 tests green; Codex adversarial review: LGTM, no REGRESSION findings. Commits: 3033a45 (red), 748554a (green). Branch: worktree-bead-CCP-imb. Do NOT close — session-close will merge feature->main, push, then close.
close_reason: rollup_run() now raises ValueError on null/empty run_id (unattributed work not silently dropped) and logs warning on orphan agent_calls with run_id IS NULL. Schema unchanged. Regression test test_rollup_null_run_id added; 4/4 tests green. Codex adversarial review: LGTM, 1 iteration. Behaviour change: external callers relying on silent no-op for rollup_run(None) must now catch ValueError.

title: Add wave-level summary card to bead-metrics TUI
description:
## Warum

bead-metrics TUI zeigt aktuell nur per-bead Cost-Breakdown. Wave-level Aggregation (Tokens pro wave_id, aufgeteilt nach Mode + Modell + Claude/Codex) fehlt — User muessen SQL gegen ~/.claude/metrics.db schreiben, um Wave-Gesamtkosten zu sehen.

Ganz konkret: im Phase 7 Learnings Report nach Wave-Orchestration kommt eine Wave-Zusammenfassung (per-mode Mode-Tabelle + per-model Model-Tabelle). Die gleichen Daten waeren in der TUI nuetzlich als Daily-Review.

## Scope

- Neues Card im TUI: Wave Summary
- Aufgerufen via `bead-metrics wave <wave-id>` oder `--wave-id` Flag
- Zeigt: Mode-Tabelle (beads, total_tokens, avg_per_bead, codex_tokens, claude_tokens) + Model-Tabelle (model, calls, total_tokens)
- SQL-Queries aus wave-orchestrator SKILL.md (Phase 7) als Vorlage

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bead-metrics wave <wave-id>` zeigt Wave-Summary-Card
- Card enthaelt Mode-Breakdown + Model-Breakdown in derselben Form wie der Learnings Report
- Graceful wenn wave_id keine Daten hat: 'No data recorded for wave <id>' statt Crash
- Existierende Per-Bead-Views unveraendert

## Out of Scope

- Fix fuer fehlende Claude-Token-Recording (separates Bead — bead_runs war in wave-20260421-* leer fuer Claude-Rufe)
- Neue Metriken

## Historie

Urspruenglich gefilet als CCP-2vo.8.11 / CCP-2vo.8.12 im A/B-Validation-Frame (CCP-2vo.8). Frame obsolet nach CCP-2vo.10 (2-pane removal); Feature bleibt nuetzlich. Gap direkt beobachtet in wave-orchestrator Learnings Report (wave-20260421-075956 + wave-20260421-081939) — bead_runs leer fuer Claude-Rufe, per-wave Aggregation schwer sichtbar.

title: codex exec timeout not propagated to agent exit code
description:
## Warum

Wenn `codex exec` in sein Timeout laeuft, wird der non-zero exit code nicht an den aufrufenden Agent propagiert. Das Ergebnis: False-Green — ein Timeout-Event sieht wie ein erfolgreicher (nur leerer) Review aus.

Besonders kritisch nach CCP-rjq (Phase 1.25 wave-review-gate): wave-reviewer ruft jetzt `codex exec --sandbox read-only` direkt via Bash auf. Timeout ohne exit-Propagierung wuerde bedeuten, dass die Wave weiterlaeuft obwohl der strukturelle Review faktisch nicht stattgefunden hat.

## Scope

- Aufrufstellen von `codex exec` identifizieren (mindestens codex-cli runtime + wave-reviewer)
- Exit code aus Codex-Timeout durchreichen (Return non-zero statt silent success)
- Sonnet-Fallback-Pfad in wave-reviewer triggert genau auf diesen non-zero exit — sicherstellen dass Timeout gleich behandelt wird wie andere non-zero exits

## Acceptance Criteria

- `codex exec` mit erzwungenem Timeout produziert non-zero exit code im aufrufenden Agent
- wave-reviewer fallback zu Sonnet triggert bei Timeout (nicht nur bei 'codex not found')
- Regression-Test dokumentiert Timeout-Fall

## Out of Scope

- Andere codex-Modi (review, consult) — falls die das gleiche Problem haben, separates Bead

## Historie

Urspruenglich gefilet als CCP-2vo.8.3 / CCP-2vo.8.4 im A/B-Validation-Frame (CCP-2vo.8). Frame obsolet nach CCP-2vo.10 (2-pane removal); inhaltlicher Befund bleibt relevant.
notes: Phase 0-5 complete: effort=small (quick-fix sized), commits a124b30 (red test) + 0b083d6 (green fix). Changed: codex-exec.sh (timeout wrapper, CODEX_EXEC_TIMEOUT env, default 300s), wave-orchestrator/skill.md (documented timeout + explicit fallback on exit 124), test_codex_exec_sh.py (test_timeout_exits_nonzero). Test suite: 5 passed, 0 failed. All 3 ACs met. Ready for review+session-close.
close_reason: Codex exec timeout now propagates non-zero exit code via codex-exec.sh timeout wrapper (CODEX_EXEC_TIMEOUT env, default 300s). wave-orchestrator Phase 1.25 prompt updated. Regression test added. 5/5 tests passing. Shipped in v2026.04.69 (merge 6ca4501).

title: SubagentStop hook — per-subagent metrics for ad-hoc Agent() spawns
description:
## Problem

`bead-metrics` tracks per-bead cost, but not 'which agent type is most expensive per bead?' or 'is reviewer overhead justified?'. Today `~/.claude/metrics.db` agent_calls table is filled ONLY for spawns dispatched by bead-orchestrator via insert_agent_call(). Ad-hoc Agent() spawns (outside orchestrator flow) leave no trace.

Split from CCP-wl6 (wave-review 2026-04-21): standards-injection concern moved to sibling bead so each track can ship independently.

## Proposed Solution — Conditional Hook (HITL decision 2026-04-21)

A SubagentStop hook that writes ONE row to agent_calls per subagent completion, **only when the orchestrator is NOT the caller**.

Mechanism:
- bead-orchestrator exports `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=<run_id>` into child env before each Agent() spawn.
- Hook reads the env var on SubagentStop.
- If PRESENT: hook exits without writing (orchestrator already handles via insert_agent_call()).
- If ABSENT: hook writes one row with `run_id="adhoc-<uuid>"`, `phase_label="adhoc"`, `agent_label=<subagent_type>`, tokens/duration from the SubagentStop payload.

Result: no dual-writer race, no new `update_agent_call()` API required, full coverage of ad-hoc spawns.

## Schema Reuse

Use the EXISTING agent_calls table (beads-workflow/lib/orchestrator/metrics.py:681). No schema changes.

Keyed insert: `(run_id, bead_id, agent_label, iteration)` — ad-hoc rows use generated run_id so they never collide with orchestrator rows.

## Consumption

`bead-metrics` skill extended to show per-subagent breakdown — filter by `phase_label='adhoc'` to see ad-hoc usage patterns, group by `agent_label` for cost attribution.

## Trinity Labels

- SubagentStop hook = Enforcer-Reactive (records after the fact)
- agent_calls table = Helper (schema owned by orchestrator/metrics.py)

## Method of Change (MoC)

| Measure | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| % of ad-hoc Agent() spawns with cost attribution | 0 | 100 |
| Dual-writer race window | N/A | 0 (env-var gated) |
| New API surface in metrics.py | — | 0 (insert-only) |

## Scope

**In scope:**
- SubagentStop hook in `~/.claude/hooks/`
- Env-var export from bead-orchestrator (one-line add before Agent() spawn)
- bead-metrics skill update (adhoc breakdown view)

**Out of scope:**
- `update_agent_call()` API (not needed under conditional design)
- TaskCreated stub rows (dropped — no spawn-timestamp signal captured)
- Cost-based routing / auto alerting

## Scenario
**Precondition:** `~/.claude/metrics.db` exists with the `agent_calls` schema from `beads-workflow/lib/orchestrator/metrics.py` (columns: run_id, bead_id, wave_id, phase_label, agent_label, model, iteration, input/cached/output/reasoning/total tokens, duration_ms, exit_code, recorded_at). The new hook file `~/.claude/hooks/log-adhoc-subagent-metrics.py` is registered under `hooks.SubagentStop` in `settings.json`. The SQLite FK on `agent_calls.run_id -> bead_runs.run_id` is NOT enforced (PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF, the project default) so ad-hoc rows with synthetic `run_id` can insert without a parent `bead_runs` row.

**Action:** A parent session spawns a subagent via the `Agent()` tool (e.g. `general-purpose` or `Explore`). The subagent runs to completion, emitting a `SubagentStop` event with a payload that includes `subagent_type`, `model`, token counts, and `duration_ms`. The hook fires once per stop.

**Expected Result:**
- **Orchestrator-spawned subagent** (env contains `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=rn-xyz`): hook exits 0 with no write; no new row appears in `agent_calls`. The orchestrator-driven `insert_agent_call()` path remains the single writer for that run.
- **Ad-hoc subagent** (env does NOT contain `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID`): hook writes exactly ONE row to `agent_calls` with `run_id=adhoc-<uuid4>`, `phase_label=adhoc`, `agent_label=<subagent_type>`, `bead_id=` (or `NULL`/unknown sentinel — no bead context available), `iteration=1`, tokens and `duration_ms` copied from payload, `model` copied from payload, `exit_code=0`, `recorded_at=<ISO8601 UTC>`.
- **Overhead:** Hook completes in < 50 ms wall time (measured at `SubagentStop`).
- **Off-switch:** When `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_METRICS=1` is in the environment of the spawning session, the hook exits 0 without writing, regardless of whether `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID` is set.
- **Downstream view:** `/bead-metrics` with the new adhoc breakdown lists one aggregated line per `agent_label` when filtered by `phase_label=adhoc` (e.g. `general-purpose | 3 calls | 42,118 tokens | claude-opus-4-7`).

### Test Data
- **Hook payload (ad-hoc case):** `{"subagent_type": "general-purpose", "model": "claude-opus-4-7", "total_tokens": 12480, "input_tokens": 10200, "cached_input_tokens": 8500, "output_tokens": 2180, "reasoning_output_tokens": 100, "duration_ms": 18345, "exit_code": 0}`.
- **Hook payload (orchestrator case):** same shape, but the child process env also carries `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=rn-9f3a2b14-8c7d-4e16-bd11-0a2f9c6e4d88` (a real UUID4 emitted by `start_run()`).
- **Bead IDs in downstream view fixtures:** `CCP-2n7`, `CCP-dzk`, `CCP-wl6` (realistic project format `<PREFIX>-<3-char-suffix>`).
- **Agent labels exercised:** `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `core:session-close`, `content-processor`.
- **Models exercised:** `claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4`.
- **Pre-seeded orchestrator row** (for the negative test): one `bead_runs` row with `run_id=rn-9f3a2b14-8c7d-4e16-bd11-0a2f9c6e4d88`, `bead_id=CCP-2n7`, plus one matching `agent_calls` row written by `insert_agent_call(phase_label=implementation, agent_label=impl-opus)` — used to assert the orchestrator case produces no *additional* rows after `SubagentStop`.

### Variants
1. **Happy path — ad-hoc write:** no `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID`, no `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_METRICS`. Assert exactly one new row with `phase_label=adhoc` and `run_id` matching `^adhoc-[0-9a-f-]{36}$`.
2. **Suppressed — orchestrator context:** `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=rn-...` present. Assert `COUNT(*)` on `agent_calls` is unchanged post-stop.
3. **Suppressed — off-switch:** `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_METRICS=1` present (with or without orchestrator var). Assert no write.
4. **Both env vars set:** orchestrator var wins (no write). The off-switch must also produce no write; behavior is indistinguishable at the DB level but hook must not raise.
5. **Malformed / missing payload fields:** `total_tokens` missing or `null`. Hook coerces to 0 and still writes one row — never raises, never blocks `SubagentStop`.
6. **Missing `subagent_type`:** hook falls back to `agent_label=unknown` rather than aborting.
7. **DB locked / unwritable:** hook logs to stderr, exits 0 (never 2 — a metrics hook must not block the subagent lifecycle). Assert no partial row.
8. **Concurrency:** two subagents stop within the same second. Assert two distinct rows with distinct `run_id` UUIDs (no collision, no overwrite).
9. **Overhead budget:** synthetic payload, measure wall time < 50 ms across 10 trials.
10. **Downstream skill integration:** after variants 1 and 8 execute, `/bead-metrics --adhoc` (or the equivalent filter) renders a table grouped by `agent_label` with correct token sums; orchestrator rows from the seeded fixture do NOT appear in the adhoc breakdown.
11. **One-line orchestrator edit:** `bead-orchestrator.md`/wrapper exports `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=$RUN_ID` in the child env before every `Agent()` spawn. Assert by spawning a real orchestrator-driven bead run and grepping `agent_calls` for any `phase_label=adhoc` row tied to that `run_id` — must be zero.
12. **entropy-scan:** passes after the new hook, orchestrator edit, and skill update are in place.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Hook detects CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID env var. If present, exits without any metrics write. 2) If absent, writes exactly one row to agent_calls at SubagentStop with run_id='adhoc-<uuid>', phase_label='adhoc', subagent_type captured as agent_label, tokens + duration + model from payload. 3) bead-orchestrator exports CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID before every Agent() spawn (one code-path change). 4) No changes to metrics.py schema; no new update_agent_call() API. 5) bead-metrics skill shows per-subagent ad-hoc breakdown (filter phase_label='adhoc', group by agent_label). 6) Off-switch via env var (CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_METRICS=1). 7) entropy-scan passes. 8) Hook overhead <50ms.
notes:
[WAVE-REVIEW-PHASE-1.25 2026-04-21]
HIGH-lokal (rename applied): Title changed from 'TaskCreated/SubagentStop hook — ...' to 'SubagentStop hook — ...' to eliminate ambiguity with CCP-dzk's TaskCreated handler.

Scope clarification: This bead does NOT register any TaskCreated handler. TaskCreated ownership belongs exclusively to CCP-dzk. This bead only registers a SubagentStop handler.

MEDIUM (filename pinning applied):
- Hook file: ~/.claude/hooks/log-adhoc-subagent-metrics.py
- settings.json key: hooks.SubagentStop (append, do not replace)
- If CCP-dzk merges first, rebase onto its settings.json entry.

LOW (dispatch order): Recommended to land CCP-2n7 before CCP-dzk so the CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID contract is in place when CCP-dzk codifies its coupling. Not a hard dependency.
Provenance logged: standards=[~/.claude/standards/python/style.md, ~/.claude/standards/python/python314-patterns.md, ~/.claude/standards/git/conventional-commits.md] skills=[none] adrs=[none] docs=[beads-workflow/skills/bead-metrics/SKILL.md]
Break analysis: insert_agent_call() has Python FK check (line 704-711) — ad-hoc hook must use raw SQL INSERT, not insert_agent_call(). No dual-writer risk since env-var gate prevents orchestrator-spawned subagents from being written. settings.json currently only has PreToolUse — SubagentStop must be appended safely. bead-orchestrator.md change is additive (one export line). bead-metrics SKILL.md change is additive (new --adhoc arg). query_adhoc_report() to add to metrics.py is new fn, no schema change.

[WAVE-LIVE-FEEDBACK 2026-04-21 T+13min]
Live manual check found: ~/.claude/hooks/log-adhoc-subagent-metrics.py is NOT executable (mode 0644). CCP-dzk's sibling hook (inject-subagent-standards.py) was correctly chmod +x. Since settings.json uses "type: command", the hook must be executable to fire.

Impl must chmod +x the hook file during install/scaffolding and add this to the scenario's "Expected Result" checks.

[WAVE-LIVE-FEEDBACK CORRECTION 2026-04-21]
Previous note about executable bit was a false alarm: settings.json uses "python3 ~/.claude/hooks/log-adhoc-subagent-metrics.py", which calls the interpreter explicitly — +x is NOT required. The hook should fire correctly as-is. No action needed.

(Retaining original note above for audit trail.)
Review: status=FINDINGS-FIXED, quality=B, tdd=A, iter=1. Blocking: export→os.environ fix in bead-orchestrator.md, absolute path in settings.json. Advisory: variant-4 test, 10-trial overhead test, unused import, model default, GROUP_CONCAT aggregation. All 12 tests pass post-fix.

title: TaskCreated hook — conditional standards injection by subagent_type
description:
## Problem

CLAUDE.md instructs "Vor Subagent-Start /inject-standards --mode=paths aufrufen". This is forgotten regularly. No enforcement mechanism.

Split from CCP-wl6 (wave-review 2026-04-21): CCP-wl6 bundled standards injection and metrics under one trigger. Both concerns now live in their own beads and can fail/ship independently.

## Proposed Solution

A TaskCreated hook that injects standards conditionally based on `subagent_type` — not universally.

## Routing by subagent_type

Mapping table at `~/.claude/agent-standards.yml`:

```yaml
dev-tools/*:       [english-only, no-emoji, tool-standards]
medical/*:         [english-only, compliance, content-isolation]
core/*:            [english-only]
beads-workflow/*:  [english-only]
# Read-only agents — no standards needed
Explore:           []
researcher:        []
general-purpose:   []
```

Rules:
- Only inject for agents with Write/Edit/Bash in toolset (structural check via agent definition).
- Skip for read-only agents — their output doesn't need coding standards, and injection bloats their context.
- Injection format: prepend standards *paths* to agent prompt (not full content), let agent read them lazily.

## Trinity Labels

- `agent-standards.yml` = Enforcer-Proactive (shapes prompt before spawn)
- `standards/dev-tools/subagent-standards.md` = ADR (routing law)
- TaskCreated hook = the checkpoint that applies the ADR via the Proactive-Enforcer

## Method of Change (MoC)

| Measure | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| % of dev-tools/medical Subagent-Spawns mit Standards injected | ~0 (manual, forgotten) | 100% (hook-enforced) |
| Context bloat on read-only agents | — | 0 (explicit skip) |
| Mean spawn overhead | baseline | <100ms added |

## Scope

**In scope:**
- TaskCreated hook (v2.1.89+) in `~/.claude/hooks/`
- `agent-standards.yml` mapping + `standards/dev-tools/subagent-standards.md` doc
- Off-switch via env var

**Out of scope:**
- Per-subagent cost metrics (see sibling bead)
- Dynamic standards composition beyond the mapping table

## Scenario
**Precondition:** Hook `~/.claude/hooks/inject-subagent-standards.py` is registered under `hooks.TaskCreated` in `~/.claude/settings.json`. Mapping file `~/.claude/agent-standards.yml` exists and contains: `dev-tools/*: [english-only, no-emoji, tool-standards]`, `core/*: [english-only]`, `Explore: []`, `researcher: []`, `general-purpose: []`, plus an `orchestrator_handled: [dev-tools/*]` flag set. Standards files referenced by paths exist under `~/.claude/standards/`.
**Action:** Claude spawns a subagent via the Agent tool (TaskCreated fires with `agent_label` and optional env vars `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID` / `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS` in the child environment).
**Expected Result:** The hook inspects `agent_label` against `agent-standards.yml` and either prepends the matching standards *paths* (not content) to the agent's system prompt, or returns cleanly with no injection. Spawn overhead is <100ms. stderr is silent unless an error occurs.

### Test Data
- `agent-standards.yml` fixture with the five mappings + `orchestrator_handled` list above.
- Fake TaskCreated JSON payloads on stdin, one per variant, each carrying a realistic `agent_label`:
  - `dev-tools:implementer` (Write/Edit/Bash-capable, from `dev-tools/agents/implementer.md`)
  - `core:researcher` (read-only, from `core/agents/researcher.md`)
  - `Explore` (read-only built-in)
  - `general-purpose` (read-only built-in)
  - `beads-workflow:bead-orchestrator` (Write-capable, not in `orchestrator_handled`)
- Env fixtures: `{}`, `{CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID: "run-42"}`, `{CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS: "1"}`, `{CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID: "run-42", CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS: "1"}`.
- Golden strings for expected injected path lists per agent class (to assert exact content, not order-sensitive).

### Variants
1. **Happy path — dev-tools writer:** `agent_label=dev-tools:implementer`, no env flags set. → Hook injects paths for `english-only`, `no-emoji`, `tool-standards` into the spawn prompt. Exit 0.
2. **Skip path — read-only Explore:** `agent_label=Explore`, no env flags. → Empty mapping, hook injects nothing, exits 0 silently.
3. **Skip path — read-only researcher:** `agent_label=core:researcher`. → No injection (mapping is empty), exit 0.
4. **Skip path — read-only general-purpose:** `agent_label=general-purpose`. → No injection, exit 0.
5. **Orchestrator handled — skip:** `agent_label=dev-tools:implementer` AND `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=run-42` set, pattern `dev-tools/*` is in `orchestrator_handled`. → Hook detects orchestrator caller for a handled pattern and skips injection (orchestrator already did it). Exit 0.
6. **Orchestrator NOT handled — still inject:** `agent_label=beads-workflow:bead-orchestrator` AND `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=run-42` set, pattern is NOT in `orchestrator_handled`. → Hook still injects this agent's standards paths. Exit 0.
7. **Off-switch master override:** `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS=1` with `agent_label=dev-tools:implementer` (would otherwise inject). → No injection regardless of other conditions. Exit 0.
8. **Off-switch beats orchestrator flag:** Both `CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS=1` and `CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID=run-42` set. → No injection, off-switch wins. Exit 0.
9. **Unknown agent_label:** `agent_label=some:unmapped-agent` not in `agent-standards.yml`. → No injection (no matching pattern), exit 0, no error.
10. **Missing mapping file:** `agent-standards.yml` absent. → Hook exits 0 with a single stderr warning, no injection, does not block spawn.
11. **Malformed mapping file:** `agent-standards.yml` is invalid YAML. → Hook exits 0, logs warning to stderr, no injection, does not block spawn.
12. **Performance:** Any variant above completes in <100ms wall-clock on the reference machine (measured via `time` around hook invocation; asserted at p95 over 20 runs).
acceptance_criteria: 1) On Agent spawn, standards injected ONLY for agents matching agent-standards.yml pattern AND having Write/Edit/Bash tools. 2) Read-only agents (Explore, researcher, general-purpose) receive NO injection. 3) agent-standards.yml documented in standards/dev-tools/subagent-standards.md with explicit Trinity labels (ADR + Enforcer-Proactive). 4) Injection adds only paths to standards, not full content. 5) Off-switch via env var (e.g. CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS=1). 6) entropy-scan passes. 7) Spawn overhead <100ms.
notes:
[WAVE-REVIEW-PHASE-1.25 2026-04-21]

MEDIUM (ac_minor, RESOLVED via HITL): CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID behavior = "Per agent_label decision".
Required AC to add:
"If CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID is set in child env, the hook consults agent-standards.yml: if the matching pattern indicates the orchestrator already handles standards for this agent_label (e.g. dev-tools/* are pre-injected by orchestrator), skip injection; otherwise still inject. Default: skip injection when orchestrator is the caller AND pattern is in the orchestrator's known-handled set."

Action: Add this AC before impl start. Add a small mapping (or flag) to agent-standards.yml indicating which agent_labels are "orchestrator-handled" vs "hook-only".

MEDIUM (filename pinning applied):
- Hook file: ~/.claude/hooks/inject-subagent-standards.py
- settings.json key: hooks.TaskCreated (append, do not replace)
- Co-located mapping: ~/.claude/agent-standards.yml
- If CCP-2n7 merges first, rebase onto its settings.json entry.

LOW (architecture, plan-time check): AC #1's "Write/Edit/Bash tools" filter requires reading the agent definition at hook time. During plan phase, confirm whether TaskCreated payload exposes the toolset OR whether the hook reads the agent-definition YAML on disk. If neither is reliable, restate AC #1 as "matches agent-standards.yml pattern" only (toolset filter implicit via pattern map).

LOW (dispatch order): Recommended to land AFTER CCP-2n7 so the CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID contract is already in place. Not a hard dependency.
Break analysis: Hook fires on TaskCreated. Payload contains agent_label field. No existing inject-subagent-standards.py hook. settings.json has PreToolUse only, no TaskCreated. agent-standards.yml does not exist yet. Standards paths exist at ~/.claude/standards/. Using PyYAML for config parsing (PEP 723 uv script). Performance <100ms confirmed achievable with file-based approach. Injection via hookSpecificOutput.additionalSystemPrompt or stdout prepend - need to verify payload schema from actual behavior. Hardest AK: #7 spawn overhead <100ms with YAML parsing on every spawn.
Provenance logged: standards=[~/.claude/standards/python/style.md, ~/.claude/standards/dev-tools/hook-exit-codes.md, ~/.claude/standards/workflow/test-quality.md, ~/.claude/standards/workflow/tdd-discipline.md] skills=[] adrs=[] docs=[~/.claude/standards/dev-tools/subagent-standards.md (to be created)]

[WAVE-LIVE-FEEDBACK 2026-04-21 T+13min]
Live manual check found: ~/.claude/settings.json hook entry was written with WRONG schema — {type,command} directly instead of {matcher?,hooks:[{type,command}]}. /doctor flagged: "hooks.TaskCreated[0].hooks: Expected array, but received undefined".

Wave-orchestrator applied emergency fix to settings.json to keep Claude Code usable. Impl must produce the correct schema shape and include a test that parses the rendered settings.json against the Claude Code JSON Schema before session-close.

Reference working example (SubagentStop block from CCP-2n7 in same settings.json):
  {"hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "..."}]}
Review: status=FINDINGS-FIXED, quality=C→B (after fix), tdd=A, iter=1. Fixed: missing standards files (english-only, no-emoji, tool-standards), core:researcher exclusion, dead code, PEP 723 requires-python, redundant orchestrator re-match, hardcoded test path.
DECISION: auto-accept codex at iter 1, still-broken: uv shebang exit code behavior in restricted sandbox (exit 2 from uv cache init in Codex env). This is a Codex sandbox restriction, not a production issue — 29 tests pass in normal env, p95=38ms. REGRESSION 2+3 VERIFIED.
Close reason: All 7 ACs verified. TaskCreated hook ships with 29 tests, p95=38ms, Axis B for uv-sandbox finding (production-non-issue). run_id=6b37736d-479e-44e5-965b-558efe0cd80b
Wave 1 report 2026-04-21: Phase 6.5 integration-verification PASS (no CRITICAL). 3 WARNINGs filed as follow-up bead. Wave closed 14:10 local, elapsed ~53min, all beads merged to main.
close_reason: All 7 ACs verified: TaskCreated hook injects standards by subagent_type pattern, 29 tests green, p95=38ms, orchestrator_handled exclusion working, off-switch via CCP_NO_SUBAGENT_STANDARDS

title: [REFACTOR] Rollout: Debrief-Template in Subagent-Prompts einfügen
description:
## Context

Teil der "Single Source of Truth pro Session"-Architektur. Gefiled nach
Wave-Review 2026-04-21 als fehlender Rollout-Bead in der Debrief-Chain.

CCP-9bm definiert den Debrief-Contract (Standard + Parser + Lint) und liefert
eine Agent-Update-Guide-Liste. CCP-5xe baut den Orchestrator-Aggregator
(mit Mocks testbar). CCP-4gi baut den session-close-Consumer. Keiner dieser
drei Beads editiert die tatsaechlichen Subagent-Prompts, damit diese im
Runtime Debriefs emittieren.

Ohne Rollout laufen Orchestrator-Aggregation und session-close-Consumer
auf leeren Debriefs -- Pipeline existiert, Daten nicht.

## Goal

Jeden im Agent-Update-Guide gelisteten Subagent-Prompt so editieren, dass
er am Ende seiner finalen Message den Debrief-Template-Block emittiert.
Danach muss `check-debrief-adherence.py` (aus CCP-9bm) gruen laufen.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Fuer jeden Agent aus der Agent-Update-Guide-Liste in CCP-9bm
      (primaer: implementer, test-author, test-engineer, review-agent,
      verification-agent -- finale Liste steht im Standard):
      Prompt erweitert um die "Return Format / Debrief"-Sektion, exakt
      aus dem Template-Block des Standards kopiert.
- [ ] Debrief-Sektion steht am Ende des Agent-Prompts vor eventuellen
      Metrics-Logging-Sections, damit sie Teil der finalen Output-Message
      ist.
- [ ] Sections nutzen die vier Headings aus CCP-9bm:
      Key Decisions / Challenges Encountered / Surprising Findings /
      Follow-up Items.
- [ ] Leerzulassen einzelner Sections ist erlaubt (Agent hat z.B. keine
      Surprises).
- [ ] `check-debrief-adherence.py` laeuft gruen gegen die editierten
      Agents.
- [ ] Allowlisted Agents (Explorer, Researcher, general-purpose, andere
      Read-Only wie im Standard definiert) werden NICHT editiert.

## Non-Goals

- Contract / Parser / Lint selbst bauen (das ist CCP-9bm).
- Runtime-Verification ob Agents wirklich sinnvolle Debriefs emittieren
  (strukturelle Template-Praesenz genuegt; Content-Qualitaet waere
  separater Follow-up).
- Edits an Orchestrator oder session-close (CCP-5xe und CCP-4gi).

## Depends on

CCP-9bm (Contract + Agent-Update-Guide-Liste muessen stehen, bevor
Rollout starten kann)

## Szenario

**Vorbedingung:** CCP-9bm ist geclosed, Standard-Doc inkl. Agent-Update-Guide
liegt auf main, `check-debrief-adherence.py` existiert.

**Aktion:** Implementer editiert nacheinander alle gelisteten Agent-Prompts
und haengt den Debrief-Template-Block an.

**Erwartetes Ergebnis:** `check-debrief-adherence.py` exit 0, alle
gelisteten Agents enthalten den Block, Allowlist-Agents sind unveraendert.

title: wave-orchestrator: Phase 1.25 wave-review-gate integrieren
description:
## Warum

Der wave-orchestrator hat aktuell keinen wave-level strukturellen Review-Gate. Phase 1.5 (Compound-Detection) und Phase 1.5b (Architecture Council) checken nur einzelne Beads gegen sich selbst. Cross-bead Defekte — Dependency-Ordering-Fehler, Ownership-Collisions, Lifecycle-Widersprüche, Validation-Gaps — fehlen komplett und sorgen für teure Review-Oszillation nach Dispatch.

## Scope

Neue Phase 1.25 zwischen Phase 1 (Wave-Table) und Phase 1.5 (Scope-Analysis):

1. Spawn wave-reviewer als general-purpose Subagent
2. Subagent ruft direkt 'codex exec --sandbox read-only' auf (KEIN /codex oder /codex:rescue Wrapper), mit Sonnet-Fallback bei Codex-Fehler
3. Findings-Triage im Orchestrator nach Severity:
   - HIGH + fundamental (Wave-Redesign, Bead-Split): STOPP, User-Dialog
   - HIGH + lokal fixbar (fehlende Dep, falsche Ownership): User-Bestätigung, dann bd update
   - MEDIUM (AC-Minor, Quality<B, kleine Inkonsistenzen): Orchestrator fixt direkt via bd update, zeigt Applied-Summary
   - LOW / Ready Verdict 'Structurally ready': weiter zu Phase 1.5
4. Maximal 1 Re-Review-Loop nach Fixes; bei erneutem 'Not ready' → Eskalation an User (keine Endlos-Schleife)
5. Neues Flag --skip-wave-review analog zu --skip-review, Skip wird in Bead-Notes geloggt

## Acceptance Criteria

- /wave-orchestrator ruft wave-reviewer VOR Phase 1.5 auf (neue Phase 1.25 in SKILL.md dokumentiert)
- wave-reviewer wird als general-purpose Subagent gespawnt, der intern 'codex exec' direkt via Bash aufruft
- Bei codex CLI fehlt oder exit != 0: Subagent führt Review selbst mit Sonnet durch (Fallback)
- Severity-basierte Entscheidungslogik in SKILL.md dokumentiert (HIGH-fundamental vs HIGH-lokal vs MEDIUM vs LOW)
- HIGH-fundamental Findings: User-Dialog, kein stiller Fix (Beispiel im SKILL.md)
- HIGH-lokal + MEDIUM: Orchestrator-Edit via bd update mit Summary an User
- Genau 1 Re-Review nach Fixes; Eskalation bei erneutem 'Not ready'
- --skip-wave-review Flag funktioniert, Skip in Bead-Notes geloggt: 'Wave review skipped (--skip-wave-review flag). User: X, Y'
- JSON-Output-Schema der codex-Invocation im SKILL.md dokumentiert

## Out of Scope

- Änderungen am /codex oder /codex:rescue Skill/Agent (wird bewusst nicht verwendet)
- factory-check-Kriterien in wave-reviewer (Teil von CCP-pvw)

## Depends on

CCP-pvw (wave-reviewer muss factory-check-Kriterien bereits haben, damit Orchestrator konsolidiertes Review bekommt)


## Scenario
**Precondition:** A user has filed 4 beads for an epic (e.g. `demo-epic-a`, `demo-epic-b`, `demo-epic-c`, `demo-epic-d`) with mixed defects: one pair has an ownership collision (two beads claim the same helper), one bead is missing a `blocked_by` edge to its prerequisite, one bead has an AC with ambiguous wording, and one is structurally fine. The user has run through Phase 0 (discovery) and Phase 1 (wave-table with dependency graph). `codex` CLI is installed on the machine and responds to `codex exec --sandbox read-only`. The `wave-reviewer` skill is installed and `CCP-pvw` (factory-check criteria) is merged.
**Action:** User runs `/wave-orchestrator demo-epic-a demo-epic-b demo-epic-c demo-epic-d`. The orchestrator completes Phase 1 and enters the new Phase 1.25.
**Expected Result:** The orchestrator spawns a `general-purpose` subagent that invokes `codex exec --sandbox read-only` directly (no `/codex` or `/codex:rescue` wrapper). The subagent returns a JSON findings report. The orchestrator triages findings by severity:
- HIGH-fundamental → halts, presents finding + proposed bead-split, waits for user
- HIGH-lokal → presents finding + proposed `bd update` (e.g. add `blocked_by`), waits for user confirmation, then applies and triggers exactly one re-review
- MEDIUM → applies `bd update` directly, reports an "Applied fixes" summary to the user
- LOW / `Structurally ready` → proceeds to Phase 1.5
If the re-review still reports `Not ready for dispatch`, the orchestrator escalates to the user (no second loop). If `codex` exits non-zero or is not on PATH, the subagent falls back to Sonnet and the orchestrator logs `codex_fallback: sonnet` in the bead notes. With `--skip-wave-review`, Phase 1.25 is skipped and logged per bead: `Wave review skipped (--skip-wave-review flag). User: <whoami>, <ISO-8601 UTC>`.

### Test Data

Four test beads seeded with deliberate defects so every triage path fires:

| Bead | Defect class | Expected severity + category |
|------|--------------|------------------------------|
| `demo-epic-a` | Compound scope: title "Add X and refactor Y", 4 ACs touching 3 orthogonal modules | HIGH `fundamental` — requires split |
| `demo-epic-b` | References `ServiceX.update()` but missing `blocked_by: demo-epic-c` (which introduces ServiceX) | HIGH `lokal` — add dep edge |
| `demo-epic-c` | AC-2 says "validate input" without specifying which validator; factory-check quality grade C | MEDIUM — tighten AC text + raise grade via `bd update` |
| `demo-epic-d` | Clean single-concern task bead with explicit ACs | LOW — passes |

Expected `codex exec` JSON contract (what the subagent must return to the orchestrator):

```json
{
  "verdict": "Not ready for dispatch",
  "reviewed_at": "2026-04-21T10:15:00Z",
  "reviewed_beads": ["demo-epic-a", "demo-epic-b", "demo-epic-c", "demo-epic-d"],
  "findings": [
    {
      "bead_ids": ["demo-epic-a"],
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "category": "fundamental",
      "finding": "Compound scope — ACs span 3 orthogonal modules. Review-oscillation risk.",
      "recommendation": "Split into demo-epic-a1 (X) + demo-epic-a2 (refactor Y); make a2 depend on a1."
    },
    {
      "bead_ids": ["demo-epic-b", "demo-epic-c"],
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "category": "lokal",
      "finding": "demo-epic-b references ServiceX.update() introduced by demo-epic-c but has no blocked_by edge.",
      "recommendation": "bd dep add demo-epic-b demo-epic-c"
    },
    {
      "bead_ids": ["demo-epic-c"],
      "severity": "MEDIUM",
      "category": "ac_minor",
      "finding": "AC-2 'validate input' is ambiguous. Factory-check quality = C.",
      "recommendation": "Replace AC-2 with explicit validator name; target quality >= B."
    },
    {
      "bead_ids": ["demo-epic-d"],
      "severity": "LOW",
      "category": "info",
      "finding": "Structurally ready.",
      "recommendation": "No action."
    }
  ],
  "codex_exit": 0,
  "codex_fallback": null
}
```

After the orchestrator applies MEDIUM fixes and user-confirmed HIGH-lokal fixes, it re-invokes the subagent. The expected second-pass JSON:

```json
{
  "verdict": "Structurally ready",
  "findings": [],
  "codex_exit": 0,
  "codex_fallback": null
}
```

### Variants

- **Happy path:** All four beads come back `Structurally ready` on first pass → orchestrator proceeds straight to Phase 1.5 with no user interaction.
- **HIGH-fundamental only:** `demo-epic-a` flagged for split → orchestrator STOPS, shows split proposal, awaits user; on reject, logs override note and continues; on accept, creates sub-beads via `bd create` + `bd supersede` and re-runs Phase 1.
- **HIGH-lokal only:** Missing dependency edge → orchestrator shows exact `bd dep add` command, awaits confirmation, applies, triggers one re-review.
- **MEDIUM only:** AC wording + quality grade → orchestrator auto-fixes via `bd update --description` / `bd update --append-notes`, shows "Applied fixes" summary, proceeds.
- **Codex CLI missing or non-zero exit:** Subagent detects `command -v codex` fails or `codex exec` returns non-zero → performs the review itself using Sonnet, sets `codex_fallback: "sonnet"` and `codex_exit: <code>` in JSON. Orchestrator continues triage unchanged but annotates the wave audit with `codex_fallback: sonnet`.
- **`--skip-wave-review` flag:** Phase 1.25 is fully skipped. For each bead in the wave, orchestrator appends: `bd update <id> --append-notes="Wave review skipped (--skip-wave-review flag). User: <whoami>, <ISO-8601 UTC>"`.
- **Re-review loop bounded:** After applying fixes, re-review still returns `Not ready for dispatch` → orchestrator does NOT loop again; it prints the second-pass findings and escalates to the user with a Continue/Abort prompt.
- **Mixed severity (the full fixture above):** Orchestrator must handle HIGH-fundamental first (STOP + user), then after resolution re-run Phase 1.25 where HIGH-lokal + MEDIUM are processed in the same pass; verifies correct ordering of halts vs auto-fixes.
notes:
AC-Ergänzungen (nach Diskussion):

- Interaktion mit Phase 1.5b dokumentieren: wave-reviewer-Findings mit Kategorie 'architecture' werden an Phase 1.5b als Kontext weitergereicht (nicht redundant re-reviewed). Architecture Council Agent bekommt die relevanten Findings im Prompt.
- --dry-run Verhalten: Phase 1.25 läuft (User sieht strukturelle Probleme), aber KEINE bd update-Edits. Findings werden nur angezeigt.
- --skip-wave-review Flag überspringt Phase 1.25 komplett (analog zu --skip-review für 1.5b)
Provenance logged: standards=[none] skills=[wave-orchestrator, wave-reviewer] adrs=[none] docs=[beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/skill.md, beads-workflow/skills/wave-reviewer/SKILL.md]
Skill Application Advisory:
| Skill | Status | Evidence |
|-------|--------|----------|
| wave-orchestrator | likely-applied | skill.md is the direct artifact — Phase 1.25 inserted at lines 221-482 |
| wave-reviewer | unclear | Referenced by name in lines 264, 307 as Sonnet-fallback checklist source; wave-reviewer skill itself not modified |
Close reason: Phase 1.25 wave-review-gate added to wave-orchestrator. Quality A, all 11 ACs verified. 2 adversarial findings fixed (PHASE_125_ARCH_FINDINGS init, dry-run path-B guard). run_id=ed826c24-fba3-4d94-b83e-0bee5b5eac00
Wave 2 report: Phase 1.25 wave-review-gate integrated. Codex adversarial caught 3 silent-success defects (PHASE_125_ARCH_FINDINGS init, dry-run guard, re-review trigger).
