Urspruenglich gefilet als CCP-2vo.8.5 / CCP-2vo.8.6 im A/B-Validation-Frame (CCP-2vo.8). Frame obsolet nach CCP-2vo.10 (2-pane removal); Refactor-Bedarf bleibt.

title: PermissionDenied v2 — interactive-deny learning via PreToolUse-block parsing
description:
## Problem

CCP-rzf v1 covers auto-mode PermissionDenied events only. Interactive denies (user clicks "Deny" in the permission prompt) do NOT fire the PermissionDenied hook — the only interception point is PreToolUse with `{"decision": "block"}`.

This bead extends the learning loop to cover the interactive path, closing the blind spot.

## Proposed Solution

A PreToolUse hook that:
1. Runs AFTER the standard PreToolUse chain (so existing block rules take precedence).
2. Inspects the final decision: if `{"decision": "block"}` AND the block is attributable to a user "Deny" click (not an automated rule), log it.
3. Routes the log entry through the same classifier + threshold logic already built in CCP-rzf.
4. Shares the draft-suggestion queue with the v1 hook (same `~/.claude/permission-suggestions.json`).

## Key Design Concern

PreToolUse fires for EVERY tool call — not just denies. Implementation must:
- Exit fast on approvals (hot path).
- Only classify + persist on deny outcomes.
- Target sub-10ms overhead on the approval path.

## Attribution Problem

Distinguishing "user clicked Deny" from "a rule blocked" requires reading the hook context (source of the block). If the source is a rule, skip logging (the rule already expresses the policy). If the source is an interactive user decision, log it.

This attribution heuristic may need iteration — ship with conservative "log everything not attributable to a known rule" and tune based on false-positive rate.

## Trinity Labels

- Reuses CCP-rzf's ADR (standards/dev-tools/permission-classifier.md) and Helper (suggestion generator).
- This bead = second Enforcer-Reactive tap covering the interactive deny path.

## Method of Change (MoC)

| Measure | Before (post-rzf) | After |
|---|---|---|
| % of permission denies captured | auto-mode only (~?%) | auto-mode + interactive (~100%) |
| PreToolUse approval-path overhead | — | <10ms |
| Shared suggestion queue with v1 | — | yes (single JSON file) |

## Scope

**In scope:**
- PreToolUse hook in `~/.claude/hooks/` that logs interactive denies.
- Attribution heuristic for distinguishing user-deny vs rule-block.
- Integration with CCP-rzf's classifier + suggestion generator.

**Out of scope:**
- New classifier categories beyond what CCP-rzf defines.
- Auto-apply of suggestions (remains HITL per CCP-rzf).
- Cross-session or fleet-wide aggregation.
acceptance_criteria: 1) PreToolUse hook logs interactive denies to the same append-only log used by CCP-rzf. 2) Attribution heuristic correctly distinguishes user-deny from rule-block in at least the common cases; false-positive rate documented. 3) Approval-path overhead <10ms (fast exit when decision != block). 4) Shared suggestion queue with CCP-rzf v1 (single ~/.claude/permission-suggestions.json). 5) Destructive denies NEVER generate suggestions (inherited from CCP-rzf classifier). 6) Off-switch via env var. 7) entropy-scan passes.

title: [REFACTOR] project-context should read BOTH CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md when both exist
description:
## Context

During CCP-c2p pilot validation (2026-04-20), user noticed that running `project-context` in Codex only picks up `AGENTS.md`, not the much richer `CLAUDE.md` that also lives in the repo root. In this project:

- `AGENTS.md` (162 lines) — minimal: bd quickref, Architecture Trinity summary, session-close rules
- `CLAUDE.md` (user-global + project-level) — rich: Safety Checks, Core Rules, Confirmation Gates, Content Isolation, full workflow patterns

Codex misses the substantive content because the current portable skill phrasing is:
```
Load `./CLAUDE.md` or the equivalent project conventions file for your harness.
```
This is harness-agnostic but assumes ONE file. Projects that ship BOTH (common in claude-code-plugins era and any multi-harness repo) lose the richer source.

## Why

The skill's Phase 1 §1a declares the project conventions file as the PRIMARY source for Architecture Principles and Critical Invariants. Picking the wrong (thinner) one silently produces a worse `docs/project-context.md`. Codex users on this repo never see Safety Checks / Confirmation Gates / Content Isolation in the generated constitution.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `.agents/skills/project-context/SKILL.md` Phase 1 §1a is updated to load ALL existing conventions files (`CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md`), concatenating them with a source header per file.
2. The source header pattern is documented (e.g. `# From CLAUDE.md` / `# From AGENTS.md`) so downstream Phase 2 analysis knows provenance.
3. If only one file exists, behavior is unchanged (no regression).
4. Phase 4 Report template lists BOTH files under `**Sources used:**` (`CLAUDE.md: found/not found`, `AGENTS.md: found/not found`).
5. `dev-tools/skills/project-context/SKILL.md` (SoT) gets the same change, then re-synced into `.agents/skills/` via `scripts/sync-codex-skills` (drift-check via `--check` must pass).
6. Portability-rules doc gets a note (adjacent to Rule 1 about single project-conventions file) that multi-file concat is the correct pattern when multiple harness conventions files coexist.
7. Regression test in `tests/test_codex_pilot.py` or `tests/test_portability_split.py` that exercises the multi-file case.

## Design Note

This is the opposite direction from Option B in the original design (which assumed ONE file). That assumption broke for multi-harness repos. The fix is additive (concat all found) not exclusive (pick one).
notes: Filed 2026-04-21 by user request during Codex pilot validation. Discovered when the user observed project-context only pulled AGENTS.md in Codex, missing the richer CLAUDE.md content. Related: CCP-bc4 (AC#2 evidence capture might surface the same gap empirically). Follow-up candidate beyond this: when project-context is refactored, consider how the skill's Phase 2 analysis reconciles conflicting rules between CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md (prefer one? cross-reference? flag divergence?).
close_reason: Implemented and merged in commit bc17330 / caf7166 (2026-04-21). project-context skill now loads BOTH CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md when both exist.

title: [REFACTOR] session-close liest Debriefs aus Orchestrator-Handoff statt globalem Transcript
description:
## Context

Teil der "Single Source of Truth pro Session"-Architektur. Blocked auf CCP-bd-aggregate (bead-orchestrator aggregiert Debriefs).

Heute extrahiert core:session-close Step 10 (Learnings Extraction) via learning-extractor-Agent Learnings aus dem globalen Session-Transcript. Das ist:
- Teuer: LLM-Parse von JSONL-Dateien
- Noisig: enthält irrelevante Tool-Outputs, Meta-Events, etc.
- Ungenau: wichtige "Decisions" sind oft implizit in Assistant-Antworten versteckt

Mit dem neuen Debrief-Contract + Orchestrator-Aggregation gibt es eine strukturierte, bereits gefilterte Quelle direkt vom bead-orchestrator.

## Goal

core:session-close Step 10/11 konsumiert das strukturierte Debrief-Aggregat vom Orchestrator-Handoff statt learning-extractor auf dem rohen Transcript laufen zu lassen. Schneller, günstiger, präziser.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] core:session-close-Agent (oder wo das Logic lebt) erkennt Orchestrator-Handoff: prüft ob Debrief-Aggregate vorhanden ist
- [ ] Wenn ja: learnings-extract überspringt das Transcript-Parsing, nutzt direkt das Aggregat
- [ ] Wenn nein (ad-hoc Session ohne bead-orchestrator): fallback auf bestehenden learning-extractor-Pfad
- [ ] Step 12 (canonical session_summary write) bekommt reichhaltigeren Content: Discoveries/Decisions/Surprises/Follow-ups strukturiert im narrative-Feld
- [ ] session-close setzt metadata.source="session-close" beim Memory-Write (für Dedup-Koordination mit SessionEnd-Safety-Net, siehe open-brain-Beads)
- [ ] Dokumentiert in core:session-close-Agent-Spec: "Wenn Orchestrator-Handoff vorhanden, skip learning-extractor"

## Non-Goals

- Ersatz von learning-extractor für ad-hoc Sessions (der bleibt als Fallback)
- Globale Änderung des session-close-Workflows (nur Step 10/11 werden optimiert)

## Depends on

CCP-* bead für bead-orchestrator Debrief-Aggregation
notes:
[WAVE-REVIEW 2026-04-21]

Severity: HIGH (1), LOW (1). Source: /wave-reviewer over Debrief-Chain.

### HIGH-1: Step 10 und Step 11 nicht konflatieren
Original-AC sagt "Step 10/11 konsumiert das strukturierte Debrief-Aggregat
statt learning-extractor auf dem rohen Transcript laufen zu lassen". Das
vermischt zwei verschiedene Mechanismen:

- Step 10 (core/agents/session-close.md:186-193) ist learning-extractor auf
  dem globalen JSONL-Transcript. Scope: cross-bead Patterns, User-Choices,
  Skill-Usage, Meta-Events. SUPERSET der vier Debrief-Listen.
- Step 11 (session-close.md:195-211) synthetisiert Debriefs aus
  Subagent-Return-Text. Das ist genau der Scope des neuen Aggregats.

Wenn Step 10 durch das Aggregat ERSETZT wird, gehen cross-bead Learnings und
User-Pattern-Detection verloren.

Concrete AC-Update:
- Step 11 wird auf das Orchestrator-Aggregat umgestellt (primaere Quelle)
- Step 10 bleibt bestehen UND behaelt learning-extractor auf Transcript
  ODER wird bewusst retired -> Entscheidung + Begruendung dokumentieren
  (nicht implizit wegfallen lassen)
- Wenn Orchestrator-Handoff fehlt (ad-hoc Session ohne bead-orchestrator):
  Step 11 Fallback auf Transcript-Parse, nicht silent skip

### Abgeleitete Aenderungen
- Heading-Taxonomie (CCP-9bm User-Entscheidung 2026-04-21):
  Key Decisions / Challenges Encountered / Surprising Findings /
  Follow-up Items -> Step 11 aendert sich NICHT (matched bereits).
- Parser-Invocation (CCP-9bm User-Entscheidung 2026-04-21):
  Python-Helper unter beads-workflow/lib/orchestrator/parse_debrief.py.
  session-close muss aber nicht parsen -> Orchestrator liefert bereits
  aggregiertes JSON. session-close liest nur.
- Handoff-Mechanismus (CCP-5xe einschraenkung): session-close muss das
  konkret in CCP-5xe gewaehlte Verfahren lesen. Entsprechende AC-Klausel
  bleibt abhaengig von CCP-5xe.

### LOW-1: Forward-Reference zu open-brain-Beads
metadata.source="session-close" koordiniert mit SessionEnd-Safety-Net, die
nicht in dieser Wave sind. Akzeptiert als cross-wave Koordinationssurface,
kein Blocker. Bei Implementierung in Notes verlinken, welche open-brain-Beads
den gleichen Kontrakt adressieren.

title: [REFACTOR] project-context SKILL.md: inline Codex-compatible gather commands
description: Lines 72-76 of .agents/skills/project-context/SKILL.md (and dev-tools/skills/project-context/SKILL.md) still say 'using your harness's file-read and glob primitives'. Inline Codex-compatible commands (rg, fd, pathlib.glob) for the gather phase so Codex can execute it without a harness adapter.

title: [EPIC STUB] Project-specific workflow extensibility (orchestrator hooks, cross-repo fixtures)
description:
## Context

Several projects (notably MIRA + mira-adapters) have workflow needs that the core bead-orchestrator doesn't handle natively:

- **Cross-repo fixture coordination:** generating test data in one repo that another repo's tests consume
- **Pre-impl hooks:** projects that need custom pre-impl setup (fixture generation, schema sync, catalog download) before the implementer runs
- **Per-project verification extensions:** domain-specific compliance checks beyond the generic verification-agent role (e.g., "every new endpoint must have an OpenAPI entry", "every mira adapter must declare a scope")
- **Post-close side-effects:** cross-repo notifications, fixture propagation

## Scope — EPIC STUB (breakdown later)

This is a placeholder epic. Actual sub-beads TBD after bead #10 (UAT audit) completes, since UAT findings will shape the fixture-coordination design.

Preliminary design sketch (non-binding):

```yaml
# .beads/orchestrator-hooks.yml  (proposed per-project config)
pre_impl:
  - script: ./scripts/generate-fixtures.sh
    when: "files_match: src/adapters/**/*"
    cross_repo: "../mira"

pre_verification:
  - script: ./scripts/cross-repo-fixture-check.sh

post_close:
  - notify: mira-adapters
    via: bd-create
    title: "Adapter {bead_id} shipped — check fixture compatibility"

verification_extensions:
  - check: openapi-entry-exists
    script: ./scripts/check-openapi.sh
```

## Expected sub-beads (to file after #10 lands)

- Design `orchestrator-hooks.yml` schema
- Implement hook runner in bead-orchestrator Phase-0 setup
- Implement cross-repo fixture coordination (MIRA-specific pilot)
- Document hook patterns in standards
- Migrate existing per-project workarounds to hooks

## Acceptance (for this stub)

- Epic exists, visible in backlog
- Referenced from bead #10's follow-up discussion
- Remains open until #10 concludes and actual sub-beads are filed

## Notes

No work starts until #10 completes. This bead exists to preserve the design context and ensure it's not forgotten.
acceptance_criteria: Stub remains open; sub-beads filed after #10 lands; references preserved for future breakdown.

title: /project-setup: /docs/adr/ scaffolding + adr-location standards doc
description:
## Context

verification-agent (CCP-2vo.9) will check for ADRs at canonical paths. Many existing projects don't have a dedicated ADR directory. `/project-setup` should bootstrap one.

**Adversarial review finding:** earlier scope targeted `standards/workflow/adr-location.md` linked from `standards/index.yml`. The actual standards loader uses `~/.claude/standards/index.yml` and `.claude/standards/index.yml` (`core/skills/inject-standards/SKILL.md:50-53`, `dev-tools/skills/project-setup/SKILL.md:114-116`). A doc at the wrong path won't be discoverable. Corrected below.

## Scope

1. **Add to `/project-setup` skill:** when scaffolding a new project OR upgrading an existing one, create `/docs/adr/` with:
   - `README.md` explaining the ADR convention (MADR-style template or similar)
   - `ADR-0001-record-architecture-decisions.md` as the bootstrap self-referential ADR
   - `.gitkeep` or placeholder to ensure directory is tracked

2. **Write `.claude/standards/workflow/adr-location.md`** (NOT `standards/workflow/...`). Document `/docs/adr/` as the canonical location. Note legacy alternatives (`/docs/adrs/`, `.claude/adrs/`) that verification-agent accepts for backwards compatibility.

3. **Index entry in `.claude/standards/index.yml`** so inject-standards can surface the ADR convention on relevant triggers.

4. **Fleet audit.** Add `bd doctor --check=adr-location` (or similar) to report projects missing the canonical ADR directory. Not enforced, reported only.

## Acceptance

- `/project-setup` creates `/docs/adr/` on new-project scaffolding
- `/project-setup` upgrade path creates `/docs/adr/` if missing
- Standards doc lives at `.claude/standards/workflow/adr-location.md`
- Entry added to `.claude/standards/index.yml` with appropriate triggers
- Fleet audit check reports missing ADR dirs
- Doc is discoverable via inject-standards on ADR-relevant triggers (test with a trigger phrase)

## Notes

Independent of CCP-2vo epic. Prerequisite for verification-agent's ADR check (CCP-2vo.9) to be meaningful fleet-wide.
acceptance_criteria: /project-setup creates /docs/adr/ on scaffold + upgrade; standards doc at .claude/standards/workflow/adr-location.md (NOT standards/workflow/); indexed in .claude/standards/index.yml; fleet audit check; discoverable via inject-standards triggers.
notes: Path corrected per adversarial review — was targeting standards/workflow/ which is not loader-discoverable.

title: bd-lint-contracts: enforce mutual exclusion of `- [ ] None` with other gap markers
description:
Currently the linter accepts `- [ ] None` AND other gap bullets (`- [ ] [ADR-NEEDED] ...`) coexisting in the same `## Gaps to Close` section. The bead implicitly contradicts itself: 'no gaps' + 'ADR needed at the same time'.

Tighten the rule: if `- [ ] None` is present, NO other gap markers allowed.

## Why this matters

Codex flagged it during CCP-0hr review as a logical inconsistency. cmux-reviewer correctly downgraded because spec 3c didn't require mutual exclusion — but the user can decide to tighten it now.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Linter rule: if `- [ ] None` present, fail on any other `- [ ] [XXX-NEEDED]` bullet
- [ ] Test fixtures:
  - only `- [ ] None` → passes
  - only NEEDED markers → passes
  - both coexist → fails with clear error message
- [ ] Update `beads-workflow/skills/create/references/contract-sections.md` to document the mutual exclusion rule
notes: Discovered during CCP-0hr review (Codex finding, downgraded by cmux-reviewer). User chose to file as follow-up rather than expand spec mid-iteration. Clean scope for a small bead.
close_reason: Mutual exclusion check implemented: None + NEEDED coexist fails with clear error. 2 new tests, 49/49 pass. contract-sections.md updated. Codex: approve, 0 findings.

title: bd-lint-contracts: PATH-shim so `bd lint --check=architecture-contracts` works without sourcing shell extension
description:
The CCP-0hr implementation requires sourcing `beads-workflow/scripts/bd-lint-extension.sh` in the user's shell for `bd lint --check=architecture-contracts` to work. Make it Just Work without that step.

## Why this matters

CI scenarios can't easily source shell extensions. New developers won't know to source it. Codex review flagged this during CCP-0hr (was downgraded because spec W1 didn't mandate it, but the friction is real).

## Approaches to investigate

1. **PATH-shim**: install `bd-lint-contracts` wrapper in `~/.local/bin` or `/usr/local/bin` that calls the Python script directly. `bd lint` plus the wrapper handle the wiring.
2. **bd plugin/extension API**: investigate if `bd` itself supports loading external lint check modules (e.g. via `~/.bd/plugins/` directory). This would be the cleanest path.
3. **Just document the source step prominently** in install docs and call it done — fallback if 1 and 2 are too complex.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Investigation report: which of 1/2/3 is the right path for this repo's beads version
- [ ] Implementation of chosen approach
- [ ] Install/setup docs updated
- [ ] CI smoke test: invoke `bd lint --check=architecture-contracts` in a clean shell with no .bashrc sourcing
- [ ] Test fixture: works on macOS bash + zsh, Linux bash
notes:
Discovered during CCP-0hr close (Phase 7 learnings, 2026-04-19). Codex flagged 'high severity' but cmux-reviewer correctly downgraded — spec W1 deferred CI/hooks. This bead addresses the residual friction without contradicting the spec.
Close reason: PATH-shim bd-wrapper implemented (POSIX sh), 6/6 smoke tests passing. Files: bd-wrapper, install-bd-wrapper.sh, tests/test_bd_wrapper.sh. Installer copies to ~/.local/bin/bd with BD_WRAPPER_MARKER for loop prevention.
close_reason: PATH-shim bd-wrapper implemented (POSIX sh), 6/6 smoke tests passing. Installer copies to ~/.local/bin/bd with BD_WRAPPER_MARKER for loop prevention. All adversarial review findings addressed.

title: Constitution drift report: vision.md vs project-context.md mismatch detection
description:
Detect drift between the prescriptive constitution (vision.md, hand-written) and the descriptive constitution (project-context.md, auto-generated). Surface mismatches as DECIDE items so vision/code can be reconciled deliberately.

**Why separate from CCP-k1u:**
- Different cadence: post-regeneration (or scheduled patrol), not per-bead
- Different consumer: human reviewer, not orchestrator gate
- Shares rules engine with k1u but trigger is different

**Mechanism:**
- Compare LOCKED sections of vision.md (boundary table, layers list, future-contract section) against current project-context.md (module map, detected patterns)
- Report mismatches in two directions:
  - **Vision is behind**: project-context shows a module/pattern that vision doesn't acknowledge (e.g. packages/agent-api/ exists but vision §4 still has it as planned)
  - **Code is behind**: vision declares a module/contract that project-context doesn't show (e.g. packages/cognovis-install-pvs/ in vision §5 but missing from filesystem)

**Surface as:**
- New skill /constitution-drift (or /project-context --drift flag)
- Output: structured DECIDE list — each mismatch + suggested resolution (update vision OR file bead to make code match)
- Optional: scheduled patrol via cron skill (weekly drift report)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] /constitution-drift (or equivalent) command exists
- [ ] Reads vision.md locked sections and project-context.md current state
- [ ] Outputs both directions of drift (vision-behind, code-behind)
- [ ] Suggested resolution per mismatch (update doc | file bead)
- [ ] Smoke test: introduce a fake drift, command detects it

**Depends on:**
- CCP-u87 (need named/parseable Reading Corpus section to know which sections to lock)
- CCP-k1u (shares rules engine — pull common code into a shared utility)
acceptance_criteria: Drift command detects vision↔project-context mismatches, surfaces both directions, suggests resolutions, smoke-tested
notes: Suggested as 'fourth child' by mira-agent review of CCP-w56 epic, 2026-04-19. Could fold into k1u if it stays small (<200 LOC), but separate file/skill keeps the cadence/consumer split clean.
close_reason: Superseded by Trinity-Harness CCP-wxv (review-agent Contract-Drift-Phase). Folded in as a 5th heuristic ('code violates a stated vision principle'). The 'vision-behind-code' case that justified a separate drift report is impossible by construction (vision has no future tense — anything future-tense is a bead). Drift is one-directional only. See CCP-wxv.

title: [CHORE] Bead-Label-Set: needs-adr, needs-helper, needs-enforcer-*, hoist-pending, allowlist-debt, pre-trinity-package, touches-contract
description:
Definiert und dokumentiert das Label-Set für Trinity-Workflow. Labels werden von architecture-scout, review-agent, allowlist-burndown und wave-orchestrator verwendet — brauchen konsistente Namen und Bedeutungen.

## Label-Set

- **touches-contract** — Bead verändert etwas, das einen Cross-Cutting-Contract berührt. Triggert bd lint --check=architecture-contracts
- **needs-adr** — Bead erzeugt oder deckt einen Contract auf, der kein ADR hat
- **needs-helper** — Ein shared Helper (typed primitive) fehlt noch
- **needs-enforcer-proactive** — Codegen/Builder fehlt
- **needs-enforcer-reactive** — Lint/Test fehlt
- **hoist-pending** — ADR hat hoist_when-Bedingung, die eingetreten ist (auto-gesetzt von /adr-gap)
- **allowlist-debt** — Bead adressiert einen Allowlist-Eintrag (auto-gesetzt von /allowlist-burndown --create-beads)
- **pre-trinity-package** — Ziel-Package hat keines der drei Artefakte für diesen Contract

## Doku

beads-workflow/skills/create/references/trinity-labels.md mit Definition, wann jedes Label gesetzt/entfernt wird, Auto-Erzeuger.

## Konvention

Beads mit needs-* Labels sind P1/P0 prioritärer als solche ohne. wave-orchestrator bevorzugt sie in Wave-Assembly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Label-Doku beads-workflow/skills/create/references/trinity-labels.md erstellt
- [ ] Alle 8 Labels dokumentiert mit Definition, Auto-Erzeuger, manuelle Nutzung
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create/SKILL.md verweist auf Label-Doku
- [ ] Konvention dokumentiert: wave-orchestrator Priorisierungsregel für needs-*
- [ ] Keine Code-Änderungen — reines Doku-Bead
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. P3 — Konvention, nicht Code. Kann parallel zu Bead-Pflicht-Sektion (CCP-0hr) laufen, weil die Pflicht-Sektion das touches-contract-Label einführt.
## Refinements migrated from CCP-660 (Layer tagging, superseded 2026-04-19)

Add to the label set:

- **layer:<name>** — architectural layer the bead touches. Values configurable per project (no hard-coded enum). For mira-adapters: platform/application/agent/ig/catalog/pvs-adapter. For simple CLI projects: layers list may be absent entirely.

**Enforcement modes** (configurable in .claude/project-config.yml):

- `warn` (default): /create suggests a layer label; bead can be created without
- `hard-fail`: /create rejects bead creation without a valid layer:* label

Default warn so adoption isn't blocked. Projects opt into hard-fail when their layer model is mature.

**Tag inheritance**: child beads inherit parent's layer label by default. User can override at /create time. Same pattern beads already uses for other labels — keep consistency.

**Layers source**: read from .claude/project-config.yml `layers:` list, OR from docs/project-context.md Module Map → Layers subsection if config absent. No hard-coded values anywhere in harness code.

title: [FEATURE] /council --mode=architecture: Historian/Documentarian/Enforcer-Trio
description:
Erweitert business/skills/council um einen Architecture-Modus mit drei neuen Perspektiven, die speziell das Tripel-Denken beim Spec-/ADR-Writing vertreten.

## Drei neue Perspektiven

- **Historian**: liest bd search + open-brain timeline auf den Domain-Schlagworten. Fragt 'welche vergangenen Bug-Cluster verhindert / wiederholt dieses Dokument?'
- **Documentarian**: fragt 'welche impliziten Contracts müssen explizit werden? welche ADR fehlt?'
- **Enforcer**: fragt 'welcher Lint/Test/Codegen würde die Invariante nicht-umgehbar machen? ist das im Dokument festgelegt?' — mit Sub-Perspektive 'Codegen vs. Lint?' (proactive vs. reactive)

## Aktivierung

/council --mode=architecture (nur für ADR-Drafts, Specs, PRDs). Default-Modus bleibt End-User/Developer/Security/PO.

## Use Case

Besonders stark bei ADR-Drafts: ein ADR ohne konkreten Enforcer-Vorschlag ist unvollständig, und der Enforcer-Perspektiven-Agent sagt das.
design: Architecture-Modus läuft parallel zu den vier bestehenden Perspektiven (nicht Ersatz). Haiku-Subagents. Output: konsolidierte Tabelle sortiert nach Severity, wie bei Standard-Council.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] business/skills/council um --mode=architecture erweitert
- [ ] Drei Subagent-Prompts: historian, documentarian, enforcer
- [ ] Enforcer hat Sub-Frage: 'proactive (codegen) oder reactive (lint) bevorzugt?'
- [ ] Doku: references/architecture-council-prompts.md
- [ ] Integration-Test mit einem ADR-Draft als Input
notes: Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split. P3 — Nice-to-have, verbessert Spec-Qualität, aber nicht blocking für die Foundation.

title: [FIX] Remove || true from integration-check reference example (CCP-a81 follow-up)
description:
Follow-up from CCP-a81 iter-3 neutral review. The per-catalog query in beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/references/integration-verification-guide.md:177-180 uses:

  KBV_RESPONSE=$(curl -fsS --max-time 10 '...' 2>/dev/null) || true
  KBV_RC=$?

The '|| true' masks the curl exit code — $? is always 0 after this line, so KBV_RC is always 0 and the error branch at lines 181-185 is dead code. Transport/auth failures silently fall through to the JSON-parse path instead of emitting ERROR.

Fix: replace with either:
  KBV_RESPONSE=$(curl -fsS --max-time 10 '...' 2>/dev/null); KBV_RC=$?
  # (no || true; the subshell exit status is captured directly by $?)

Or:
  if ! KBV_RESPONSE=$(curl -fsS --max-time 10 '...' 2>/dev/null); then
    add_finding 'ERROR' 'infrastructure' ...
    STATUS='ERROR'
  else
    ...
  fi

Apply the same pattern to all per-catalog curls in the reference example.

title: [ARCH] Add catalog-keyword signals to arch-signal-detect.sh
description:
PRE_EXISTING finding from CCP-a81 Codex adversarial review.

Low-scoring catalog beads (score < 3) bypass both the arch-signal scorer AND the Haiku fallback, so a simple-looking bead importing KBV/DIMDI data can skip the Architecture Council entirely. Phase 6.5 integration-verification acts as a backstop but does not remove the upstream blind spot.

## Fix
Add catalog-specific keyword signals to scripts/arch-signal-detect.sh:
- STRONG signal (+3 pts) on: KBV|DIMDI|WHO|IFA|LOINC|SNOMED|ICD|Fachabteilungsschluessel
- STRONG signal (+3 pts) on: canonical.*catalog|external.*authorit
- OR force REVIEW_YES whenever bead description mentions these terms (regardless of total score)

This ensures any bead touching canonical reference catalogs gets architecture review, not just beads that happen to score >= 6 via other signals.

## Source
Discovered during CCP-a81 Codex adversarial review (phase 2). Not included in CCP-a81 scope per reviewer instruction.

title: Convert next 10 candidate skills to agentskills-compatible format
description: Why: after the first Codex pilot works, broader portability requires a prioritized batch conversion of additional skills. What: identify the next ten best candidates and convert them into the agreed portable format and structure. Acceptance: (1) ten additional candidate skills are selected and tracked, (2) each converted skill meets the agreed portability rules, (3) non-portable skills are explicitly deferred with rationale.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A candidate list of 10 additional skills is selected and tracked in this repo (suggested: docs/codex-skills-candidates.md or as a checklist appended to the rollout plan).
2. Selection criteria are documented and visible on the candidate list. Suggested criteria: instruction-heavy, low tool-dependency, no cmux / worktree / Claude-agent dependency, no hard dependency on Claude-specific slash commands or MCP tool names. Deferrals (e.g. beads-workflow/*, dev-tools/codex) must carry a short rationale.
3. Each converted skill conforms to the portability-rules note produced by CCP-tkd (portable core vs harness adapter, naming convention for adapter files).
4. Non-portable skills are explicitly deferred with rationale, not silently skipped.
5. All converted skills pass the CCP-u01 --check mode against their plugin-scoped source (no drift).
notes:
Wave review 2026-04-20: Acceptance tightened. "Agreed portability rules" now resolves to a concrete artifact (CCP-tkd note). Selection criteria made explicit. Drift-check gate via CCP-u01 --check mode added as a functional acceptance.
Provenance logged: standards=[git/conventional-commits.md] skills=[/codex,/codex-skills-candidates,/skills] adrs=[docs/architecture/2026-04-14-session/] docs=[none]
Review: status=FINDINGS-FIXED, quality=C, tdd=A, iter=1
Close reason: 10 candidate skills converted to portable format; sync-codex-skills extended with skills-registry.json; all 13 skills pass --check; 6 tests pass; review iter 1 FINDINGS-FIXED quality C tdd A; verification VERIFIED
close_reason: 10 candidate skills converted to agentskills-compatible portable format with SKILL.claude-adapter.md harness adapters; sync-codex-skills extended with skills-registry.json; docs/codex-skills-candidates.md created with selection criteria; all 13 skills pass --check drift gate; 6 new tests, 106 total passing; review iter 1 FINDINGS-FIXED quality C tdd A; verification VERIFIED

title: wave-status.sh: session_close pattern match is too loose, flags any scrollback mention
description:
Current check in wave-status.sh line ~85-86:
```bash
if echo "$SCREEN" | grep -q "session-close"; then
  STATUS="session_close"
  DETAIL="Session close in progress"
fi
```

Matches literal string 'session-close' anywhere in the 60-line scrollback. False-matches when:
- bead-orchestrator prints 'next phase: session-close' or 'handoff to session-close' in Phase 3/4 recaps
- quick-fix HANDOFF message mentions 'session close' in past tense
- any phase description text references session-close as an upcoming step

Observed: CCP-a81 was in Phase 3 (Implementation via Codex rescue, 10m into composing) but wave-status reported status=session_close. Caused a T+10min false reading during monitoring of CCP-a81.

Fix idea: only match when session-close is the ACTIVE process. Check for:
- core:session-close agent invocation marker (⏺ core:session-close)
- 'Bash(git push' or similar merge/push tool-use markers near the bottom
- Or require the string 'session close' to appear in one of the last 5 lines, not the full 60-line scrollback

Priority: P3 — cosmetic monitoring error, no functional impact (BD status is authoritative).
notes:
## May auto-resolve after CCP-2vo.10 (wave-review 2026-04-20)

CCP-2vo.10 deletes cld -br launcher support and the -review surface pattern. Once that lands, wave-status.sh no longer needs to parse scrollback for 'session-close' in the -review surface, and the parser simplifies substantially. Re-evaluate whether this bug still reproduces after CCP-2vo.10 closes — may be auto-resolved.

title: [CHORE] quick-fix agent: retrospective bead for Agent()-based session-close rewiring
description:
Infrastructure fix shipped during CCP-yun Wave 2 without a bead. Retrospective entry for traceability.

Commit: 4d741c6 fix(quick-fix): trigger session-close via Agent tool, not cmux send
(Superseded earlier attempt: 2049300 chore(beads-workflow): clarify cmux surface detection in quick-fix agent)

Changes:
- Phase 0 cmux surface detection removed (env var CMUX_SURFACE_ID not reliably propagated through cld → claude-code Bash tool)
- Phase 2 fix loop: spawn Sonnet subagent via Agent() instead of cmux send --surface $IMPL_SURFACE
- Phase 3 handoff: Agent(subagent_type='core:session-close', ...) runs inline in the same turn — no cmux send, no HANDOFF message
- LEARN: updated to forbid cmux-send-to-self for single-pane quick-fix agents

Trigger: during CCP-yun Wave 1 (CCP-1ul) and Wave 2 (CCP-2a2), the quick-fix agent finished implementation + review but failed to trigger session-close — beads sat idle at 'Next: session close' prompt until user manually triggered it. Root cause was confused single-pane vs multi-pane design: agent tried to cmux send to its own (undetectable) surface instead of spawning session-close inline.

Status: shipped to main, closing immediately. Closes CCP-tcy as obsolete.
notes: Retrospective bead — commit already on main. Supersedes deleted draft CCP-tcy.

title: [CHORE] wave-orchestrator: retrospective bead for dead-pane detection fix

Infrastructure fix shipped during CCP-yun Wave 1 monitoring without a bead. Retrospective entry for traceability.

Commit: e60feda fix(wave-orchestrator): detect dead cmux panes via error pattern

Changes:
- wave-status.sh + wave-completion.sh now match cmux error patterns (invalid_params, not a terminal, Surface.*not found) on combined stdout+stderr
- Wrap cmux read-screen with || true so set -euo pipefail doesn't kill parallel subshells on dead panes
- wave-status.sh adds new 'dead' status value distinct from 'unreachable'
- wave-completion.sh: fix BD status extraction (was grepping for Status: prefix that bd show never emits)
- skill.md: document the new 'dead' status

Trigger: during CCP-yun Wave 1, status script reported CCP-qo0 and CCP-1ul as 'in_progress' / 'session_close' for 30+ min AFTER panes had actually closed and beads were closed in DB. Misled orchestration into thinking wave was stuck.

Status: shipped to main, closing immediately.

title: [security] Add PreToolUse Skill gate for high-value skills
description: High-value skills can be invoked implicitly by any agent. Add a PreToolUse hook matching Skill tool with if:'Skill(business:*|infra:hetzner-*|content:linkedin)' that forces ask-permission. Protects: business:mail-send, business:op-credentials, business:amazon, business:google-invoice, content:linkedin, infra:hetzner-cloud. Does NOT gate research/analysis skills (summarize, playwright-cli, etc) to keep normal workflows fast.
acceptance_criteria: PreToolUse hook matches Skill with if: filter; invoking business:mail-send or infra:hetzner-cloud prompts user; research skills run without prompts

title: [FEAT] Wave-2: Evaluierungs-Gate für Phase-2 (adversarial)
description:
## Ziel

Automatische Evaluierung ob Phase 2 (CMUX adversarial Review) noch Mehrwert liefert. Hinweis an User wenn Phase 2 konsistent keine CRITICAL Findings mehr produziert.

## Scope

### 1. Wave-Close Metriken-Auswertung

Nach jedem Wave-Close:
- Aggregiere phase2_findings und phase2_critical aus metrics.db für alle Beads der Wave
- Vergleiche mit Baseline (erste Waves nach Einführung der Model-Strategie)

### 2. 3-Waves-Gate

Wenn 3 Waves in Folge keine CRITICAL Findings aus Phase 2:
- Hinweis an User: "Phase 2 hat in den letzten 3 Waves keine CRITICAL Findings produziert. Überprüfen ob Phase 2 noch nötig ist?"
- NICHT automatisch deaktivieren — User entscheidet

### 3. ROI-Dashboard

Auswertungs-Script/Skill-Erweiterung für bead-metrics:
- Follow-up-Beads pro Wave (aus bd list mit parent-Referenz)
- Fix:Feat-Commit-Ratio pro Wave (aus git log)
- Phase-2 CRITICAL Findings Trend über Waves
- Model-Attribution: welches Model hat was gefunden

## Abhängig von

Wave-1 (metrics.db Erweiterung + Model-Routing) muss zuerst implementiert sein.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Nach Wave-Close: automatische Metriken-Zusammenfassung wird ausgegeben
- [ ] 3-Waves-Gate: Hinweis erscheint wenn Bedingung erfüllt
- [ ] bead-metrics Skill zeigt Wave-aggregierte Daten (phase2 findings trend)
- [ ] Follow-up-Beads und Fix:Feat-Ratio pro Wave querybar
notes:
## Schema rework required (wave-review 2026-04-20)

CCP-2vo.2 replaces the flat bead_runs.phase2_findings / phase2_critical columns (added by closed CCP-80r) with a child agent_calls table keyed by run_id, where phase is a string label (codex-adversarial, codex-review, codex-fix-check). CCP-2vo.4 also removes the cmux-reviewer pane entirely, so 'Phase 2' becomes 'Codex adversarial gate'.

Impact on this bead:
- The three acceptance criteria that rely on 'phase2_findings' / 'phase2_critical' columns no longer have a data source.
- Rework needed: group agent_calls by phase_label='codex-adversarial' and join findings-count via follow-up beads (dep-chain from the reviewed bead).
- Alternative: close this bead if CCP-2vo.7's Token Breakdown section (per-mode, per-model) already covers the ROI visibility need.

Reopen decision after CCP-2vo.7 and CCP-2vo.8 land.
close_reason: Covered by CCP-2vo.7 Token Breakdown (per-mode + per-model) and CCP-2vo.2 agent_calls schema. Original schema (bead_runs.phase2_findings / phase2_critical) is obsolete after CCP-2vo.2 lands. ROI visibility now flows through CCP-2vo.7's Learnings Report. Closed without rework per /wave-reviewer 2026-04-20.

value: Wave 2a (CCP-xpr vision-parser helper) closed 2026-04-19. 3 review iterations caught 3 genuine silent-success failures (missing boundary table, duplicate principle IDs, not_in_vision type). 30/30 tests passing. 4-col boundary table (rule_id|rule|scope|source-section) is the shared integration contract for CCP-hf1, CCP-a67, CCP-2hd architecture-scout, CCP-wxv review-agent. Trinity Helper validated: single helper prevents format drift across 4 consumers. Token cost 196k (review-heavy as expected for contract work). Quality A, TDD B. Unblocks CCP-hf1 and CCP-a67.

Wave 1 (CCP-2q2, CCP-9yh, CCP-qrg) closed 2026-04-19. Key: Codex adversarial review caught exec-loop bug in CCP-qrg PATH-shim (2 iterations, full mode). Quick-fix mode sufficient for CCP-2q2 tense-gate + CCP-9yh mutual-exclusion (1 iteration each). tense-gate now available for /vision-author /vision-review skills.

Wave 1 hook infrastructure (CCP-mit WorktreeCreate + CCP-dzk TaskCreated standards-injection + CCP-2n7 SubagentStop metrics) closed 2026-04-21 ~53min elapsed. Key decision: CCP_ORCHESTRATOR_RUN_ID env var is canonical semaphore distinguishing orchestrator vs ad-hoc Agent() spawns (metrics skips universally, injection skips only for orchestrator_handled patterns). Phase 1.25 wave-review caught dual-writer bug early (split CCP-wl6 into dzk+2n7). Codex adversarial caught settings.json hook schema bug in CCP-dzk. Phase 6.5 integration-verification PASS with follow-up CCP-1m6 (agent-standards.yml coverage: beads-workflow/infra/meta missing). Open: CCP-rzf (auto-mode PermissionDenied hook, spec now complete, ready for dispatch), CCP-8wm (interactive-deny v2, blocked by rzf).
